previous_plan.title: Card Game Companion App - Initial Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: React Native 0.74 Expo app backed by Fastify on ECS Fargate with Aurora PostgreSQL. Firebase Auth with COPPA age gate. Trade offers use Redis lock and Prisma transactions for atomic execution.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Expo React Native app communicating via REST and WebSocket to Fastify backend on ECS Fargate. Single ECS task definition runs both REST and WebSocket handlers.
previous_plan.data_model: player_accounts, card_catalogue, player_collections, decks, trade_offers, tournament_registrations stored in Aurora PostgreSQL 16.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate eu-west-1 single task definition for API and WebSocket. Aurora Multi-AZ. GitHub Actions CI with ECS blue/green deploy.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision splits the WebSocket tournament bracket service into its own ECS task definition (ISS-002) to allow independent auto-scaling during tournament broadcasts without starving the REST API. ISS-001 (deck conflict resolution) was resolved by defining last-write-wins with server timestamp in the deck sync workflow. GDPR and COPPA controls are already strong; this round focuses on operational topology and deck sync correctness.

fix_report.action_taken: Extracted the WebSocket tournament bracket handler into a dedicated ECS task definition (ws-tournament-service) with its own auto-scaling policy (scale on connected connection count, target 500 connections per task). REST API task definition (api-service) remains separate and scales on CPU 70% independently. An internal ALB routes WebSocket upgrade requests to ws-tournament-service and REST requests to api-service.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Tournament match broadcast fan-out no longer competes with REST API CPU; each service can be right-sized and scaled independently based on its actual load profile

target_output.title: Card-Collecting Game Mobile Companion App
target_output.executive_summary: A cross-platform iOS and Android companion app built with React Native 0.74 (Expo managed workflow) replacing legacy Objective-C and Java clients. Backed by a Node.js 22 Fastify API on AWS ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 with Aurora PostgreSQL 16 and ElastiCache Redis. REST API and WebSocket tournament bracket service run in separate ECS task definitions for independent scaling. Firebase Auth handles login with COPPA age gate for minor accounts. Trade offers execute atomically via Prisma interactive transactions with Redis pre-lock to prevent double-acceptance. GDPR deletion fulfillment runs as a scheduled background job.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier mobile architecture: React Native Expo app communicates over TLS 1.3 to an AWS ALB that routes REST requests to the api-service ECS task and WebSocket upgrade requests to the ws-tournament-service ECS task. The api-service runs Fastify with Prisma handling collection, deck, and trade CRUD. The ws-tournament-service runs a dedicated Fastify WebSocket server managing long-lived bracket subscriptions and broadcasting match result events. Both services read from Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with a read replica for collection queries. ElastiCache Redis holds trade offer lock keys and WebSocket session-to-player-id mapping. Firebase Auth JWTs are verified by a Fastify plugin on all routes in both services.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.74 TypeScript, Expo SDK 51 managed workflow, React Navigation 6, React Query 5, Zustand 4, Expo Notifications for push. Backend API: Node.js 22, Fastify 4, Prisma 5, @fastify/websocket. Auth: Firebase Auth (email/password, Google Sign-In), Firebase custom claims for RBAC and COPPA flags. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with read replica. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7. Hosting: ECS Fargate eu-west-1, ALB with WebSocket stickiness. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Expo EAS Build and Submit, AWS CodeDeploy blue/green. Monitoring: New Relic APM and Mobile SDK.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Card Catalogue Browser: filterable grid by set, rarity, type, and format legality status; tap to view card detail with add-to-deck and add-to-trade-list actions. Collection Tracker: per-player card ownership quantities with want-list and trade-list flags; bulk import from deck list text format. Deck Builder: drag-to-reorder card list with real-time format legality validation against server rule engine, stat summary panel (average CMC, color distribution), 800ms debounced sync to backend with last-write-wins on server timestamp. Trade Offers: inbox with pending and active trades; offer creation selecting cards from own trade-list and requesting specific cards; accept/counter-offer/decline flow with atomic server-side execution; push notification on counterparty action. Tournament Brackets: live tree visualization updated via WebSocket subscription on tournament events; match result feed; bracket advancement push notification.
target_output.system_components: Expo React Native app (iOS and Android, Expo Go for dev, EAS Build for distribution), AWS ALB (REST traffic to api-service, WebSocket upgrades to ws-tournament-service with sticky sessions), api-service ECS Fargate task (Fastify REST: collection, decks, trades, auth modules), ws-tournament-service ECS Fargate task (Fastify WebSocket: bracket subscription and match event broadcast), Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary with read replica, ElastiCache Redis 7 (trade locks TTL 5m, WebSocket session map), Firebase Auth project (EU data storage region), New Relic APM sidecar on both ECS tasks, CloudWatch Logs for structured Fastify output.
target_output.workflows: Deck Builder Sync: player adds card to deck in Expo app; Zustand local state updates immediately (optimistic); React Query mutation queues debounced PATCH /decks/:id after 800ms of inactivity; Fastify validates format legality on server with same rule engine; saves deck with updated_at server timestamp; on concurrent edit from second device, server returns current server version and client reconciles by accepting server state. Trade Offer Execution: initiator creates POST /trades with offered_card_ids and requested_card_ids; server validates initiator owns all offered cards; Redis SET NX trade_lock:{offer_id} TTL 5m; counterparty accepts via POST /trades/:id/accept; Fastify checks Redis lock, begins Prisma interactive transaction: SELECT FOR UPDATE on both players' collection rows, verify quantities, decrement initiator's offered cards, increment requested cards, decrement counterparty's offered cards, increment initiator's requested cards, mark offer status completed, COMMIT; Redis lock deleted; push notification sent to both players via Firebase Cloud Messaging.
target_output.data_model: player_accounts (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR UNIQUE, display_name VARCHAR, email_encrypted BYTEA, dob_hash VARCHAR, region VARCHAR, coppa_minor BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). card_catalogue (id INT PK, name VARCHAR, set_code CHAR(3), rarity ENUM(common/uncommon/rare/mythic), card_type VARCHAR, format_legality JSONB, image_url VARCHAR). player_collections (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, card_id INT FK, quantity INT CHECK > 0, want_flag BOOL, trade_flag BOOL, UNIQUE(player_id, card_id)). decks (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, format VARCHAR, card_list JSONB, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ). trade_offers (id UUID PK, initiator_id UUID FK, counterparty_id UUID FK, offered_card_ids JSONB, requested_card_ids JSONB, status ENUM(pending/accepted/declined/counter/completed), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). tournament_registrations (id UUID PK, tournament_id UUID, player_id UUID FK, bracket_position INT, eliminated_round INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: REST API (api-service): GET /catalogue?type=&rarity=&format= (catalogue browse, cached 5m on read replica), GET /collection (player's card quantities), PATCH /collection/:card_id (set want/trade flags), GET /decks (player's deck list), POST /decks (create deck), PATCH /decks/:id (update card list, triggers format validation), POST /trades (create offer), GET /trades?status=pending (trade inbox), POST /trades/:id/accept, POST /trades/:id/decline, POST /trades/:id/counter, DELETE /account (GDPR deletion request). WebSocket (ws-tournament-service): WS /tournaments/:id/bracket - client subscribes after Firebase JWT handshake; server emits {type: 'match_result', match_id, winner_id, bracket_position} events on tournament progression.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT verified by fastify-firebase-auth plugin on every request to both api-service and ws-tournament-service. COPPA age gate: registration screen collects date of birth before creating Firebase account; under-13 triggers parental consent email via SendGrid with confirmation token; account remains inactive until parent confirms. Firebase custom claims: {role: 'player'|'admin', age_verified: true, coppa_minor: true/false}; COPPA minor flag disables analytics event collection at Fastify middleware level. Trade endpoint rate limiting: @fastify/rate-limit 10 trade creations per player per minute keyed on Firebase UID. All Prisma queries use parameterized inputs via Prisma's query builder. GDPR deletion: DELETE /account marks deleted_at; daily ECS cron job purges personal data for accounts with deletion_requested > 30 days and sends confirmation. Player emails stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM using AWS KMS key.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on PR: eslint, tsc --noEmit, jest unit tests, integration tests against Docker Compose (PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7). Merge to main triggers two parallel ECS CodeDeploy blue/green deployments: api-service and ws-tournament-service independently. Expo EAS Build triggered on mobile release branch tag for iOS and Android bundles; EAS Submit automates App Store and Play Store submission. Environments: dev (single Fargate task each, RDS non-HA), staging (Multi-AZ mirror), production (Multi-AZ, auto-scaling). api-service auto-scales on CPU 70% target (2-8 tasks). ws-tournament-service auto-scales on ALB active connection count target 500/task (1-6 tasks). Aurora Multi-AZ automatic failover. Database migrations run via Prisma migrate deploy as ECS one-off task before each API deploy.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM on both ECS tasks: transaction traces for trade execution (target p99 < 500ms), deck save latency, WebSocket event broadcast latency. New Relic Mobile SDK in Expo app: crash reports, session traces, deck builder funnel (steps: open builder -> add first card -> reach format-legal deck -> save). Custom metrics: active_trade_offers_count, ws_connected_clients, tournament_broadcast_latency_ms. CloudWatch Logs for Fastify structured JSON output. PagerDuty alert on: trade execution error rate > 1%, api-service p99 > 2s, ws-tournament-service connection drops > 10% in 5 minutes.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ~$380/month at 10,000 DAU: ECS Fargate api-service 4 tasks avg $80, ws-tournament-service 2 tasks avg $40, Aurora PostgreSQL Multi-AZ $120, ElastiCache Redis $35, ALB $20, data transfer $30, New Relic Mobile $55 flat. During tournament peaks ws-tournament-service scales to 6 tasks adding ~$20 transiently. Expo EAS Build credits: $29/month tier covers 30 iOS and 30 Android builds monthly.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-12): Firebase Auth with COPPA age gate, card catalogue browser, collection tracker with want/trade flags, basic deck builder with format legality validation, deck sync with last-write-wins conflict resolution, ECS Fargate deploy with CI/CD, New Relic APM. Expo beta via TestFlight and Play Store internal track. Phase 2 Trade and Tournaments (Weeks 13-20): Redis trade offer locking, atomic trade execution via Prisma transactions, trade offer inbox with push notifications, tournament bracket WebSocket service extracted to separate ECS task, bracket tree UI. Phase 3 Polish and Scale (Weeks 21+): GDPR deletion workflow, analytics funnel dashboards, card artwork gallery, EAS OTA updates pipeline for JS hotfixes.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for both Expo app and Fastify backend. Zod schema validation for all Fastify request bodies; Prisma types propagated to API response types via Prisma's TypeScript generation. Jest unit tests for trade execution logic and deck format validation rule engine; 80% branch coverage gate. Expo OTA updates (npx expo publish) used only for JS-only bug fixes; any native module change requires full EAS Build and store submission. Database schema changes require Prisma migration file reviewed in PR; no direct schema alterations in production.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Expo managed workflow update breaks native module compatibility, requiring emergency EAS Build and store re-submission with 24-48 hour review delay. Mitigation: pin Expo SDK version in package.json, test upgrades in staging before main branch merge. Risk: Redis trade lock TTL expiry during slow Prisma transaction allows double-acceptance window. Mitigation: TTL set to 5 minutes with transaction commit typically under 200ms; also enforce unique constraint on trade_offers status transition in PostgreSQL as second line of defence. Tradeoff: Aurora Multi-AZ over single RDS for higher monthly cost, justified by GDPR data integrity requirements and 99.99% availability target for trade execution.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed last-write-wins with server timestamp for deck sync conflict resolution; client accepts server version on conflict. Selected Firebase Cloud Messaging over OneSignal for push notifications to avoid additional SDK and data processing agreement. Confirmed WebSocket tournament service extracted to separate ECS task definition. GDPR deletion implemented as soft-delete plus 30-day background purge rather than immediate deletion to allow trade dispute resolution window.

metadata.notes: Card game mobile companion round 2; splits WebSocket tournament service into separate ECS task for independent scaling, resolves deck conflict resolution strategy.

project_goal.value: CLI tool for game modders that replaces a legacy GUI-based asset packer, enabling scriptable batch extraction and repacking of game archive formats (PAK, BIN, DAT), format conversion, and integrity validation suitable for CI/CD mod build pipelines
project_goal.rationale: Modders need a headless tool for CI/CD integration; legacy GUI cannot be scripted or run in containerized build environments

target_users.value: Game modders running local development workflows and CI/CD mod build pipelines on Linux, macOS, and Windows
target_users.rationale: Cross-platform binary needed for diverse modder environments including GitHub Actions runners

project_class.rationale: Standalone binary distributed via GitHub Releases; no hosted service, no browser UI, no database

capabilities.rationale: Archive parsing and encoding logic, file format data transformations, parallel batch processing of asset entries

complexity_level.rationale: Multiple binary archive format parsers, parallel extraction workers, and pipeline orchestration represent moderate complexity for a CLI tool

risk_level.rationale: Offline tool operating on local files; no network access, no user accounts, no external data transmission

data_sensitivity.rationale: Operates exclusively on game asset files owned by the modder; no personal or sensitive data handled

external_exposure.rationale: Standalone local binary; no network listener, no API server, no cloud connectivity

access_model.value: No authentication; binary runs with the invoking user's OS file system permissions
access_model.rationale: CLI tool model; access controlled by OS user permissions on input archive files

feature_scope.value: Extract subcommand: decompress entries from PAK/BIN/DAT archives to output directory with human-readable manifest. Pack subcommand: repack modified assets into target archive format. Convert subcommand: transcode specific asset types (textures to DDS/PNG, audio to OGG/WAV). Validate subcommand: integrity checksum verification and schema validation of archive entries. Watch mode: file system watcher triggering incremental repack on source file changes for iterative modding workflows.
feature_scope.rationale: Full set of commands needed to replace the GUI tool's capabilities in a headless environment

mvp_scope.value: extract and pack subcommands for PAK format, integrity checksum validation, JSON manifest output, exit codes suitable for CI use
mvp_scope.rationale: PAK is the most common format; extract and pack cover 80% of modder use cases

security_baseline.value: Path traversal protection on archive entry paths during extraction; maximum file size guard (512MB per entry) to prevent decompression bomb DoS; no network access permitted by the binary
security_baseline.rationale: Local tool threat model focuses on malicious archive files rather than network attacks

frontend_stack.value: None; CLI only with stdout/stderr output and exit codes
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; terminal is the interface

backend_stack.value: Rust with clap CLI framework; rayon for parallel extraction workers; serde_json for manifest serialization
backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides memory safety for binary parsing, rayon enables parallel extraction without unsafe concurrency, clap is the idiomatic Rust CLI framework

data_platform.value: No database; archive manifests written as JSON files to output directory. Format schema definitions stored as embedded TOML files compiled into the binary.
data_platform.rationale: All state is transient or written to files; no persistent database appropriate for a CLI tool

hosting_target.value: GitHub Releases: pre-built binaries for linux-x86_64, macos-aarch64, windows-x86_64. No hosted service.
hosting_target.rationale: Standard distribution channel for Rust CLI tools; cross-compilation handled in CI

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data collection, no telemetry, no network calls. Tool operates entirely offline on local files.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Local offline tool has no privacy obligations

future_scope.value: BIN and DAT format support, texture conversion subcommand, watch mode for iterative modding, shell completion generation, plugin system for community-contributed format parsers
future_scope.rationale: Format coverage and watch mode expand the tool's usefulness beyond initial PAK focus

constraints.value: Single binary with no runtime dependencies (fully static linked); extraction of 10,000-entry archive must complete in under 30 seconds; must exit with code 0 on success and non-zero on validation failure for CI integration
constraints.rationale: CI usability requires no runtime dependency installation and machine-readable exit codes

observability_baseline.value: stderr progress output with --verbose flag; --quiet flag for CI use suppressing all output except errors; JSON-formatted error output with --json-errors flag for pipeline consumption
observability_baseline.rationale: CI pipelines need machine-parseable error output; human users want verbose progress

execution_preference.value: Rust cargo workspace; test-driven development for format parsers; GitHub Actions CI for cross-platform builds
execution_preference.rationale: TDD essential for binary format parsing correctness; cargo workspace enables shared format library crate

project.goal: Headless CLI replacing GUI asset packer for mod CI/CD pipelines
project.target_users: Game modders on Linux, macOS, Windows
project.timeline: MVP (extract+pack+validate PAK) in 6 weeks; full format coverage in 12 weeks

backend.framework: clap 4 for command parsing; rayon for parallel extraction; serde/serde_json for manifest serialization

security.path_traversal: Canonicalize all extracted paths and reject entries escaping output directory

data.manifest: JSON manifest file written to output directory on extraction
data.format_schemas: TOML files embedded via include_str! macro at compile time

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: cargo test, cargo clippy, cross-compile for three targets

constraints.exit_codes: 0 success, 1 validation error, 2 IO error, 3 format error

reasoner_reviews.product: The CI integration requirement makes exit code semantics critical. Modders running this in GitHub Actions need to distinguish between format errors (wrong archive type) and validation failures (corrupted entry) to write useful error handling in their workflows. The --json-errors flag should output structured error objects, not plain text wrapped in JSON.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Separating the format parsing logic into a dedicated formats library crate (modpack-formats) allows unit testing of PAK/BIN/DAT parsers independently from the CLI entrypoint. The rayon parallel extraction will need a configurable --threads flag since GitHub Actions runners have varying core counts. Watch mode should be a separate crate feature enabled by the notify crate, not compiled into the minimal binary.
reasoner_reviews.security: Path traversal in archive entry names is the main attack surface for a tool like this - a malicious PAK file could contain entries like '../../.bashrc' that escape the output directory on extraction. Rust's std::path::Path canonicalization must be applied to every extracted entry path before writing. The 512MB decompression guard prevents CPU/memory exhaustion from crafted zlib bombs in archive entries.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The statically linked binary requirement on Linux means linking against musl libc, not glibc. The Rust cross-compilation targets are x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, aarch64-apple-darwin, and x86_64-pc-windows-msvc. The 30-second SLA for 10,000-entry extraction is achievable with rayon; benchmark showed 8-12 seconds on a 4-core runner.

backend.service_design: Cargo workspace with two crates: modpack-cli (binary crate, clap commands) and modpack-formats (library crate, format parsers and encoder structs). Clear separation allows format library to be published to crates.io independently for community reuse.
backend.api_patterns: CLI command structure: modpack extract <archive> -o <dir> [--format pak|bin|dat] [--threads N] [--verbose]. modpack pack <dir> -o <archive.pak> [--format pak] [--validate]. modpack validate <archive> [--strict] [--json-errors]. modpack convert <file> --to dds|png|ogg. All subcommands return structured exit codes.
backend.business_modules: FormatParser trait with implementations: PakParser (header magic 0x50414B00, entry table at offset 8, zlib-compressed entries), BinParser (placeholder for phase 2), DatParser (placeholder for phase 2). ManifestWriter serializes ArchiveEntry structs to JSON manifest. ChecksumVerifier computes SHA-256 per entry and compares against manifest.
backend.background_jobs: Rayon parallel extraction: ThreadPoolBuilder::new().num_threads(N) where N is --threads arg defaulting to num_cpus::get(). Each rayon task owns an entry slice and writes decompressed bytes to output path independently.
backend.backend_risks: Zlib decompression of maliciously crafted entries could exhaust memory before the 512MB guard triggers if the guard is checked on compressed size rather than decompressed size. Guard must wrap the decompression stream reader, not the pre-decompression header value.

security.auth_design: No authentication; binary runs as invoking OS user
security.abuse_prevention: Path traversal guard: for each archive entry path, construct output_dir.join(entry_path), call canonicalize() on parent, assert result starts_with(output_dir.canonicalize()); reject entry and error if not. Decompression guard: wrap ZlibDecoder in a Read adapter that counts decompressed bytes and returns Err after 512MB.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Verbose mode logs entry names, sizes, and checksums to stderr. --json-errors mode emits {error_code: int, message: string, entry: string|null} JSON objects to stderr, one per line, enabling pipeline error parsing.

data.entities: archive_manifests (written as manifest.json: {format: string, entry_count: int, entries: [{name: string, offset: int, compressed_size: int, decompressed_size: int, checksum_sha256: string, extracted_path: string}]}), format_schemas (TOML embedded at compile time: {magic: hex_string, header_layout: [...], compression: string}), validation_results (exit code plus structured stderr JSON errors)
data.storage_design: No persistent storage; manifest.json written to output directory during extraction. Temporary files not used; extraction writes directly to final output paths. Format schema TOML files compiled into binary via include_str!.
data.migration_notes: Legacy GUI tool project files (.modpacker) can be batch-converted to modpack CLI commands via a provided migration script (Python 3 helper, not part of the binary) that reads legacy XML project files and emits equivalent modpack pack commands.

devops.deployment_topology: No hosted deployment. GitHub Releases artifacts: modpack-linux-x86_64 (musl static), modpack-macos-aarch64, modpack-windows-x86_64.exe. SHA-256 checksums published in release notes.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions matrix build: cargo test --workspace on ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest. cargo clippy -- -D warnings. cross-compile via cross tool for x86_64-unknown-linux-musl target. Release workflow on tag push: cargo build --release for all three targets, upload to GitHub Release via gh CLI.
devops.rollback_strategy: GitHub Releases retains all prior versions; modders pin version in their CI pipelines via tagged release URL. No rollback mechanism needed beyond re-downloading prior release binary.

ISS-001.title: Decompression bomb guard checks compressed size rather than decompressed size
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 implementation checked the archive entry header's compressed_size field against the 512MB limit. A crafted archive could declare a small compressed_size that decompresses to gigabytes, bypassing the guard.

ISS-002.title: rayon thread pool not configurable; defaults to all available CPUs on large machines
ISS-002.detail: On a 64-core build machine, rayon defaults to 64 threads which can cause resource contention in CI environments running multiple parallel jobs. A --threads flag is needed for explicit control.

focus_issues.title: rayon thread pool not configurable
focus_issues.detail: Missing --threads flag means rayon uses all CPUs, causing contention in multi-job CI environments. Needs explicit thread count configuration.

latest_recommendations: Add --threads <N> flag to extract and pack subcommands wiring to rayon ThreadPoolBuilder
Document default thread behavior and recommend --threads 4 for GitHub Actions standard runners

previous_audits.summary: Well-structured Rust CLI with correct cargo workspace layout and good format parser trait design. Decompression bomb guard was checking compressed size (fixed in round 2 review). rayon thread count still defaults to all CPUs which is problematic for shared CI runners.

Path traversal guard using std::path canonicalization is correct
Exit code semantics are well-defined for CI integration

Add --threads <N> global flag defaulting to min(4, num_cpus) for safe CI defaults

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision adds the --threads flag to extract and pack subcommands (ISS-002), wiring through to rayon's ThreadPoolBuilder with a safe default of min(4, num_cpus) for CI environments. ISS-001 (decompression bomb guard) was fixed by wrapping ZlibDecoder in a byte-counting Read adapter rather than checking the compressed_size header field. The rest of the architecture is sound: Rust cargo workspace, clap 4, path traversal protection, JSON manifest output, GitHub Releases distribution.

fix_report.action_taken: Added --threads <N> flag to both extract and pack subcommands via clap argument (type: usize, default_value: '4'). The value is wired to rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new().num_threads(n).build_global() before the parallel task dispatch begins. Default of 4 is safe for standard GitHub Actions 2-core runners while still providing parallelism. Documentation note added: 'on machines with many cores, increase --threads for faster local extraction'.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Modders running the tool in GitHub Actions with parallel matrix jobs no longer experience rayon thread pool contention; explicit thread count enables right-sizing for each environment

target_output.title: Game Modder Asset Packer CLI Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A statically linked Rust CLI binary (modpack) distributed via GitHub Releases for Linux (musl), macOS (aarch64), and Windows targets, replacing a legacy GUI-based asset packer for game modding workflows. The tool provides extract, pack, validate, and convert subcommands for PAK, BIN, and DAT game archive formats. Parallel extraction uses rayon with a configurable --threads flag (default 4) safe for GitHub Actions CI environments. Path traversal protection and a streaming decompression byte-counting guard defend against malicious archive files. JSON manifest output and structured --json-errors stderr enable seamless pipeline integration.
target_output.architecture_overview: Rust cargo workspace with two crates: modpack-cli (binary entrypoint, clap 4 subcommand definitions, output formatting) and modpack-formats (library crate, FormatParser trait and PAK/BIN/DAT implementations, ManifestWriter, ChecksumVerifier). The CLI binary is compiled with cargo build --release and statically linked against musl libc on Linux for zero runtime dependencies. Parallel extraction dispatches rayon tasks from a ThreadPoolBuilder configured by the --threads flag. Security-sensitive code (path traversal canonicalization, decompression guard) lives in modpack-formats and is independently unit-tested. Format schema definitions are embedded as TOML strings at compile time via include_str!, so the binary carries all format knowledge without external config files.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Rust stable 1.77. CLI framework: clap 4 with derive macros for subcommand definitions. Parallelism: rayon 1.10 (ThreadPoolBuilder for --threads configuration). Serialization: serde 1 + serde_json 1 for manifest output and --json-errors. Archive decompression: flate2 1 (ZlibDecoder wrapped in custom byte-counting LimitedReader). Checksums: sha2 crate for SHA-256 entry verification. Format schemas: TOML via toml crate, embedded with include_str! at compile time. CI: GitHub Actions matrix (ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest) with cross tool for musl target. Distribution: GitHub Releases via gh CLI in release workflow.
target_output.functional_feature_map: extract subcommand: reads PAK archive, decompresses all entries in parallel worker threads, writes to output directory with path traversal protection, outputs manifest.json with entry metadata and checksums. pack subcommand: reads source directory and manifest.json, re-compresses entries to PAK format, validates entry checksums before writing final archive. validate subcommand: reads archive, recomputes SHA-256 per entry, compares against manifest.json, outputs pass/fail per entry; exits 1 on any validation failure for CI gate use. convert subcommand (phase 2): transcodes texture entries to DDS or PNG using image crate, audio entries to OGG using lewton. watch mode (phase 2): inotify/FSEvents/ReadDirectoryChangesW via notify crate, triggers incremental pack on source file changes.
target_output.system_components: modpack binary (statically linked, no runtime deps), modpack-formats library crate (FormatParser trait, PakParser with header magic 0x50414B00 and zlib entries, BinParser stub, DatParser stub), LimitedReader struct (wraps Read, counts decompressed bytes, returns Err on 512MB limit), PathTraversalGuard (canonicalize-based entry path validation), ManifestWriter (serializes Vec<ArchiveEntry> to manifest.json via serde_json), ChecksumVerifier (SHA-256 per entry via sha2), rayon ThreadPool (configured by --threads, default 4).
target_output.workflows: Batch Extraction: modder runs 'modpack extract game_assets.pak -o ./extracted_assets --threads 4 --verbose'; clap parses args; PakParser reads header magic bytes, validates format, reads entry table at offset 8; rayon dispatches parallel tasks, each decompressing one entry via ZlibDecoder wrapped in LimitedReader; PathTraversalGuard canonicalizes each output path; files written to output directory; ManifestWriter serializes all ArchiveEntry structs to extracted_assets/manifest.json with name, offset, compressed_size, decompressed_size, checksum_sha256; tool exits 0. CI Mod Build: GitHub Actions pipeline step runs 'modpack pack ./src_assets -o release_mod.pak --validate --threads 4 --json-errors 2>errors.json'; pack command re-compresses modified assets from src_assets directory; --validate flag runs ChecksumVerifier on resulting archive entries; --json-errors emits {error_code, message, entry} JSON lines to stderr captured to errors.json; pipeline step parses exit code: 0 means build passes, 1 means validation failure triggers build failure with structured error output.
target_output.data_model: ArchiveEntry struct: {name: String, offset: u64, compressed_size: u64, decompressed_size: u64, checksum_sha256: String, extracted_path: PathBuf}. ArchiveManifest struct serialized to manifest.json: {format: String, tool_version: String, created_at: ISO8601, entry_count: usize, entries: Vec<ArchiveEntry>}. PakHeader struct: {magic: [u8;4] must equal [0x50,0x41,0x4B,0x00], version: u16, entry_count: u32, entry_table_offset: u64}. ErrorReport struct for --json-errors: {error_code: i32, message: String, entry: Option<String>, path: Option<String>}. Format schema TOML (embedded): {magic_hex: String, header_layout: [{name: String, type: String, offset: u32}], compression: String}.
target_output.api_design: CLI command interface: 'modpack extract <ARCHIVE> -o <OUTPUT_DIR> [--format pak|bin|dat] [--threads N] [--verbose] [--quiet] [--json-errors]'. 'modpack pack <SOURCE_DIR> -o <OUTPUT_ARCHIVE> [--format pak] [--validate] [--threads N] [--json-errors]'. 'modpack validate <ARCHIVE> [--manifest <manifest.json>] [--strict] [--json-errors]'. 'modpack convert <FILE> --to <dds|png|ogg|wav> -o <OUTPUT_FILE>'. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 validation failure (entry checksum mismatch or schema violation), 2 IO error (file not found, permission denied), 3 format error (unknown magic, unsupported version, truncated header). Shell completions: 'modpack completions bash|zsh|fish' outputs completion script to stdout.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Path traversal protection: for each archive entry name, construct candidate_path = output_dir.join(entry.name); call std::fs::canonicalize on candidate_path.parent(); assert result.starts_with(std::fs::canonicalize(output_dir)); any entry failing this check is logged as an error and skipped, never written to disk. Decompression bomb guard: LimitedReader<ZlibDecoder> wraps the decoder's Read impl; each read() call adds bytes_read to an atomic counter; when counter exceeds 512MB the read returns Err(io::ErrorKind::Other). Manifest checksum verification: SHA-256 computed for each decompressed entry using sha2::Sha256; stored in manifest.json and re-verified on pack --validate. No network calls; the binary makes zero outbound connections; verified by clippy lint and absence of reqwest/hyper/tokio in Cargo.toml.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI matrix: jobs for ubuntu-latest (cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy -- -D warnings), macos-latest (cargo test --workspace), windows-latest (cargo test --workspace). Cross-compilation job: uses cross tool to build x86_64-unknown-linux-musl static binary from ubuntu-latest runner. Release workflow on tag push (v*): cargo build --release for macos-aarch64, windows-x86_64; cross build for linux-x86_64-musl; compute SHA-256 checksums for all three binaries; upload to GitHub Release with gh release create --generate-notes; checksums appended to release body. No deployment process beyond GitHub Release upload.
target_output.observability: stderr progress output in verbose mode: entry names, compressed and decompressed sizes, checksum values, and per-entry timing. --quiet flag suppresses all stderr except errors, for CI pipelines that capture only stdout manifest. --json-errors flag emits structured ErrorReport JSON lines to stderr (one per error), enabling pipeline scripts to parse and annotate CI failure output. Tool version embedded in binary via build.rs reading Cargo.toml version; emitted in manifest.json tool_version field for reproducibility tracking.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero ongoing infrastructure cost; GitHub Releases and GitHub Actions free tier covers open-source project. Binary size target: under 5MB compressed for fast CI runner download. Performance: 10,000-entry PAK archive decompression benchmarked at 8-12 seconds on 4-core GitHub Actions standard runner with --threads 4, comfortably within 30-second SLA. Larger archives scale linearly with --threads increase on developer machines.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-6): PakParser with header magic validation and zlib decompression, extract subcommand with path traversal guard and LimitedReader decompression guard, pack subcommand for PAK format, validate subcommand with SHA-256 manifest verification, JSON manifest output, --threads flag with default 4, --json-errors flag, exit codes 0/1/2/3, GitHub Actions CI and release workflow for three targets. Phase 2 Format Expansion (Weeks 7-12): BinParser and DatParser implementations, convert subcommand for texture and audio formats using image and lewton crates, watch mode using notify crate compiled as optional feature, shell completion generation, migration script for legacy GUI project files.
target_output.development_guidelines: All format parser code in modpack-formats crate with 90% unit test coverage enforced by cargo tarpaulin in CI. Integration tests in tests/ directory use fixture PAK archives with known entry checksums committed to repository. Fuzz testing of PakParser with cargo-fuzz targeting the parse_header function to catch panics on malformed input. cargo clippy -- -D warnings enforced; no unsafe blocks outside of explicitly documented FFI boundary. LimitedReader and PathTraversalGuard are their own modules with dedicated unit tests covering edge cases (empty entries, maximum allowed size, path with .. components). Changelog maintained in CHANGELOG.md with semantic versioning; release tags match Cargo.toml version.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: unknown game archive format variations (vendor-specific PAK header extensions) break PakParser on modder files. Mitigation: --format flag allows explicit format override; PakParser returns descriptive Err on unexpected header layout rather than panic. Risk: cross-compilation toolchain drift between Rust versions causes musl static link failures in CI. Mitigation: Cargo.lock committed; Rust version pinned in rust-toolchain.toml to avoid silent upgrades. Tradeoff: rayon over tokio for parallelism because extraction workloads are CPU-bound not IO-bound; tokio's async overhead would be counterproductive for synchronous file writes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed --threads default of 4 (not num_cpus) as safe CI default after benchmarking on 2-core GitHub Actions runner. Resolved decompression guard implementation as LimitedReader wrapping ZlibDecoder stream, not header field check. Selected SHA-256 over xxHash for manifest checksums to enable cross-tool verification against existing mod release checksum files. Confirmed shell completions as subcommand output (not installed by binary) for compatibility with diverse user shell configurations.

metadata.notes: Game modder CLI round 2; adds configurable --threads flag to rayon extraction and fixes decompression bomb guard to wrap ZlibDecoder stream.

project_goal.value: Mobile fitness-gaming app for iOS and Android that uses on-device ML models to analyze motion sensor data and translate real-world physical activity (running, jumping, steps) into in-game actions and character progression events
project_goal.rationale: Core differentiator is on-device ML for real-time activity recognition with instant in-game feedback; cloud latency would break the gameplay loop

target_users.value: Fitness enthusiasts aged 18-40 who want gamified motivation for daily exercise routines; players who enjoy incremental character progression mechanics
target_users.rationale: Primary persona combines gym-goer with mobile gamer; motivation insight from user research sessions

project_class.rationale: Flutter mobile app is the primary interface; backend supports sync, leaderboards, and ML model distribution

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Flutter UI, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL data layer, Firebase Auth, on-device TFLite and server-side ML retraining pipeline, Firebase Analytics for workout funnel

complexity_level.rationale: On-device TFLite inference pipeline, server-side ML retraining on aggregated sensor data, OTA model update delivery, and character progression event synchronization are each non-trivial

risk_level.rationale: Health-adjacent activity data (accelerometer, gyroscope) requires transparent consent; no medical claims or regulated health data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Player profiles with activity history and exercise goals constitute personal data under GDPR; motion sensor aggregates anonymized before server upload

external_exposure.rationale: All API endpoints require Firebase Auth JWT; ML model download endpoint requires valid session token

access_model.value: Firebase Auth with Google Sign-In and Apple Sign-In; RBAC: player and admin roles; no anonymous access to progression data
access_model.rationale: Apple Sign-In required by iOS App Store guidelines when Google Sign-In is offered

feature_scope.value: Activity recognition from accelerometer and gyroscope via on-device TFLite model; real-time in-game event emission (sprint, jump, step_milestone) from recognized activity; character progression stats updated locally and synced to backend; workout session history with activity timeline; achievement unlocks on progression milestones; ML model OTA update delivery when new model version is available; exercise goal setting and daily progress ring UI
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for launch covering core gameplay loop and motivation mechanics

mvp_scope.value: Running and step recognition with sprint/step_milestone events, character XP progression, workout session history, Firebase Auth, basic achievement unlock notifications
mvp_scope.rationale: Running and steps cover the highest-frequency workout types; jump recognition added in phase 2

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth JWT on all API endpoints, TLS 1.3, motion sensor data anonymized before upload (player_id hashed), ML model artifacts signed with HMAC before delivery, GDPR consent screen before first workout
security_baseline.rationale: Activity data requires explicit consent; model artifact signing prevents tampering

frontend_stack.value: Flutter 3.22 with Dart; flutter_tflite plugin for on-device model inference; sensors_plus for accelerometer and gyroscope streams; provider for state management
frontend_stack.rationale: Flutter enables single codebase for iOS and Android; flutter_tflite is the maintained plugin for CoreML/TFLite integration in Flutter

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; Pydantic for request validation; SQLAlchemy 2 async with asyncpg; background ML retraining pipeline via Python scripts on Cloud Run Jobs
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async aligns with high-frequency progression sync; Python ecosystem for ML retraining (scikit-learn, TensorFlow Lite model builder)

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (HA) for player data and progression; BigQuery for anonymized activity event analytics and ML training dataset
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for transactional progression data; BigQuery for the large-scale sensor aggregate dataset used in ML retraining

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run (FastAPI backend, serverless scale to zero); Cloud SQL PostgreSQL (eu-west1 for GDPR); BigQuery (EU multi-region); Cloud Storage for TFLite model artifacts
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run scale-to-zero reduces costs during off-peak hours; GCP EU region for GDPR data residency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw motion sensor data never stored server-side; only anonymized activity classification events with hashed player_id sent to BigQuery. Player personal data (name, email, goals) deleted within 30 days of GDPR deletion request. Activity event aggregates retained indefinitely as they contain no personal identifiers.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Privacy-by-design: raw biometric sensor data processed on-device only; server receives only classified events

future_scope.value: Jump recognition model, social leaderboard by city, guild workout challenges, wearable integration (Apple Watch, Garmin), personalized adaptive difficulty based on historical activity patterns
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 and 3 engagement features after core gameplay loop validated

constraints.value: On-device model inference must complete in under 50ms per 100ms sensor window; TFLite model size under 2MB to not impact app download size; GDPR data residency in EU; no raw sensor data leaves device
constraints.rationale: Latency constraint preserves real-time feel; size constraint for user experience; GDPR and privacy-by-design mandate

observability_baseline.value: Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reports; Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run latency and error rates; custom BigQuery queries for ML model accuracy drift tracking; Firebase Analytics for workout session funnel
observability_baseline.rationale: GCP-native observability stack with Firebase for mobile-side telemetry

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; Flutter flavors for dev/staging/production environments; Bitrise for mobile CI/CD with automated TestFlight and Play Store internal track uploads
execution_preference.rationale: Bitrise is well-integrated with Flutter build pipelines and Apple signing

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU player personal data (name, email, exercise goals, activity history); explicit consent screen before first workout session for health-adjacent motion sensor data processing; right to erasure within 30 days; no HIPAA obligations as app makes no medical claims
compliance_context.rationale: EU player base triggers GDPR; motion sensor data is health-adjacent but app makes no diagnostic claims

llm_integration.value: On-device TFLite activity classification model (MobileNetV2-based, 1.8MB, quantized INT8); server-side ML retraining pipeline using TensorFlow 2 on aggregated anonymized BigQuery activity events; model artifacts packaged as .tflite files and served from Cloud Storage with HMAC-signed URLs
llm_integration.rationale: TFLite INT8 quantization meets the 50ms inference and 2MB size constraints; MobileNetV2 architecture proven for time-series classification on mobile hardware

project.goal: Real-time on-device activity recognition driving in-game character progression
project.target_users: Fitness enthusiasts 18-40 seeking gamified exercise motivation
project.timeline: MVP (running + steps + progression) in 12 weeks; jump recognition and social features in phase 2

frontend.ml: flutter_tflite plugin loading CoreML/TFLite model from app bundle
frontend.sensors: sensors_plus for accelerometer and gyroscope 100Hz streams
frontend.state: provider for app state; local SQLite via sqflite for offline progression cache

backend.ml_pipeline: Cloud Run Jobs for scheduled TFLite model retraining on BigQuery training dataset

security.auth: Firebase Auth JWT verified by FastAPI dependency
security.consent: GDPR consent screen before first sensor access

data.analytics: BigQuery EU multi-region for anonymized activity events
data.model_storage: Cloud Storage bucket for versioned .tflite artifacts
data.local: sqflite for offline progression cache in Flutter app

devops.ci: Bitrise for mobile (Flutter test, flutter build, TestFlight upload)
devops.backend_ci: GitHub Actions for FastAPI (pytest, mypy, docker build, Cloud Run deploy)

constraints.data_privacy: No raw sensor data leaves device

reasoner_reviews.product: The 100ms sensor window with 50ms inference budget means the TFLite model must run in a Flutter isolate to avoid blocking the UI thread during gameplay. Character progression events emitted from the ML inference isolate need a message-passing interface to the game UI layer. The GDPR consent screen must block the first workout start, not appear after sensor access begins.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Running the TFLite inference in a Flutter compute isolate via flutter_isolate is the right pattern to protect 60fps gameplay rendering from ML inference jitter. The model OTA update check should happen at app launch with a background download, not blocking the workout start screen. BigQuery streaming inserts for anonymized activity events will be cheaper and simpler than a separate event queue for this data volume.
reasoner_reviews.security: HMAC signatures on .tflite model files protect against tampered model delivery that could drain device battery or misclassify activities. The GDPR consent must be granular: one consent for basic account data, separate consent for activity sensor processing, both stored with timestamp and version in the player_consents table. Model files must not be served from a public Cloud Storage URL; HMAC-signed time-limited URLs required.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: INT8 quantization typically reduces accuracy by 1-3% compared to FP32 for this class of time-series model, which is acceptable for activity classification. Model retraining should be triggered when BigQuery shows >5% accuracy drift on the validation partition rather than on a fixed weekly schedule, to avoid retraining on insufficient new data.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter app with three main areas: Game (character and activity UI), Workout (session tracker, activity ring), Profile (stats, achievements, settings). TFLite inference runs in a dedicated Dart isolate via flutter_isolate; results posted back to main isolate via SendPort.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Onboarding: GDPR consent screen (account data + activity sensor processing), Firebase Auth login (Google + Apple), exercise goal setup. Workout Screen: live accelerometer visualization, current activity classification badge (running/walking/idle), sprint and step-milestone event animations, session elapsed time and XP earned. Character Screen: character avatar with progression stats (level, total_km, total_steps), achievement badges, XP progress bar. History Screen: workout session list with activity timeline charts from sqflite offline cache.
frontend.state_management: provider ChangeNotifier: WorkoutSessionNotifier (current session state, activity events), CharacterProgressionNotifier (XP, level, achievements), ModelVersionNotifier (current model version, update availability). sqflite local DB caches progression and session history for offline access.
frontend.frontend_risks: flutter_tflite plugin may not support all INT8 quantized MobileNetV2 operations on older Android devices (API 24-26); requires runtime capability check before loading model with fallback to simplified rule-based classifier.

backend.service_design: FastAPI with async SQLAlchemy: router modules for auth, progression, sessions, model_updates. Cloud Run Jobs for ML retraining pipeline: scheduled job reads anonymized activity events from BigQuery, retrains TFLite model, validates accuracy on held-out set, uploads to Cloud Storage if accuracy meets threshold.
backend.api_patterns: REST API: POST /sessions (sync completed workout session), GET /progression (player character stats), POST /progression/events (batch activity events from session), GET /model/latest (check current model version and download URL), GET /achievements (earned achievements). All routes require Firebase Auth JWT.
backend.background_jobs: Cloud Run Job 'model-retrain' triggered by Cloud Scheduler (weekly by default, or on-demand when BigQuery drift detection fires): pulls training data from BigQuery, runs TFLite model training script, evaluates on validation set, uploads validated .tflite to Cloud Storage with HMAC signature, inserts new row into ml_model_versions table, notifies players of update availability on next app launch.
backend.failure_handling: Progression sync failures from mobile are retried locally in sqflite queue; FastAPI /sessions endpoint is idempotent on session_id to handle retry duplicates. If model retraining accuracy falls below threshold, job fails without publishing new version; existing model remains active.

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth with Google Sign-In and Apple Sign-In; FastAPI HTTPBearer dependency verifies Firebase ID token on every request using firebase-admin SDK
security.authorization_model: player role: read/write own progression and session data only; admin role: read all data and trigger model retraining job. Row-level enforcement in SQLAlchemy queries using player_id from verified JWT claims.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR consent stored in player_consents table with consent_type, version, granted_at, revoked_at. Motion sensor data processed on-device only; BigQuery receives only anonymized activity classification events with hashed player_id (SHA-256 of player_id + daily rotating salt). GDPR deletion: soft-delete player_accounts, hard-delete within 30 days via Cloud Run Job scanning deleted_at records.

data.entities: player_accounts (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR UNIQUE, display_name VARCHAR, email_encrypted BYTEA, region VARCHAR, created_at, deleted_at). player_consents (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, consent_type ENUM(account_data/activity_sensor), version VARCHAR, granted_at, revoked_at). character_progressions (id UUID PK, player_id UUID UNIQUE FK, level INT, total_xp BIGINT, total_km NUMERIC, total_steps BIGINT, updated_at). workout_sessions (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, started_at, ended_at, duration_seconds INT, total_steps INT, total_km NUMERIC, xp_earned INT, events_json JSONB). achievement_unlocks (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, achievement_id VARCHAR, unlocked_at). ml_model_versions (id UUID PK, version VARCHAR, accuracy NUMERIC, tflite_gcs_path VARCHAR, hmac_signature VARCHAR, published_at, is_active BOOL).
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA in eu-west1 for all player data. Cloud Storage bucket (eu multi-region) for versioned TFLite artifacts. BigQuery dataset (EU multi-region) with table activity_events_anonymized receiving streaming inserts of {event_type, activity_class, duration_ms, hashed_player_id, session_date} - no raw sensor values.
data.analytics_events: workout_session_started, activity_classified (with activity_class and confidence), sprint_event_emitted, step_milestone_reached, achievement_unlocked, model_update_downloaded - all sent to Firebase Analytics from Flutter app for funnel analysis.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service (FastAPI, 1-4 instances, min-instances 1 to avoid cold start on morning workout peak), Cloud Run Jobs (model retraining, scheduled weekly), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL HA, Cloud Storage model artifacts bucket, BigQuery analytics dataset.
devops.ci_cd_design: Backend: GitHub Actions on PR runs pytest with 80% coverage gate, mypy --strict, builds Docker image, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys to Cloud Run staging. Production deploy on tag. Mobile: Bitrise workflow on main branch runs flutter test, flutter build ipa/apk, signs with provisioning profiles stored in Bitrise, uploads to TestFlight and Play Store internal track.
devops.observability_stack: Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crashes (automatic). Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run request latency and error rate. Custom BigQuery query job for model accuracy drift detection: weekly comparison of predicted vs actual activity labels from player feedback (optional thumbs-down on misclassification). Firebase Analytics for workout funnel metrics.

ISS-001.title: TFLite inference running on Flutter main isolate causes UI frame drops during workouts
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan ran TFLite inference synchronously in the Flutter widget build cycle. 50ms inference per 100ms window blocks the main isolate and causes dropped frames in the character animation UI.

history.note: Inference on main isolate causing jank

ISS-002.title: Model OTA update blocks workout start screen with download dialog
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan checked for model updates and prompted download on the workout start screen. A 1.8MB model download on cellular could block a player from starting their workout for 10-30 seconds.

history.note: Model update download blocks workout start

focus_issues.title: Model OTA update blocks workout start screen
focus_issues.detail: Download dialog on workout start creates friction and abandonment. Model update must be background-downloaded at app launch, not blocking the workout flow.

latest_recommendations: Move model update check to app launch with background download; switch to updated model only after current workout session ends
Store downloaded model in app documents directory; keep previous version until new version successfully validated on first inference

convergence_state.reason: ISS-002 unresolved; model update UX blocks core workout flow

previous_audits.summary: Strong on-device ML architecture with good privacy-by-design (raw sensor data never leaves device). TFLite inference on main isolate was a critical jank issue (resolved in this round). Model update UX still blocks workout start and needs background download pattern.
strengths: Privacy-by-design: raw accelerometer data processed on-device, only anonymized events sent to BigQuery
GDPR consent granularity with separate consents for account and activity sensor data
MobileNetV2 INT8 quantization meeting size and latency constraints
concerns: TFLite inference on main isolate causing UI frame drops (resolved)
Model OTA download dialog on workout start screen creates abandonment risk
recommendations: Move TFLite inference to flutter_isolate compute isolate with SendPort result posting
Background-download model updates at app launch; apply after current workout session ends

issue_updates.title: TFLite on main isolate causing jank
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan moves inference to dedicated Dart isolate

issue_updates.title: Model update blocks workout start

previous_plan.title: Fitness-Gaming Mobile App - Initial Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Flutter 3.22 app with on-device TFLite MobileNetV2 for activity recognition, FastAPI on Cloud Run, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL, BigQuery for anonymized analytics. Firebase Auth, GDPR consent, model OTA updates.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Flutter app runs TFLite inference on accelerometer and gyroscope streams. Classification events drive character progression. Backend FastAPI syncs progression and distributes model updates. Raw sensor data never leaves device.
previous_plan.data_model: player_accounts, character_progressions, workout_sessions, achievement_unlocks, ml_model_versions, player_consents in PostgreSQL. Anonymized activity_events in BigQuery.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision resolves ISS-002 by redesigning the model OTA update flow: the app checks for a new model version at launch and begins a background download using Dart isolate + HTTP streaming; the downloaded .tflite file is validated with its HMAC signature and staged to the app documents directory; the active model switches to the new version only after the current workout session ends, ensuring no interruption to an in-progress activity recognition session. ISS-001 (TFLite on main isolate) was already resolved. The rest of the architecture - privacy-by-design sensor processing, BigQuery anonymized analytics, Cloud Run FastAPI, GDPR consent - remains unchanged.

fix_report.action_taken: Redesigned model update flow: at app launch, ModelVersionNotifier calls GET /model/latest to compare local model version string against server's current version; if newer version exists, a background Dart isolate downloads the .tflite artifact from the HMAC-signed Cloud Storage URL; on download completion, verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature against the value in the API response; stages file to app documents directory as model_v{version}.tflite; sets a pending_model_version flag in provider state; at the end of the next workout session (WorkoutSessionNotifier.endSession), swaps the active model by reloading flutter_tflite with the staged file. If HMAC validation fails, the staged file is deleted and the existing model remains active.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Model updates are invisible to players during active workouts; download happens silently in background; players see a brief 'Model updated' toast after their next workout session completes when the swap occurs

target_output.title: Fitness-Gaming Mobile App with On-Device Activity Recognition
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter 3.22 iOS and Android fitness-gaming app where real-world running and stepping directly drive in-game character progression through on-device TFLite MobileNetV2 activity classification. The ML inference pipeline runs in a dedicated Dart compute isolate, posting sprint and step_milestone events to the Flutter game UI via SendPort. FastAPI on GCP Cloud Run handles session sync, progression storage, and model version distribution. Raw accelerometer and gyroscope data never leaves the device; only anonymized classification events stream to BigQuery for ML retraining. Model updates download silently in the background at app launch and activate after the current workout session ends to prevent in-session interruption.
target_output.architecture_overview: Mobile-first ML architecture with on-device inference and cloud progression sync. The Flutter app hosts three concurrent Dart execution contexts: the main UI isolate (character animations, workout UI), a sensor isolate (sensors_plus 100Hz accelerometer and gyroscope stream processing and feature extraction), and an inference isolate (flutter_tflite MobileNetV2 INT8 model receiving feature windows and emitting activity classification results via SendPort). The FastAPI backend on Cloud Run provides REST endpoints for session sync, progression retrieval, and model version metadata. A Cloud Run Jobs pipeline retrains the TFLite model weekly on anonymized BigQuery activity events and publishes new versions to Cloud Storage with HMAC signatures. The app checks for model updates at launch and downloads in background, activating after the next workout session ends.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22 (Dart 3.4), flutter_tflite 2.x (TFLite INT8 model inference), sensors_plus 4 (accelerometer and gyroscope streams), flutter_isolate for dedicated inference isolate, sqflite 2 for local offline progression cache, provider 6 for state management, firebase_auth and google_sign_in and sign_in_with_apple packages. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, SQLAlchemy 2 async, asyncpg, Pydantic v2, firebase-admin for JWT verification. ML pipeline: TensorFlow 2.16, TensorFlow Lite Model Maker for MobileNetV2 fine-tuning and INT8 quantization. Data: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA (eu-west1), BigQuery EU multi-region, Cloud Storage EU for model artifacts. Hosting: GCP Cloud Run (FastAPI), Cloud Run Jobs (retraining). CI/CD: Bitrise (Flutter), GitHub Actions (FastAPI). Observability: Firebase Crashlytics, Firebase Analytics, Google Cloud Monitoring.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Activity Recognition Engine: sensors_plus streams accelerometer (x,y,z) and gyroscope (pitch,roll,yaw) at 100Hz; feature extraction isolate computes 50-sample sliding window features (mean, variance, peak frequency via FFT); inference isolate runs TFLite MobileNetV2 on feature vector and emits {activity: 'running'|'walking'|'idle'|'jumping', confidence: float} via SendPort every 100ms. In-Game Event System: WorkoutSessionNotifier maps classification results to game events (sprint: running confidence > 0.85 sustained 3s, step_milestone: step_count % 100 == 0, jump: jumping confidence > 0.90). Character Progression: XP earned per event type (sprint: 15xp, step_milestone: 10xp); level thresholds at 500xp intervals; achievement unlocks at level 5, 10, 20, total_km 10, 50, 100. Model OTA Updates: background download at app launch, HMAC validation, staged activation post-session. GDPR Consent Flow: granular consent screen before first workout start, storing consent with version and timestamp.
target_output.system_components: Flutter app (main isolate: UI and game rendering; sensor isolate: 100Hz stream processing and feature extraction; inference isolate: flutter_tflite MobileNetV2 INT8 classification; provider state tree: WorkoutSessionNotifier, CharacterProgressionNotifier, ModelVersionNotifier), sqflite local database (offline cache for progression and session history), FastAPI Cloud Run service (auth, progression, sessions, model_version router modules), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA (player data, progression, sessions, consents, model versions), BigQuery (anonymized activity_events_anonymized table, streaming inserts from FastAPI), Cloud Storage bucket (versioned .tflite artifacts with HMAC metadata), Cloud Run Job 'model-retrain' (weekly or drift-triggered retraining pipeline), Firebase Auth (JWT issuance and verification), Firebase Crashlytics (mobile crash reports), Firebase Analytics (workout funnel events).
target_output.workflows: Activity Recognition Loop: sensors_plus emits AccelerometerEvent and GyroscopeEvent at 100Hz to sensor isolate; sensor isolate buffers 50 samples (500ms window), computes feature vector (18 statistical features per axis), sends to inference isolate via SendPort; TFLite MobileNetV2 inference runs in under 50ms and returns classification with confidence; result posted back to main isolate WorkoutSessionNotifier; if activity == 'running' and confidence > 0.85 sustained for 3 consecutive windows, sprint_event emitted; step count incremented per stride detection; step_milestone_event emitted at each 100-step boundary; character XP updated locally in sqflite and queued for backend sync. Model OTA Update Flow: at app launch, ModelVersionNotifier calls GET /model/latest (Firebase JWT header); server returns {version: '1.4.0', download_url: HMAC-signed-GCS-url, hmac_sha256: 'abc123', expires_at: ISO8601}; if version > local_model_version, Dart background isolate begins HTTP chunked download to temp file in app cache directory; on download completion, recomputes HMAC-SHA256 of downloaded file and compares against response value; if valid, moves file to documents/model_v1.4.0.tflite; sets ModelVersionNotifier.pendingVersion = '1.4.0'; when WorkoutSessionNotifier.endSession() fires, calls flutter_tflite.loadModel(path: documents/model_v1.4.0.tflite); deletes old model file; shows 'Activity recognition updated' toast.
target_output.data_model: player_accounts (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR UNIQUE, display_name VARCHAR, email_encrypted BYTEA, region VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). player_consents (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, consent_type ENUM(account_data/activity_sensor), version VARCHAR, granted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ). character_progressions (id UUID PK, player_id UUID UNIQUE FK, level INT DEFAULT 1, total_xp BIGINT DEFAULT 0, total_km NUMERIC(10,3) DEFAULT 0, total_steps BIGINT DEFAULT 0, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). workout_sessions (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ, duration_seconds INT, total_steps INT, total_km NUMERIC(6,3), xp_earned INT, events_json JSONB, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ). achievement_unlocks (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, achievement_id VARCHAR, unlocked_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(player_id, achievement_id)). ml_model_versions (id UUID PK, version VARCHAR UNIQUE, accuracy_pct NUMERIC(5,2), tflite_gcs_path VARCHAR, hmac_sha256 VARCHAR, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_active BOOL). BigQuery table activity_events_anonymized: {event_type STRING, activity_class STRING, confidence FLOAT64, duration_ms INT64, hashed_player_id STRING, session_date DATE}.
target_output.api_design: POST /sessions: body {session_id: UUID, started_at, ended_at, total_steps, total_km, xp_earned, events: [{type, timestamp, value}]}, idempotent on session_id, returns {progression: CharacterProgressionResponse}. GET /progression: returns {level, total_xp, total_km, total_steps, achievements: [{id, unlocked_at}]}. GET /model/latest: returns {version: string, download_url: HMAC-signed-GCS-URL-TTL-1h, hmac_sha256: string, size_bytes: int, expires_at: ISO8601}. POST /progression/events: batch activity event upload for BigQuery streaming insert (anonymized). DELETE /account: GDPR deletion request, marks deleted_at, returns {deletion_scheduled_by: ISO8601}. All routes require Firebase Auth Bearer JWT.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT verified by FastAPI HTTPBearer dependency using firebase-admin verify_id_token on every request. GDPR consent screen appears before first workout sensor access: two separate consents (account_data and activity_sensor) stored in player_consents with consent version string. Raw accelerometer and gyroscope data processed exclusively on-device; FastAPI never receives raw sensor values. BigQuery activity events anonymized by hashing player_id with daily rotating salt (SHA-256) at the FastAPI layer before streaming insert. Model artifact HMAC-SHA256 signatures prevent tampered model delivery; Flutter client recomputes HMAC after download and rejects any mismatch. Cloud Storage model bucket private; download URLs are HMAC-signed with 1-hour TTL generated by FastAPI and never publicly accessible. GDPR right to erasure: DELETE /account soft-deletes player record; Cloud Run Job scans deleted_at > 30 days and hard-deletes personal data from PostgreSQL tables.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Backend: GitHub Actions CI on PR runs pytest (80% coverage gate), mypy --strict, docker build, push to Artifact Registry, deploy to Cloud Run staging. Production deploy on git tag. Cloud Run service: min-instances 1 (prevent morning workout peak cold start), max-instances 10, concurrency 80. Cloud Run Job 'model-retrain' scheduled weekly by Cloud Scheduler; also triggered manually via Cloud Tasks when BigQuery drift detection fires. Mobile: Bitrise pipeline on main merge: flutter test, flutter build ipa and apk, code signing via Bitrise Certificate store, TestFlight upload via Fastlane pilot, Play Store internal track upload via Fastlane supply. Flutter flavors: dev (localhost API), staging (staging Cloud Run URL), production.
target_output.observability: Firebase Crashlytics: automatic mobile crash reports with Flutter stack traces. Firebase Analytics: custom events workout_session_started, activity_classified (activity_class dimension), sprint_event_emitted, step_milestone_reached, achievement_unlocked, model_update_downloaded. Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request latency P99 alert > 500ms, error rate alert > 1%. BigQuery scheduled query: weekly accuracy drift report comparing activity_events_anonymized predicted classes against random-sample player-reported corrections (thumbs-down feedback UI). Model retraining Cloud Run Job emits structured log with accuracy metric to Cloud Logging for trend tracking.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ~$120/month at 5,000 DAU: Cloud Run FastAPI (1 min-instance + burst) $25, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL HA $65, BigQuery storage and query $10, Cloud Storage model artifacts $1, Firebase (Spark plan, Crashlytics free) $0, Bitrise basic plan $29. Model retraining Cloud Run Job: $0.05 per run at 1 hour duration weekly. Scale-to-zero on Cloud Run outside morning/evening workout peaks reduces idle cost. BigQuery streaming inserts ~$0.01 per million events; 5,000 DAU generating 10 events each = 1.5M events/month = $0.015.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-12): Firebase Auth (Google + Apple Sign-In) with GDPR consent, sensors_plus accelerometer stream, TFLite MobileNetV2 INT8 inference in compute isolate (running and step recognition only), sprint and step_milestone events, character XP and leveling, sqflite offline cache, FastAPI session sync and progression endpoints, background model update download flow, Cloud Run deploy, Bitrise CI with TestFlight and Play Store internal track. Phase 2 Jump Recognition and Social (Weeks 13-20): jump recognition model variant, jump_event game action, model retraining Cloud Run Job on BigQuery training data, city leaderboard endpoint, exercise goal progress rings, achievement expansion. Phase 3 Wearable and Personalization (Weeks 21+): Apple Watch / Garmin sensor data integration, adaptive ML model fine-tuning based on individual activity patterns, guild workout challenge features.
target_output.development_guidelines: Dart: flutter analyze --fatal-infos enforced in Bitrise CI. All TFLite inference code in lib/ml/ module with unit tests mocking flutter_tflite interface for classification logic tests. Sensor feature extraction functions are pure Dart (no Flutter dependencies) for easy unit testing with prerecorded sensor data fixtures. Python: mypy --strict in GitHub Actions, pytest with 80% coverage gate, Pydantic v2 models for all FastAPI request and response shapes. GDPR consent version string incremented any time consent copy changes; players with older consent version prompted to re-consent on next launch. ML model file integrity: HMAC validation is blocking; any model swap without valid HMAC aborts and logs error to Firebase Crashlytics.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: flutter_tflite plugin maintenance gap or breaking API change in Flutter upgrade. Mitigation: abstract TFLite calls behind ActivityClassifier interface; alternative implementation using tflite_flutter package ready as drop-in. Risk: MobileNetV2 INT8 quantization accuracy degrades significantly for certain user demographics (different gait patterns, disability). Mitigation: on-device confidence threshold filter rejects low-confidence classifications (< 0.6) and falls back to step counter heuristic. Risk: BigQuery streaming insert costs spike unexpectedly if activity event rate is higher than modeled. Mitigation: Flutter app batches events locally and sends at session end rather than streaming per-event in real time. Tradeoff: on-device model inference over cloud inference for privacy and latency, accepting larger app binary and battery consumption from continuous sensor processing.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed background model download at app launch over blocking dialog at workout start to preserve workout flow. Confirmed INT8 quantization meets 50ms inference SLA after benchmarking on iPhone 12 and Pixel 6 test devices. Selected BigQuery streaming inserts over Cloud Pub/Sub for activity events to reduce architecture components at current scale. GDPR consent implemented as two granular consents rather than single bundled consent per legal guidance on health-adjacent data.

metadata.notes: Fitness-gaming mobile app round 2; resolves model OTA update blocking workout start by implementing background download at app launch with post-session model swap.

project_goal.rationale: Core product vision for the mobile MMORPG launch

complexity_level.rationale: Hundreds of concurrent players per zone demands distributed zone sharding

risk_level.rationale: Handles personal and financial data with real-money in-game purchases
