reasoner_reviews.product: The MMORPG scope is ambitious but well-partitioned. Zone sharding and auction house atomicity are the two highest-value engineering bets. Guild management and real-time party chat are table stakes for the genre and must be rock-solid on launch.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go zone servers with Redis-backed handoff payloads is the right call for sub-100ms zone transitions. The polyglot persistence approach is justified: PostgreSQL for ACID auction transactions, MongoDB for flexible world documents, Redis for ephemeral session state. Recommend a dedicated auction house service to isolate transaction load from zone servers.
reasoner_reviews.security: COPPA age gating must be implemented before soft launch — not as a fast-follow. Auction house requires idempotency keys and velocity limits to prevent currency duplication exploits. GDPR deletion must cascade across all three databases and anonymize telemetry.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $80k/month cap is achievable with EKS Spot node pools for zone servers; on-demand for stateful game services. Multi-region adds roughly 35% overhead versus single-region. Zone capacity must be pre-scaled before influencer campaigns go live.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan left the auction house transaction model underspecified — concurrent bid conflicts and rollback paths were not addressed. The zone handoff Redis payload schema also lacked field definitions. These gaps must be closed in this round.

backend.service_design: Dedicated microservices: ZoneServer (Go StatefulSet per shard), GatewayService (Go, routes players to zone shards), InventoryService (FastAPI), GuildService (FastAPI), AuctionService (FastAPI with PostgreSQL serializable transactions), TokenService (custom JWT issuance)
backend.api_patterns: UDP binary protocol for zone state; REST+JSON for game services at /api/v1; WebSocket for party and guild chat; Kafka events for cross-service domain events
backend.business_modules: Zone capacity manager allocates and tears down zone shards dynamically; AuctionService uses SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for concurrent bid processing; GuildService enforces membership caps and role hierarchies
backend.failure_handling: Zone crash triggers Redis-backed player reconnect with last-known state recovery within 30 seconds; auction write failures roll back fully with idempotency key deduplication
backend.scaling_notes: Zone servers scale horizontally as StatefulSets; game services scale on CPU 60% target; AuctionService scales independently given its PostgreSQL bottleneck characteristics

frontend.app_structure: Unity 6 scene graph with additive zone loading; UI built with Unity UI Toolkit for inventory, guild, and auction house panels
frontend.pages_and_flows: Character select, world map with zone entry, zone gameplay canvas, party HUD overlay, guild roster panel, auction house browse and list screens
frontend.state_management: Client-side prediction for player movement with server reconciliation at each snapshot; inventory and auction state fetched via REST on panel open with local cache invalidation on WebSocket events
frontend.performance_notes: Zone geometry streamed via addressable asset bundles; texture compression BC7 for iOS (ASTC fallback); frame budget targets 60fps on iPhone 14 class hardware

security.auth_design: Cognito for initial player registration and social login federation; custom TokenService issues short-lived game tokens (15-minute TTL) signed with HMAC-SHA256 for zone server authentication
security.authorization_model: Player roles: standard, premium, guild_officer, guild_master; admin panel roles: gm (game master), senior_gm, ops with AuditLog write-back on all admin actions
security.abuse_prevention: Auction house: 50 concurrent listings cap per player, velocity limit 20 listings per hour, price floor and ceiling bounds per item category; zone: server-authoritative position validation rejecting teleport deltas
security.privacy_controls: GDPR deletion job cascades across PostgreSQL player records, MongoDB world references, and anonymizes ClickHouse telemetry; COPPA accounts restricted from chat and auction house until parental consent recorded

data.entities: player_characters (id, account_id, name, class ENUM warrior/mage/archer/healer, level, zone_id, position_json, created_at), game_zones (id, zone_template_id, shard_index, current_players, status ENUM active/draining/terminated), guild_memberships (id, guild_id, player_id, role ENUM member/officer/master, joined_at), auction_listings (id, seller_player_id, item_id, quantity, buyout_price, currency, status ENUM active/sold/expired, listed_at, expires_at), inventory_items (id, player_id, item_template_id, quantity, attributes_json, acquired_at), transaction_records (id, buyer_id, seller_id, auction_listing_id, amount, currency, completed_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with pgpool-II for connection pooling; MongoDB 7 replica set for world state documents; Redis 7 cluster (6 nodes) for zone handoff, session tokens, and auction listing cache
data.retention_and_deletion: Player PII purged 2 years post-deletion; transaction records archived to S3 Parquet at 7 years; telemetry anonymized at 90 days via scheduled PostgreSQL and ClickHouse jobs

devops.deployment_topology: EKS multi-region with dedicated node groups: Spot m6i.2xlarge pool for zone server StatefulSets, on-demand c6i.xlarge pool for game services, GPU nodes absent (all CPU workloads)
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite runs parallel iOS (Xcode 16) and Android (Gradle) Unity builds; server builds containerized via Docker and pushed to ECR; ArgoCD GitOps syncs Kubernetes manifests per region
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with custom spans for zone tick processing and auction transaction latency; Datadog metrics for zone_player_density, auction_listings_per_minute, chat_message_lag_p99; PagerDuty for zone crash rate > 0.1% or auction failure rate > 0.01%
devops.rollback_strategy: Game service rollouts use EKS rolling updates with readiness gates; zone server upgrades drain zones before terminating old pods via preStop lifecycle hook

target_output.title: Mobile MMORPG Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale mobile MMORPG platform for iOS and Android built with Unity 6, Go zone servers, and Python FastAPI game services on multi-region AWS EKS. Supports hundreds of simultaneous players per zone via dynamic shard provisioning, real-time party and guild communication over WebSocket, an atomic PostgreSQL auction house, and GDPR-plus-COPPA compliant player onboarding with parental consent flows. Round 3 resolves auction concurrent bid serialization and COPPA consent gating.
target_output.architecture_overview: Unity 6 C# client communicates over DTLS 1.2 UDP to stateful Go zone server shards for sub-100ms gameplay state and over TLS 1.3 HTTPS to Python FastAPI game services for inventory, guild, and auction operations. A Go gateway service routes incoming player connections to the correct zone shard by evaluating current shard player density from Redis. Zone server StatefulSets on EKS Spot nodes handle zone lifecycle; on-demand node groups host stateless FastAPI services. Three database layers: PostgreSQL 16 for player accounts and auction transactions (ACID), MongoDB 7 replica set for game world documents, Redis 7 cluster for zone handoff payloads and session tokens. Multi-region EKS clusters in us-east-1, eu-west-1, and ap-southeast-1 with Route 53 latency-based routing for regional player affinity.
target_output.technology_stack: Client: Unity 6 C# with Netcode for GameObjects and custom UDP zone protocol. Zone backend: Go 1.22 with custom game server framework, DTLS 1.2 via pion/dtls. Game services: Python 3.12 FastAPI with SQLAlchemy (async) and Motor (MongoDB async driver). Databases: PostgreSQL 16 with pgpool-II, MongoDB 7 replica set, Redis 7 cluster. Auth: AWS Cognito + Firebase Auth federation + custom HMAC-SHA256 game token service. Infrastructure: AWS EKS multi-region, ECR, ALB, Route 53, S3, SES, Buildkite CI, ArgoCD GitOps, Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Zone Navigation: dynamic shard provisioning with player density balancing and seamless handoff. Party System: real-time formation, party chat via WebSocket, leader role delegation. Guild Management: creation, invitation, officer roles, guild chat channel, membership roster. Auction House: item listing with expiry, concurrent-safe buyout via SELECT FOR UPDATE, atomic currency and item transfer. In-App Purchases: IAP currency top-up via Apple/Google IAP with backend receipt verification and currency credit. COPPA Compliance: age gate at registration, parental consent flow, restricted mode for unverified minors. Admin Panel: GM tools for player lookup, suspension, item grant, auction house audit log review.
target_output.system_components: GatewayService (Go): authenticates game tokens, routes players to zone shard, manages shard registry in Redis. ZoneServer (Go StatefulSet per shard): authoritative game state, player position validation, party event broadcasting. InventoryService (FastAPI): CRUD for player inventory items with optimistic locking. GuildService (FastAPI): guild lifecycle, membership management, role enforcement. AuctionService (FastAPI): listing creation, bid processing with SELECT FOR UPDATE serialization, expiry sweep job. TokenService (Go): issues and validates HMAC-SHA256 game tokens. PostgreSQL 16: player_characters, auction_listings, transaction_records, parental_consent_records. MongoDB 7: world region documents, NPC spawn configs, zone template definitions. Redis 7 cluster: zone handoff payloads (TTL 60s), session tokens, auction listing hot cache.
target_output.workflows: Player Registration with COPPA Gate: player submits DOB and email; if age >= 13, Cognito account created and game token issued immediately; if age < 13, account created in restricted mode, SES sends parental consent email with verification link, account remains in read-only mode until parent confirms consent -- auction house and chat features gated behind consent flag. Zone Transition: player approaches zone boundary, client sends zone_enter request to GatewayService; gateway queries Redis shard registry for target zone, selects shard with headroom below 200-player cap; Go zone server serializes player state (position, HP, buffs, party_id) into Redis handoff payload with 60-second TTL; client disconnects from source shard and reconnects to target shard UDP endpoint; target shard reads handoff payload, validates freshness, and emits player_entered event to all nearby clients. Auction House Concurrent Buyout: buyer sends POST /api/v1/auction/:listing_id/buy with idempotency_key; AuctionService opens PostgreSQL serializable transaction, executes SELECT FOR UPDATE on the auction_listings row; if listing status is active, debits buyer currency, credits seller, sets listing status to sold, transfers inventory item ownership atomically, commits; if another transaction holds the lock, the loser receives 409 Conflict with the winning buyer's idempotency_key; client retries up to 3 times with 200ms exponential backoff. IAP Currency Purchase: player initiates currency purchase in Unity client via StoreKit/Billing Library; on successful platform purchase, client sends receipt to /api/v1/payments/iap/verify; backend validates receipt with Apple/Google validation API, credits currency to player account in PostgreSQL, records transaction.
target_output.data_model: player_characters (id UUID PK, account_id FK->cognito_sub, display_name, class ENUM warrior/mage/archer/healer, level INT, experience BIGINT, zone_id FK, position_json, hp_current INT, coppa_restricted BOOL DEFAULT false, created_at). parental_consent_records (id UUID PK, player_account_id FK, parent_email_encrypted, consent_token, consent_status ENUM pending/confirmed/revoked, consented_at, revoked_at). game_zones (id UUID PK, zone_template_id FK, shard_index SMALLINT, node_endpoint TEXT, current_players INT, max_players INT DEFAULT 200, status ENUM active/draining/terminated, created_at). guild_memberships (id UUID PK, guild_id FK, player_id FK, role ENUM member/officer/master, joined_at). auction_listings (id UUID PK, seller_player_id FK, item_template_id, quantity INT, buyout_price BIGINT, currency ENUM gold/gems, status ENUM active/sold/expired/cancelled, listed_at, expires_at). transaction_records (id UUID PK, buyer_id FK, seller_id FK, auction_listing_id FK, amount BIGINT, currency, idempotency_key TEXT UNIQUE, completed_at). inventory_items (id UUID PK, player_id FK, item_template_id, quantity INT, attributes_json, acquired_at).
target_output.api_design: REST game services at /api/v1 with HMAC game token auth. POST /auth/register (create Cognito account, return game token or initiate COPPA flow). POST /auth/token/refresh (rotate game token). GET /players/:id/inventory (paginated item list). POST /auction/listings (create listing: item_id, quantity, buyout_price, expires_in_hours). GET /auction/listings?item_template_id=&sort=price_asc (browse). POST /auction/listings/:id/buy (idempotency_key required, returns 200 ok / 409 conflict). GET /guilds/:id/members. POST /guilds/:id/invite. POST /payments/iap/verify (receipt validation and currency credit). Admin: GET /admin/players/:id, POST /admin/players/:id/suspend, GET /admin/auction/audit-log. WebSocket /ws/party/:party_id for real-time party chat and member events. UDP zone protocol: ZoneEnter, PositionUpdate, ZoneLeave, PartyEvent message types.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth: Cognito for account creation with Firebase social login federation; custom HMAC-SHA256 game tokens (15-minute TTL) presented to zone servers and game service APIs; token refresh endpoint issues new token before expiry. Authorization: player roles standard/premium; guild roles member/officer/master; admin panel roles gm/senior_gm/ops with MFA enforced and all actions written to AuditLog. COPPA: DOB collected at registration; under-13 accounts enter restricted mode; SES parental consent email dispatched within 30 seconds; auction house, party chat, and guild creation gated behind consent_status=confirmed flag. GDPR: right-to-deletion cascades across PostgreSQL player records, MongoDB world references, and anonymizes ClickHouse telemetry within 30 days; lawful basis is contract performance for game services. Auction anti-abuse: 50 active listing cap per player, 20 new listings per hour velocity limit, price bounds enforced per item category. Transport: TLS 1.3 for REST; DTLS 1.2 for UDP zone traffic. WAF rules on ALB block SQL injection, anomalous auction patterns, and account takeover credential stuffing.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: EKS multi-region clusters in us-east-1, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1. Node groups: Spot m6i.2xlarge pool for ZoneServer StatefulSets (auto-scaling 10-80 nodes per region); on-demand c6i.xlarge pool for FastAPI game services (HPA on CPU 60%); on-demand r6i.2xlarge for PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ per region. Buildkite CI: parallel Unity iOS (Xcode 16 on macOS runners) and Android (Gradle on Linux) builds; server Docker builds pushed to ECR; unit and integration tests gated before ECR push. ArgoCD GitOps syncs Kubernetes manifests from git per environment (dev, staging, us-prod, eu-prod, apac-prod). Game service deployments use rolling update strategy with readiness probe on /health; ZoneServer upgrades drain active players via preStop hook before pod termination. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation for PostgreSQL credentials, game token signing key. Route 53 latency-based routing distributes players to nearest regional EKS cluster.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with distributed traces spanning GatewayService -> ZoneServer UDP handling -> AuctionService PostgreSQL transactions. Custom Datadog metrics: zone_player_density (per shard), auction_buyout_latency_p99, auction_conflict_rate, chat_message_lag_p99, iap_verification_failure_rate, coppa_consent_pending_count. PagerDuty alerts: zone shard crash rate > 0.1%, auction failure rate > 0.01%, game token issuance error rate > 0.5%, IAP verification timeout > 2%. PostgreSQL slow query log shipped to Datadog for auction and inventory query analysis. Redis cluster memory utilization alert at 75%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $62k/month at soft launch (500 CCU per region): ECS Spot zone nodes ~$18k, on-demand game services ~$12k, RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL 3 regions ~$14k, MongoDB Atlas ~$6k, Redis cluster ~$5k, data transfer and misc ~$7k. Scales to $78k at 2000 CCU with Spot node pool expansion. Zone server horizontal scaling triggered at shard player density > 180. AuctionService Postgres connection pool managed via pgpool-II preventing connection exhaustion at scale. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for telemetry cold data reduces storage cost 40% after 30-day threshold.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Soft Launch (12 weeks): player registration with COPPA age gate, zone navigation with 50-player shards, basic party formation and chat, auction house with concurrent bid serialization, IAP currency purchase, Datadog observability, multi-region EKS us-east-1 and eu-west-1. Phase 2 Launch Hardening (6 weeks): guild system full feature set including wars and treasury, auction house performance tuning for 10k concurrent listings, ap-southeast-1 region, admin GM panel with audit log, push notification for guild and auction events via SNS. Phase 3 Expansion (8 weeks): player housing zone type, cross-region item trading federation, PC client port build pipeline, advanced analytics dashboard for engagement KPIs.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go zone server code requires benchmark tests for zone tick processing to maintain < 5ms tick budget. FastAPI game services use async SQLAlchemy with connection pool limits enforced. All auction-related database operations must be in explicit transactions with idempotency_key uniqueness constraint. Unity client networking code reviewed by senior engineer before merging to prevent client-authoritative exploits. Feature flags (LaunchDarkly) gate all new game features for gradual rollout. Buildkite pipeline fails on any unit test regression. COPPA flow changes require legal sign-off before merging.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Zone server Spot instance interruption during peak concurrent zone load causing player disconnection spikes. Mitigation: preStop drain hook saves player state to Redis handoff payload; client reconnect logic retries to newly provisioned shard within 30 seconds. Risk: PostgreSQL AuctionService becomes bottleneck at 10k+ concurrent listings with high bid volume. Mitigation: pgpool-II connection pooling, read replica for listing browse queries, archive expired listings to S3 hourly to keep active index small. Tradeoff: DTLS 1.2 for UDP zone traffic adds 8ms handshake overhead on initial zone entry but provides integrity guarantees preventing position injection attacks. Tradeoff: Polyglot persistence (3 databases) increases operational complexity but each database is justified by workload characteristics -- ACID for finance, document for world config, in-memory for session state.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Auction concurrent bid serialization: SELECT FOR UPDATE in serializable PostgreSQL transaction with 409 Conflict response and 3-retry client backoff -- resolved in round 3. COPPA onboarding gate: DOB capture at registration, under-13 restricted mode, SES parental consent email with 30-second SLA -- resolved in round 3. Zone shard capacity: 200-player hard cap per shard enforced by gateway before routing -- confirmed. Multi-region database consistency: each region has independent PostgreSQL primary; cross-region trades deferred to Phase 3 as a federated design problem.

metadata.notes: Round 3 MMORPG revision closing COPPA onboarding and auction concurrency gaps with full SELECT FOR UPDATE semantics and parental consent flow.

project.goal: Node-based adaptive music graph editor with real-time preview and Wwise/FMOD export
project.target_users: Indie game composers and audio designers
project.timeline: MVP in 14 weeks, v1.0 in 22 weeks

frontend.approach: Reactive graph canvas with drag-and-drop node placement and connection routing

backend.framework: Tauri command handlers for audio engine IPC
backend.api: Tauri invoke API between frontend and Rust backend

security.auth: None (local app)
security.code_signing: Required for macOS notarization and Windows SmartScreen

data.cache: In-memory Rust graph state during active session

open_questions.export_format: Confirm whether Wwise WAAPI export requires local Wwise installation or generates project XML directly

confirmed_decisions.audio_engine: Rust CPAL for cross-platform audio output

reasoner_reviews.product: The core authoring loop -- place clip nodes, wire transitions, preview in real time, export to middleware -- is clearly scoped and validated against composer pain points. Wwise as the first export target covers the majority of indie game audio workflows. The constraint on no proprietary runtime dependencies is critical to maintain.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Tauri 2 with Svelte 5 is well-suited here: the IPC boundary between the Svelte canvas and Rust audio engine is clean, and Tauri sidecar support allows bundling the CPAL audio runtime without requiring user-installed runtimes. The SQLite project file approach is correct; schema versioning strategy must be defined early to avoid migration pain on format changes.
reasoner_reviews.security: Binary signing for all three platforms must be integrated into the CI pipeline before first public release; macOS notarization requires an Apple Developer account and cannot be bolted on later. The opt-in Sentry crash reporter must present a clear consent dialog on first launch.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 50MB binary size budget is achievable with Tauri but requires auditing Rust dependency chains; large audio processing crates can inflate size. The offline-first constraint is solid and no external service calls should be introduced without explicit design review.

backend.service_design: Rust Tauri command handlers in modules: audio_engine (CPAL playback, graph evaluation loop), graph_compiler (validates node graph, resolves transition DAG, computes audio routing), exporter (Wwise WAAPI XML generator, FMOD .fspro serializer), project_io (SQLite read/write with schema migrations via rusqlite)
backend.api_patterns: Tauri invoke API with typed command signatures; async Rust handlers for audio graph evaluation; synchronous handlers for project save/load
backend.failure_handling: Audio engine panics caught at Tauri command boundary and returned as structured error payloads to Svelte; project saves are atomic via write-to-temp-then-rename

frontend.app_structure: Single-window Svelte 5 SPA with three panels: graph canvas (center), audio clip library (left sidebar), properties inspector (right sidebar)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Onboarding screen on first launch (project create/open), main editor view with graph canvas, export dialog with format selection and path picker
frontend.state_management: Svelte 5 runes for reactive graph node and edge state; project dirty-state tracking for unsaved change prompts

security.auth_design: Not applicable; no authentication layer required for local single-user tool
security.secrets_management: No secrets required; Sentry DSN embedded at build time via Tauri environment injection
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Local log file in OS app data dir; Sentry error captures with user opt-in consent recorded in local config file

data.entities: audio_projects (id, name, created_at, updated_at, format_version), music_layers (id, project_id, name, clip_path, volume, loop_enabled), graph_nodes (id, project_id, node_type ENUM clip/mixer/trigger, position_x, position_y, config_json), transition_rules (id, project_id, source_node_id, target_node_id, trigger_state, fade_duration_ms, crossfade_curve), export_configurations (id, project_id, target_format ENUM wwise/fmod, output_path, last_exported_at)

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions matrix build for three targets: macos-latest (x86_64 + arm64 universal), windows-latest (x86_64 MSI), ubuntu-22.04 (AppImage)

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an offline-first desktop audio composition tool. Key decisions: Tauri 2 with Svelte 5 for the canvas UI and Rust CPAL for the audio engine communicating over Tauri IPC commands. SQLite project files with schema versioning handle persistence without external services. The export pipeline targets Wwise XML generation first with FMOD as a future target. Binary signing and macOS notarization must be CI-native from day one.
target_output.title: Adaptive Audio Graph Composer -- Desktop Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A cross-platform desktop tool built with Tauri 2 and Svelte 5 enabling game composers to author node-based adaptive music systems and export them to Wwise or FMOD middleware formats. The Rust audio engine provides real-time graph evaluation and CPAL-based preview playback within a 50MB offline binary. Projects are stored as portable SQLite files with schema versioning for forward compatibility across releases.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Tauri 2 desktop application with a WebView frontend (Svelte 5) and a Rust backend communicating over the Tauri IPC command bus. The Svelte canvas renders the audio node graph using reactive runes, invoking typed Tauri commands for engine operations. The Rust side hosts four modules: the CPAL audio engine for real-time playback, the graph compiler for DAG validation and routing computation, the exporter for Wwise WAAPI XML and FMOD .fspro generation, and the project I/O layer backed by a rusqlite connection. No network egress occurs at runtime; the optional Sentry crash reporter uses a consent-gated background uploader.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2, Svelte 5, TypeScript, custom SVG-based graph canvas with D3 edge routing. Backend: Rust stable, CPAL 0.15 for cross-platform audio I/O, rusqlite 0.31, serde_json for graph serialization. Build: cargo build + Tauri CLI, cross-platform via GitHub Actions matrix. Distribution: macOS universal binary (notarized), Windows MSI (code-signed), Linux AppImage. Crash reporting: Sentry Rust SDK with user-consent gate.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Audio Graph Canvas: drag-and-drop clip nodes, mixer nodes, and state_trigger nodes; SVG edge routing for transition connections; inline property editing. Real-Time Preview: Rust CPAL engine evaluates the compiled graph and streams audio output at 48kHz; supports layer soloing and muting. State Trigger Configuration: composers define named in-game state variables and bind them to transition edges with fade duration and crossfade curve settings. Export Pipeline: serializes the validated graph to Wwise WAAPI XML project structure or FMOD .fspro JSON with embedded clip references. Project Management: open/save/autosave to .acproj SQLite files; recent projects list; format migration on open.
target_output.system_components: Svelte 5 WebView (graph canvas, sidebars, export dialog), Tauri IPC bridge (typed command and event bus), Rust audio_engine module (CPAL device setup, graph evaluation loop, real-time mixing), Rust graph_compiler module (DAG validation, cycle detection, routing resolution), Rust exporter module (Wwise XML serializer, FMOD JSON serializer), Rust project_io module (rusqlite connection, schema migration runner), SQLite .acproj project file, GitHub Releases artifact store, opt-in Sentry crash uploader.
target_output.workflows: Graph Authoring and Preview: the composer drags an audio_clip node onto the canvas, sets the clip path relative to the project directory, adds a state_trigger node bound to variable 'combat_intensity', draws a transition edge with 500ms fade_duration, and clicks Preview; the 'compile_and_play' Tauri command is invoked, the Rust graph compiler validates the DAG and resolves audio routing, and CPAL begins streaming the layered output to the default audio device. Wwise Export: the composer selects File > Export > Wwise, picks an output directory, and invokes 'export_wwise'; the exporter traverses the compiled graph, generates Wwise WAAPI-compatible XML with Work Units per layer and Event entries per transition, embeds relative clip references, and writes the output package; a dialog confirms file count and any missing clip warnings.
target_output.data_model: audio_projects (id INTEGER PK, name TEXT, format_version INTEGER, created_at TEXT, updated_at TEXT). music_layers (id INTEGER PK, project_id FK, name TEXT, clip_relative_path TEXT, base_volume REAL, loop_enabled INTEGER). graph_nodes (id INTEGER PK, project_id FK, node_type TEXT CHECK IN (clip, mixer, state_trigger), position_x REAL, position_y REAL, config_json TEXT). transition_rules (id INTEGER PK, project_id FK, source_node_id FK, target_node_id FK, trigger_state TEXT, fade_duration_ms INTEGER, crossfade_curve TEXT). export_configurations (id INTEGER PK, project_id FK, target_format TEXT CHECK IN (wwise, fmod), output_path TEXT, last_exported_at TEXT). Schema version in project_meta (key TEXT PK, value TEXT).
target_output.api_design: Tauri IPC commands invoked from Svelte: compile_and_play(project_id: number) -> AudioEngineStatus, stop_playback() -> void, save_project(payload: ProjectPayload) -> SaveResult, open_project(path: string) -> ProjectPayload, export_wwise(project_id: number, output_dir: string) -> ExportResult {files_written: number, warnings: string[]}, export_fmod(project_id: number, output_dir: string) -> ExportResult, get_audio_devices() -> AudioDevice[], set_preview_device(device_id: string) -> void. Tauri events from Rust: 'engine-state-changed', 'autosave-completed', 'sentry-consent-required'.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Code signing: macOS universal binary notarized via Apple Developer ID; Windows MSI signed with EV certificate to suppress SmartScreen warnings. Auto-update checksums verified by Tauri updater before applying. Sentry: DSN embedded at build time; first-launch consent dialog records choice in local config; no Sentry calls until consent granted. No network sockets opened at runtime except opt-in Sentry uploader thread. File access restricted to paths opened via OS file picker dialogs.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix with jobs for macos-latest (universal .dmg), windows-latest (.msi), ubuntu-22.04 (.AppImage). Pipeline stages: checkout, cargo test --all-features, vitest, tauri build per platform, platform-specific code signing, upload to GitHub Release. Release cadence: tagged releases only; pre-release builds on main for internal testing. No server infrastructure; all operational burden is GitHub Actions runner minutes and GitHub Releases bandwidth. Rollback by directing users to re-download prior tagged release.
target_output.observability: Sentry Rust SDK captures panics, unhandled errors, and structured breadcrumbs; session replays disabled. Local log file at ~/Library/Application Support/AudioComposer/logs/ on macOS with daily rotation and 7-file retention. Sentry release health dashboard shows crash-free session rate and top error fingerprints per release version. Key metric: export success rate per format (Wwise vs FMOD) tracked via Sentry breadcrumbs.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Near-zero infrastructure cost: GitHub Actions minutes for CI (~30 min per release across three platform jobs) and GitHub Releases CDN bandwidth for downloads. Sentry free tier covers indie-scale crash volume. No server hosting required. Distribution scales automatically via GitHub CDN without configuration changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks): Svelte 5 canvas with clip and mixer nodes, Rust CPAL engine with compile_and_play, SQLite project save/load, Wwise XML export, GitHub Actions CI for all three platforms with code signing. Phase 2 v1.0 (8 weeks): state_trigger nodes, transition rule editor with crossfade curve presets, FMOD .fspro export, autosave with crash-safe temp file, recent projects list, Sentry opt-in crash reporting. Phase 3: cloud project sync, plugin API for custom node types, P2P collaborative session sharing.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy with deny(warnings) enforced in CI; unit tests for graph_compiler (DAG cycle detection, routing resolution) and exporter (Wwise XML output schema validation). Svelte: TypeScript strict mode; Vitest component tests for canvas state. All Tauri commands return typed Result<T, AppError> and never panic at the IPC boundary. SQLite migrations are append-only numbered SQL files embedded in binary; existing migration files are never mutated.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Wwise WAAPI XML schema changes between Wwise versions breaking exports. Mitigation: target Wwise 2023.x schema with documented version check on export; warn users if Wwise project version is incompatible. Risk: CPAL device enumeration failures on Linux with ALSA vs PipeWire backends. Mitigation: expose device selection UI and fall back to system default device. Tradeoff: SQLite single-file project format over custom binary -- chosen for human-readable debugging and tooling compatibility at the cost of slightly larger file sizes for dense node graphs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Wwise export generates WAAPI-compatible XML directly without requiring a local Wwise installation; no proprietary SDK runtime needed at export time. Selected CPAL over PortAudio to keep dependency tree pure Rust. Autosave writes to a sibling .acproj.autosave file rather than the main project to prevent corruption on crash.

metadata.notes: Offline desktop audio graph tool for indie composers; Tauri+Rust+Svelte stack with SQLite project files and Wwise/FMOD export.

project_goal.value: Dedicated leaderboard and ranking microservice extracted from monolithic game backend, exposing REST and WebSocket APIs for score submission, ranked list queries with percentile context, and live rank-change event subscriptions across multiple game modes and geographic regions
project_goal.rationale: Microservices decomposition to isolate ranking workload from game monolith

target_users.value: Game servers and partner game studios submitting scores and querying ranked lists via authenticated service tokens
target_users.rationale: Service-to-service API with no direct end-user browser traffic

project_class.rationale: Pure backend service with REST and WebSocket interfaces, no frontend required

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, realtime, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Ranking service needs score writes, ranked reads, service auth, public API surface, real-time subscriptions, and analytics snapshotting

complexity_level.rationale: Millions of concurrent players across global regions with sub-100ms rank query SLO

risk_level.rationale: Competitive ranking data drives in-game rewards; integrity matters but no PII at risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Score and ranking data is game-internal; no personal financial or health data

external_exposure.rationale: Partner game studios consume the API with service tokens; not open to anonymous public

access_model.value: HMAC-signed JWT service tokens issued per partner game studio; scoped to read or write per game mode
access_model.rationale: Service-to-service auth model; human user auth not applicable

feature_scope.value: Score submission with anti-cheat bounds validation, ranked list queries with percentile and rank-delta context, WebSocket subscriptions for live rank-change events, multi-region leaderboard partitioning, per-game-mode leaderboard configurations, nightly archive snapshots for rewards and analytics
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature scope extracted from monolith roadmap

mvp_scope.value: Score submission, top-1000 ranked list queries, basic WebSocket rank-change events, single-region deployment for one game title
mvp_scope.rationale: Validate microservice extraction before rolling out multi-region and multi-title support

security_baseline.value: mTLS between game servers and leaderboard service, HMAC-signed JWT token validation, score bound enforcement per game mode config, rate limiting 10k req/min per partner token
security_baseline.rationale: Competitive integrity requires token scoping and anti-cheat bounds; mTLS for service mesh

frontend_stack.value: None - API service only, no frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: api_service project class with no browser-facing UI

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin; gorilla/websocket for WebSocket handling
backend_stack.rationale: Go performance characteristics suit sub-100ms ranking queries at high concurrency

data_platform.value: Redis 7 sorted sets for live leaderboards; PostgreSQL 16 for persistent score history and game mode configs
data_platform.rationale: Redis ZADD/ZRANK provide O(log N) rank operations; PostgreSQL for durable historical records

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 with read replicas deployed to eu-west-1 and ap-southeast-1
hosting_target.rationale: Multi-region read replicas reduce latency for partner studios outside US

privacy_retention_policy.value: Score submission logs retained 90 days; end-of-season leaderboard snapshots retained indefinitely in cold storage; no personal data collected
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No PII in the service; retention driven by reward audit and analytics requirements

future_scope.value: Per-player historical rank graphs, anti-cheat ML anomaly scoring integration, cross-title unified ranking
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap pending successful microservice extraction

constraints.value: P99 score submission latency under 50ms; P99 rank query latency under 100ms; budget under $4000/month
constraints.rationale: SLOs inherited from monolith baseline and partner SLA commitments

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on all ECS tasks; custom metrics for score submission rate, rank query latency, WebSocket subscriber count, Redis sorted set cardinality per leaderboard
observability_baseline.rationale: Competitive service requires fine-grained visibility into rank computation performance

execution_preference.value: Agile with 2-week sprints; trunk-based development with feature flags for multi-region rollout
execution_preference.rationale: Iterative extraction approach to reduce risk of monolith disruption

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II controls required; deployment pipeline and API access must be logged for audit; partner token issuance process documented
compliance_context.rationale: Service handles competitive ranking data influencing in-game rewards with real monetary value

project.goal: Extract leaderboard service from game monolith into standalone ranked-scoring microservice
project.target_users: Game servers and partner studios via service token auth
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks, multi-region in Q4

security.auth: HMAC-signed JWT service tokens per partner

reasoner_reviews.product: The microservice extraction is well-scoped. Redis sorted sets are the correct primitive for O(log N) ZADD and ZRANK operations at millions of entries. The nightly PostgreSQL snapshot strategy cleanly decouples live ranking performance from historical analytics workloads.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Gin is an excellent choice for the sub-50ms submission SLO. Use a dedicated goroutine pool for WebSocket broadcast to avoid head-of-line blocking when rank-change events burst during popular match completions. The regional read replica strategy needs a cache invalidation plan when the primary Redis is updated.
reasoner_reviews.security: HMAC-signed JWTs with game-mode-scoped claims prevent partner studios from writing scores to leaderboards they do not own. Anti-cheat score bounds must be validated server-side against the game_mode_configs table before any Redis write. SOC 2 requires comprehensive API access logging including partner token ID on every request.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $4000/month budget is achievable with Fargate auto-scaling and Redis ElastiCache reserved instances. P99 50ms submission SLO is tight for cold-start scenarios; ensure minimum task count keeps at least 2 warm instances per region.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The WebSocket subscriber architecture needs a Pub/Sub fan-out design using Redis channels for rank-change event propagation rather than a naive per-connection goroutine model, which would exhaust file descriptors at high_scale with thousands of simultaneous connections per region.

backend.service_design: Three Go packages in one binary: ScoreService (ZADD/ZRANK operations, anti-cheat bounds validation from game_mode_configs), QueryService (ranked list pagination with percentile from ZRANK/ZCARD), SubscriptionService (WebSocket session management with Redis Pub/Sub fan-out)
backend.api_patterns: REST endpoints versioned at /v1/leaderboards; WebSocket upgrade at /v1/leaderboards/{id}/subscribe; all routes require Authorization: Bearer {HMAC-JWT} header validated by middleware before handler invocation
backend.background_jobs: Nightly archiver cron ECS task snapshots top-10000 entries per leaderboard from Redis ZRANGE into PostgreSQL player_snapshots with season_id and snapshot_at; completes within 20 minutes at current cardinality
backend.scaling_notes: Horizontal scaling via ECS CPU 60% target auto-scaling; stateless handler design allows any task to serve any request; Redis ElastiCache cluster-mode for sorted set sharding across game modes
backend.failure_handling: Score submission idempotency key stored in Redis with 24h TTL to prevent duplicate rank updates; WebSocket reconnect with session resume token to re-subscribe without missing events

security.auth_design: HMAC-SHA256 signed JWTs with kid claim for partner key version; 1-hour token expiry; partner key exchange endpoint protected by MFA-gated IAM role
security.authorization_model: Token claims include allowed_game_modes[] and permission ENUM read/write; middleware enforces game mode scope before any Redis ZADD or ZRANGE operation
security.abuse_prevention: Score bound enforcement: submitted scores outside game_mode_config min/max rejected with 422; velocity check holds player submissions if >50 per minute per player_id for manual review
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON logs on every API call include partner_token_kid, player_id, leaderboard_id, score, rank_before, rank_after, latency_ms; forwarded to CloudWatch Logs with 90-day retention for SOC 2 audit evidence

data.entities: leaderboard_definitions (id, game_mode, region, score_min, score_max, tick_rate_hz, active), score_submissions (id, leaderboard_id, player_id, score, submitted_at, partner_token_kid, accepted, reject_reason), player_snapshots (leaderboard_id, player_id, rank, score, percentile, season_id, snapshot_at), regional_partitions (leaderboard_id, region, redis_endpoint, is_primary)
data.storage_design: Redis sorted sets keyed lb:{leaderboard_id}:{region} with player_id as member and score as sort value; PostgreSQL for game_mode_configs, score audit trail, and end-of-season snapshots; Redis Pub/Sub channels lb:{leaderboard_id}:events for rank-change broadcast
data.analytics_events: Submission rate per game_mode, rank-change event volume per leaderboard, query latency histograms, WebSocket subscriber gauge per leaderboard, archiver snapshot duration per season

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate primary cluster us-east-1 with ElastiCache primary Redis; read-optimized replicas in eu-west-1 and ap-southeast-1 with local Redis replicas serving GET and WebSocket subscribe; score writes geo-routed to us-east-1
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go test with race detector, go vet, golangci-lint, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with minimum healthy percent 100% and health check grace 30s; integration tests via testcontainers-go
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM via dd-trace-go; custom metrics: leaderboard_submission_rate, rank_query_p99_ms, ws_subscriber_count, redis_zset_cardinality; PagerDuty alert on P99 submission >60ms
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS deploy circuit breaker automatic rollback on >5% 5xx over 60s; Redis sorted sets write-idempotent so partial rollback does not corrupt ranking state

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a high-scale leaderboard microservice extracted from a game monolith. Core decisions: Redis sorted sets as primary ranking store for O(log N) ZADD and ZRANK operations; PostgreSQL as historical audit and snapshot tier; WebSocket fan-out via Redis Pub/Sub to avoid per-connection goroutine exhaustion at scale; multi-region ECS read replicas for global P99 latency compliance; SOC 2 audit logging on every API call with partner token attribution.
target_output.title: Game Leaderboard and Ranking Microservice Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale, partner-facing leaderboard API service built in Go 1.22 on AWS ECS Fargate, using Redis 7 sorted sets for live ranking operations and PostgreSQL 16 for durable score history and end-of-season snapshots. The service exposes REST endpoints for score submission and ranked list queries, WebSocket subscriptions for live rank-change events, and multi-region read replicas to meet sub-100ms global query SLOs for partner game studios.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-binary Go service organized into three internal packages: ScoreService handling Redis ZADD score writes with anti-cheat bounds validation, QueryService serving paginated ranked lists with percentile computation from ZRANK and ZCARD, and SubscriptionService managing WebSocket sessions with Redis Pub/Sub fan-out for rank-change events. Traffic enters via ALB with TLS termination; partner JWTs validated at middleware layer before any leaderboard operation. Primary deployment in us-east-1 with ElastiCache primary; eu-west-1 and ap-southeast-1 host read-only replicas serving ranked list queries with local Redis replicas for low-latency reads. Nightly archiver ECS task snapshots top-N ranked entries from Redis into PostgreSQL player_snapshots for reward calculations.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22 with Gin, gorilla/websocket, dd-trace-go, golang-jwt/jwt v5. Data: Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster-mode (sorted sets for live rankings, Pub/Sub for event fan-out), PostgreSQL 16 RDS for score history and game_mode_configs. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate across us-east-1, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1; ECR, ALB, CloudWatch Logs. Observability: Datadog APM. CI: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint and testcontainers-go integration tests.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Score Submission: game servers POST authenticated score events; service validates HMAC JWT scope, enforces anti-cheat score bounds from game_mode_configs, atomically writes Redis ZADD and publishes rank-change event to Pub/Sub channel. Ranked List Query: partners GET paginated top-N with rank, score, percentile (ZRANK / ZCARD ratio), and rank-delta since last query. WebSocket Subscriptions: partners upgrade to WebSocket; Pub/Sub fan-out goroutine broadcasts rank-change payloads to all active sessions. Leaderboard Config Management: admin REST endpoints to create and update game_mode_configs. Nightly Archive: archiver job snapshots ranked entries into PostgreSQL player_snapshots for end-of-season reward processing.
target_output.system_components: ALB with TLS termination and WAF, ECS Fargate cluster (min 2 tasks per region, auto-scales to 20), Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster-mode for sorted set sharding, Redis Pub/Sub channels per leaderboard for rank-change broadcast, PostgreSQL 16 RDS (score_submissions, game_mode_configs, player_snapshots, leaderboard_definitions), ECS scheduled task for nightly archiver, CloudWatch Logs for structured audit logs, Datadog APM sidecar per ECS task.
target_output.workflows: Score Submission: partner game server POSTs to /v1/leaderboards/{id}/scores with Bearer JWT; middleware validates HMAC signature and extracts allowed_game_modes claim; ScoreService checks submitted score against game_mode_config bounds, rejects with 422 if outside range; atomically executes Redis ZADD updating sorted set and fetches new ZRANK; computes rank-delta and publishes JSON payload to lb:{id}:events Pub/Sub channel; writes audit record to score_submissions asynchronously; returns assigned_rank and percentile within 50ms P99. Historical Archive: nightly archiver ECS task starts at 02:00 UTC per region; iterates all active leaderboard_definitions; ZRANGE WITHSCORES fetches top-10000 entries from Redis; bulk-inserts into PostgreSQL player_snapshots with current season_id; entire job completes in under 20 minutes.
target_output.data_model: leaderboard_definitions (id UUID PK, game_mode VARCHAR, region VARCHAR, score_min BIGINT, score_max BIGINT, tick_rate_hz INT, active BOOL, created_at). score_submissions (id UUID PK, leaderboard_id UUID FK, player_id VARCHAR, score BIGINT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, partner_token_kid VARCHAR, accepted BOOL, reject_reason VARCHAR). player_snapshots (id UUID PK, leaderboard_id UUID FK, player_id VARCHAR, rank INT, score BIGINT, percentile NUMERIC, season_id VARCHAR, snapshot_at TIMESTAMPTZ). regional_partitions (leaderboard_id, region, redis_endpoint, is_primary). Redis: lb:{leaderboard_id}:{region} ZSET with player_id as member and score as sort value; lb:{leaderboard_id}:events Pub/Sub channel with JSON rank-change payloads.
target_output.api_design: REST v1 base /v1. POST /v1/leaderboards/{id}/scores body {player_id, score, idempotency_key} returns {rank, percentile, rank_delta}. GET /v1/leaderboards/{id}/scores?limit=100&offset=0 returns [{rank, player_id, score, percentile}]. GET /v1/leaderboards/{id}/scores/{player_id} returns single player rank with surrounding 5 entries. WebSocket GET /v1/leaderboards/{id}/subscribe upgrades connection, receives JSON rank-change events. POST /v1/leaderboards admin creates leaderboard_definition. GET /v1/leaderboards/{id}/snapshots?season_id=S1 returns archived snapshot for reward processing.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HMAC-SHA256 signed JWTs with kid header for key rotation; middleware validates signature, expiry, and allowed_game_modes claim before any leaderboard operation. Score bound enforcement per game_mode_config with 422 rejection and audit log entry on violation. Velocity check holds player submission queue if >50 per minute per player_id. mTLS enforced between ECS tasks and ElastiCache via AWS PrivateLink. Structured audit log on every API call includes partner_token_kid, player_id, leaderboard_id, action, latency_ms for SOC 2 audit evidence with 90-day CloudWatch retention. WAF blocks malformed JWT patterns and excessive request rates per source IP.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: go test with race detector, go vet, golangci-lint, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with minimum healthy percent 100% and health check grace 30s. ECS service auto-scaling: CPU 60% target, min 2 tasks, max 20 tasks per region. Multi-region: primary us-east-1 with active ECS service and ElastiCache primary; eu-west-1 and ap-southeast-1 deployed as read-replica clusters serving GET and WebSocket subscribe only; score writes route to us-east-1 via DNS geo-routing. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for PostgreSQL credentials and partner HMAC signing keys with 30-day rotation. Migrations: golang-migrate via ECS one-off task pre-deploy.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM via dd-trace-go with trace propagation across Gin handlers and Redis client calls. Custom metrics: leaderboard_submission_rate_per_second per game_mode, rank_query_latency_p99_ms, ws_subscriber_count per leaderboard, redis_zset_cardinality per leaderboard, archiver_snapshot_duration_seconds. PagerDuty alerts: P99 submission >60ms, P99 query >120ms, WebSocket error rate >2%, archiver job failure. Structured JSON logs include correlation_id, partner_token_kid, leaderboard_id, latency_ms on every request.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3200/month for 3-region deployment: ECS Fargate 6 tasks baseline across 3 regions, ElastiCache cluster-mode Redis $600/month, RDS PostgreSQL multi-AZ $400/month, ALB and data transfer $200/month. Redis sorted sets handle 10M+ members per leaderboard. ECS auto-scales to 60 tasks across regions during peak tournament hours. Reserved Fargate compute for 2-task baseline per region reduces costs 25% vs on-demand.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Single-region us-east-1 deployment, score submission and ranked list query REST endpoints, HMAC JWT auth, Redis sorted set store, PostgreSQL audit trail, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Datadog APM, anti-cheat bounds enforcement, SOC 2 audit logging. Phase 2 (4 weeks): WebSocket rank-change subscriptions via Redis Pub/Sub fan-out, nightly archiver writing to player_snapshots, multi-game-mode leaderboard configs, percentile computation in query responses. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-region read replicas in eu-west-1 and ap-southeast-1, DNS geo-routing for regional query affinity, velocity check hold queue, per-partner rate limit enforcement.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with go.sum committed; golangci-lint enforced in CI with staticcheck, errcheck, and gosec linters. Table-driven unit tests for score bounds validation and percentile computation. Integration tests using testcontainers-go for Redis and PostgreSQL submission and query flows. Trunk-based development with feature flags for multi-region rollout via environment variables. ADRs recorded for Redis key schema and Pub/Sub fan-out architecture decisions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis sorted set memory exhaustion if leaderboards accumulate millions of members across hundreds of game modes. Mitigation: per-leaderboard max_members cap in game_mode_config; ZPOPMIN to evict lowest scores when cap exceeded. Risk: WebSocket fan-out latency spike during tournament burst. Mitigation: non-blocking channel sends with slow-consumer drop and reconnect-with-resume design. Tradeoff: us-east-1 primary writes with global read replicas introduces eventual consistency for rank queries in replica regions (typically <500ms lag), acceptable for leaderboard use case. Risk: partner HMAC key compromise. Mitigation: kid versioning allows per-key rotation without service downtime.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Redis sorted sets over DynamoDB for O(log N) rank operations without full table scans. Confirmed ECS Fargate over EKS for reduced operational overhead at current team size. Chose PostgreSQL over ClickHouse for history tier given write volumes in thousands per second. HMAC-signed JWT chosen over OAuth for service-to-service simplicity without identity provider dependency.

metadata.notes: High-scale leaderboard microservice extraction with Redis sorted sets, multi-region read replicas, and SOC 2 audit logging.

project_goal.value: Migrate a live-service game studio from Jenkins-based CI/CD to a GitOps workflow using GitHub Actions and ArgoCD with standardized Helm charts for all game service deployments and automated canary rollouts
project_goal.rationale: Current Jenkins system has no audit trail, lacks deployment standardization across 40+ game services, and requires manual rollback

target_users.value: Game backend engineers, DevOps platform engineers, and release managers at the studio
target_users.rationale: Internal engineering teams are the primary consumers of the deployment infrastructure

project_class.rationale: The deliverable is deployment pipeline infrastructure, not a user-facing application

capabilities.rationale: Covers CI/CD pipelines, Helm chart management, and ArgoCD integration with GitHub and GKE

complexity_level.rationale: 40+ heterogeneous game services with live traffic require careful migration sequencing and canary strategy

risk_level.rationale: Deployment pipeline changes affect all live-service games; mitigated by phased migration with rollback paths

data_sensitivity.rationale: Pipeline configs, Helm values, and deployment secrets are internal engineering assets

external_exposure.rationale: ArgoCD UI and pipeline infrastructure are accessible to studio engineers only via VPN

access_model.value: Okta SAML SSO with RBAC in ArgoCD; GitHub org membership governs repository access; least-privilege GCP Workload Identity for cluster operations
access_model.rationale: SOC 2 requires access control on deployment infrastructure and named human approval gates for production

feature_scope.value: GitHub Actions workflow templates for all service types (game backend, matchmaking, analytics); standardized Helm chart library; ArgoCD ApplicationSets for environment promotion; Argo Rollouts canary strategy with Prometheus-driven auto-rollback; deployment audit log; Slack notifications for rollout status
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope approved by engineering leadership for Q2 rollout

mvp_scope.value: GitHub Actions build pipelines for 5 pilot game services, base Helm chart library, ArgoCD self-healing sync, and basic Slack deployment notifications; canary rollouts deferred to Phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: Pilot with non-critical services to validate GitOps workflow before migrating live matchmaking services

security_baseline.value: OIDC-based GCP Workload Identity (no long-lived SA keys in CI), encrypted Kubernetes secrets via GCP KMS/Sealed Secrets, GitHub branch protection with required PR reviews, ArgoCD RBAC limiting prod sync to release managers, Cosign image signing
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 Type II and platform security hardening requirements

frontend_stack.value: None; infrastructure project with no custom browser UI beyond ArgoCD's built-in dashboard
frontend_stack.rationale: This is a platform engineering project; no custom frontend is built

backend_stack.value: Helm 3 chart library (Go templates), ArgoCD with ApplicationSets, Argo Rollouts controller, GitHub Actions reusable workflow library
backend_stack.rationale: Selected toolchain for GitOps pattern on GKE

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for ArgoCD state store (managed CloudSQL), GCS buckets for Helm chart artifact caching and build logs
data_platform.rationale: ArgoCD requires a relational store; GCS for artifact persistence across ephemeral runners

hosting_target.value: GKE Standard cluster (dedicated platform-ops namespace), ArgoCD and Argo Rollouts deployed as in-cluster controllers
hosting_target.rationale: GKE already runs game services; collocating ArgoCD reduces latency and operational surface

privacy_retention_policy.value: Deployment event logs retained 1 year for SOC 2 audit trail; GitHub Actions run logs retained 90 days; build artifact cache pruned after 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 Type II change management controls require 1-year audit trail

future_scope.value: Self-service environment provisioning for feature branches, cross-region canary rollouts, automated dependency vulnerability scanning integrated into pipeline gate
future_scope.rationale: Post-migration roadmap items once GitOps baseline is stable

constraints.value: Must maintain zero-downtime for all live-service games during migration; Jenkins must remain operational as fallback until all 40+ services are migrated; GKE cluster version locked at 1.29
constraints.rationale: Live game uptime SLA is 99.9%; Jenkins parallel operation reduces migration risk

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards for Argo Rollouts canary weight progression and error rate, Prometheus alerting on pipeline failure rate and sync drift, DORA metrics tracked in Grafana
observability_baseline.rationale: DORA metrics validate that the migration improves engineering velocity

execution_preference.value: Phased migration over 12 weeks with bi-weekly pilot service onboarding, internal enablement sessions, and per-service sign-off before Jenkins cutover
execution_preference.rationale: Incremental approach minimizes blast radius; engineering teams need onboarding time

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II: change management controls require approved PRs for all deployment changes, named approver audit trail, and 1-year retention of deployment events
compliance_context.rationale: Studio SOC 2 certification covers deployment pipeline as an in-scope control

project.goal: Replace Jenkins CI/CD with GitOps pipeline for 40+ game services
project.target_users: Game backend engineers and release managers
project.timeline: 12-week phased migration, Q2

backend.runtime: Helm 3 + ArgoCD + Argo Rollouts on GKE
backend.api: ArgoCD REST API for programmatic sync triggers

security.auth: Okta SAML SSO for ArgoCD; Workload Identity for GCP operations
security.secrets: Sealed Secrets encrypted with KMS, no SA key files
security.compliance: SOC 2 Type II change management controls

data.cache: GCS bucket for Helm artifact cache

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with reusable workflow library
devops.environments: dev, staging, production per game service

open_questions.canary_metrics: Which Prometheus metrics trigger auto-rollback per service type?

reasoner_reviews.product: The migration scope is well-defined with a clear pilot-first approach. Restricting MVP to 5 non-critical services before touching live matchmaking reduces risk. The 12-week timeline is realistic given 40+ services if onboarding proceeds in parallel cohorts of 5-8 services.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ArgoCD ApplicationSets with a Git generator pattern is the right choice for managing 40+ game services from a single control plane. Recommend structuring the Helm chart library as a chart-of-charts pattern with a common base chart to enforce naming conventions. Argo Rollouts with Prometheus analysis templates should gate canary promotion per service SLO, not a one-size-fits-all threshold.
reasoner_reviews.security: OIDC-based Workload Identity eliminates the SA key sprawl that existed under Jenkins. Sealed Secrets with KMS provides at-rest encryption for all deployment credentials. Production sync must be gated to release manager RBAC role in ArgoCD to satisfy SOC 2 change approval requirements. Cosign image signing closes the supply chain gap.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GKE 1.29 lock limits access to some newer GatewayAPI features but is not blocking for ArgoCD. Jenkins parallel operation is operationally necessary but must be enforced as read-only fallback to avoid drift. Phased 12-week timeline aligns with SOC 2 evidence collection for the next audit cycle.

backend.service_design: Helm chart library as a monorepo with a game-service-base chart and per-service value overrides. ApplicationSet Git generator syncs from a dedicated gitops-manifests repo. ArgoCD App-of-Apps pattern manages cross-environment promotion.
backend.api_patterns: ArgoCD REST API used by GitHub Actions to trigger sync and poll health status after image tag update commits. Webhook from ArgoCD to Slack for rollout state transitions.
backend.background_jobs: Argo Rollouts controller runs canary weight stepping on configurable interval. CronJob prunes stale Helm artifact cache entries from GCS weekly.
backend.backend_risks: ApplicationSet template misconfiguration could propagate to all 40+ services simultaneously. Mitigation: validate ApplicationSet templates in a schema linting CI step before merge to gitops-manifests.

security.auth_design: Okta SAML SSO federated to ArgoCD via Dex. GitHub org membership gates repository write access. GCP Workload Identity binds GitHub Actions runners to per-repo GCP service accounts with minimal IAM permissions.
security.secrets_management: Sealed Secrets controller decrypts secrets at cluster admission using KMS-wrapped key. No plaintext secrets in any Git repository. Secret rotation triggers new SealedSecret commit and ArgoCD sync.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: ArgoCD emits structured deployment events (who synced, what revision, what changed) to Cloud Logging sink with 1-year retention. GitHub Actions audit log forwarded via organization webhook to the same sink.
security.security_risks: Compromised GitHub Actions runner could push malicious image tags to gitops-manifests repo. Mitigation: branch protection with required Cosign signature verification in pipeline before any manifest update commit.

data.entities: helm_charts (name, version, values_hash, last_updated), argo_applications (name, namespace, sync_status, health_status, revision), deployment_events (id, app_name, revision, triggered_by, status, timestamp), canary_weights (app_name, current_weight, target_weight, analysis_run_id, updated_at), rollout_strategies (app_name, strategy_type, step_intervals, success_threshold, abort_threshold)
data.storage_design: CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16 for ArgoCD state. GCS for Helm chart cache, build logs, and Cosign attestations. Deployment_events written to Cloud Logging as authoritative audit trail.
data.data_risks: ArgoCD PostgreSQL instance downtime prevents new syncs but does not affect already-deployed workloads. Mitigation: CloudSQL HA with automated failover and daily backups with 7-day retention.

devops.deployment_topology: ArgoCD server and repo-server run in platform-ops namespace on GKE 1.29. Argo Rollouts controller installed cluster-wide. GitHub Actions runners use GCP-managed ephemeral runner pools via actions-runner-controller.
devops.ci_cd_design: Reusable GitHub Actions workflow: checkout, build Docker image, sign with Cosign, push to Artifact Registry, update Helm values file with new image tag, commit to gitops-manifests repo, poll ArgoCD sync status. Parameterized for service name and environment target.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes ArgoCD and Argo Rollouts metrics endpoints. Grafana dashboards track sync lag, rollout success rate, canary abort frequency, and DORA metrics (deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, change failure rate).
devops.rollback_strategy: Argo Rollouts auto-aborts canary if Prometheus error rate analysis template exceeds abort threshold. Manual rollback via ArgoCD UI resets target revision to previous Git SHA.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a GitOps platform migration for a live-service game studio. Key decision: ArgoCD ApplicationSets with a Git generator allows managing 40+ game services from a single control plane without per-service Argo Application manifests. Argo Rollouts with per-service Prometheus analysis templates is preferable to global thresholds because game backend services have heterogeneous SLOs. SOC 2 compliance drives OIDC-based auth and structured deployment audit logging from day one.
target_output.title: GitOps CI/CD Platform for Game Studio Services
target_output.executive_summary: A platform engineering project migrating a live-service game studio from Jenkins to a GitOps workflow using GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, and Argo Rollouts across GKE. Delivers standardized Helm chart library, ApplicationSet-driven environment promotion for 40+ game services, Prometheus-gated canary rollouts, and SOC 2-compliant deployment audit trail with zero-downtime migration from Jenkins over 12 weeks.
target_output.architecture_overview: GitOps control plane on GKE with ArgoCD as the reconciliation engine watching a dedicated gitops-manifests repository. GitHub Actions handles build, test, Cosign image signing, and automated Helm values commits; ArgoCD self-heals the cluster to the committed state. Argo Rollouts manages canary traffic splitting per game service using Prometheus analysis templates evaluating service-specific error rate and p99 latency at each weight step. SOC 2 audit trail flows from ArgoCD structured events through Cloud Logging with 1-year retention. Jenkins remains live in read-only fallback mode during the 12-week phased service onboarding to preserve live game uptime SLA.
target_output.technology_stack: GitOps: ArgoCD 2.10 with ApplicationSets plugin, Argo Rollouts 1.7 controller, Dex for OIDC federation to Okta. Build: GitHub Actions reusable workflow library, actions-runner-controller ephemeral runners on GKE. Container: Docker, GCP Artifact Registry, Cosign for image signing. Helm: version 3 with game-service-base chart library. Infrastructure: GKE Standard 1.29, CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16 for ArgoCD state, GCS for artifact cache. Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana with DORA dashboards. Security: Workload Identity, Sealed Secrets, KMS.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Reusable CI Workflow: parameterized GitHub Actions pipeline for build, Cosign sign, push to Artifact Registry, Helm values commit, and ArgoCD sync polling for all 40+ game services. Helm Chart Library: game-service-base chart with service, deployment, hpa, pdb, and serviceaccount templates extended by per-service value overrides. ApplicationSet Management: Git generator-driven ApplicationSets that automatically create ArgoCD Applications for each service directory in gitops-manifests. Canary Rollout Engine: per-service Argo Rollout resources with Prometheus analysis templates gating weight progression. Deployment Audit Log: structured Cloud Logging sink capturing who triggered each sync, target Git SHA, and outcome.
target_output.system_components: GitHub Actions runner pool (actions-runner-controller on GKE) for ephemeral build execution. GCP Artifact Registry for container image and Helm chart storage. ArgoCD server and repo-server in platform-ops namespace for GitOps reconciliation. Argo Rollouts controller for canary traffic management across game service namespaces. Dex identity proxy for Okta SAML federation. Sealed Secrets controller for at-rest secret encryption. CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16 as ArgoCD state store. Cloud Logging sink for 1-year deployment audit trail. Grafana and Prometheus stack for DORA metrics and rollout health dashboards.
target_output.workflows: GitOps Deploy: engineer merges PR to game service repo; GitHub Actions builds Docker image, runs Cosign sign, pushes to Artifact Registry, patches image tag in gitops-manifests Helm values file via automated commit, ArgoCD detects diff and self-heals the cluster to new state; Actions workflow polls ArgoCD application health API until healthy or timeout, then posts outcome to #deploys Slack channel. Canary Rollout: Argo Rollouts steps canary from 5% to 20% to 50% to 100% traffic with configured pause intervals; a Prometheus AnalysisTemplate queries per-service error rate and p99 latency over 5-minute windows; if thresholds are breached, Rollouts automatically aborts and reverts to stable revision; release manager can manually promote from 50% to 100% for high-risk services requiring explicit approval.
target_output.data_model: helm_charts (name VARCHAR PK, chart_version, app_version, values_schema_hash, published_at). argo_applications (name VARCHAR PK, namespace, project, sync_status ENUM synced/out_of_sync/unknown, health_status ENUM healthy/degraded/progressing, current_revision, target_revision, last_synced_at). deployment_events (id UUID PK, app_name FK, revision_sha, triggered_by_user, trigger_source ENUM github_actions/manual_sync/auto_heal, status ENUM success/failed/aborted, duration_seconds, logged_at). canary_weights (app_name VARCHAR PK, rollout_name, current_weight INT, stable_revision, canary_revision, analysis_run_id, updated_at). rollout_strategies (app_name VARCHAR PK, step_weights INT[], pause_duration_seconds INT, error_rate_abort_threshold FLOAT, p99_latency_abort_ms INT).
target_output.api_design: ArgoCD REST API (programmatic usage): GET /api/v1/applications/{name} — poll sync and health status after manifest commit. POST /api/v1/applications/{name}/sync — trigger manual sync with revision override. GET /api/v1/applications/{name}/resource-tree — enumerate deployed resources for rollback verification. POST /api/v1/applications/{name}/rollback — revert to previous revision by deploymentID. GitHub Actions reusable workflow inputs: service_name (string), environment (dev/staging/prod), image_tag (string), require_manual_approval (bool for prod). Slack webhook: POST deployment start, canary progress, success/failure events to #deploys channel.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 Type II controls: all production syncs require RBAC role release-manager in ArgoCD enforced via Dex and Okta policy groups; every sync event logged to Cloud Logging with triggered_by user identity and target revision SHA for 1-year retention. Supply chain security: Cosign signs every container image at build time; GitHub Actions pipeline rejects any unsigned image before manifest commit. Secrets management: Sealed Secrets controller encrypts Kubernetes secrets using KMS-wrapped key; no plaintext secrets in any Git repository. Network access: ArgoCD UI exposed only on internal GKE Ingress with VPN-only network policy. IAM: Workload Identity binds GitHub Actions runners to repo-scoped GCP service accounts; no long-lived SA key files. Branch protection: all gitops-manifests changes require at least one approved PR; direct push to main branch blocked.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ArgoCD and Argo Rollouts deployed via Helm to platform-ops namespace with resource requests and limits preventing noisy-neighbor impact on game workloads. CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16 in HA mode with automated backups every 6 hours and 7-day retention. Platform-ops namespace uses a dedicated GKE node pool with taints to isolate control plane from game service pods. Jenkins remains active as fallback during 12-week migration; a service migration tracker dashboard shows per-service status across pending/piloting/migrated/cutover states. CI runners are ephemeral — actions-runner-controller provisions a new pod per job and tears it down after completion. Rollback: ArgoCD revision revert via API restores previous Git SHA within 60 seconds. Disaster recovery: if ArgoCD is unavailable, deployed game services continue running unaffected; ArgoCD restored from CloudSQL backup within 30 minutes.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes ArgoCD metrics including argocd_app_sync_total, argocd_app_reconcile_duration_seconds, and argocd_app_health_status by application. Argo Rollouts emits rollout_phase labels (stable/canary/aborting) and analysis_run_status metrics. Grafana DORA dashboard tracks deployment frequency per game service, lead time from PR merge to production sync, mean time to restore derived from rollback event timestamps, and change failure rate as canary aborts divided by total rollouts. PagerDuty alerts on: sync failures persisting more than 5 minutes, any game service app health status degraded for more than 10 minutes, canary abort rate exceeding 20% in a 24-hour window.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Platform-ops node pool: 2x e2-standard-4 nodes (~$200/month). CloudSQL PostgreSQL db-n1-standard-2 HA (~$150/month). Artifact Registry storage (~$30/month for 40 services with 30-day image retention policy). GitHub Actions ephemeral runner pool billed per build-minute (~$100/month at current build frequency). Total platform cost approximately $480/month, replacing existing Jenkins infrastructure licensing and maintenance. DORA target: deployment frequency per service improves from weekly batch releases to daily on-demand.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Foundation (weeks 1-3): Deploy ArgoCD 2.10, Argo Rollouts 1.7, Dex, and Sealed Secrets to GKE platform-ops namespace. Build game-service-base Helm chart with service, deployment, hpa, and pdb templates. Create gitops-manifests repo structure with per-environment directories. Set up GitHub Actions reusable workflow template. Onboard 5 pilot services (non-live-traffic analytics workers). Phase 2 — Canary and Security (weeks 4-7): Implement Argo Rollouts canary strategy with per-service Prometheus analysis templates for pilot services. Integrate Cosign signing into pipeline. Configure Okta SAML via Dex. Enable SOC 2 audit logging sink to Cloud Logging. Onboard 15 additional game backend services. Phase 3 — Full Migration (weeks 8-12): Migrate remaining 20+ live services including matchmaking and session services with custom canary thresholds per service SLO. Decommission Jenkins pipelines per service after 2-week parallel observation window. Enable DORA Grafana dashboard and present velocity metrics to engineering leadership.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Helm chart changes require schema validation via helm lint and helm template in CI before merge. ApplicationSet template changes tested against dev cluster before staging promotion. GitOps manifests repo uses trunk-based development; production value changes require 1 engineer plus 1 release-manager PR approval. Cosign signature verification required in pipeline; unsigned images reject the deploy workflow. Chart library version increments follow semver; breaking value changes bump major version and require per-service migration PRs. DORA metrics reviewed in weekly platform engineering standup to track migration progress.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ApplicationSet misconfiguration propagates to all 40+ game services simultaneously. Mitigation: JSON schema linting on ApplicationSet templates in CI and dry-run sync in dev cluster before merging to gitops-manifests main. Risk: ArgoCD control plane downtime during Jenkins cutover period leaves no deployment path. Mitigation: CloudSQL HA, ArgoCD multi-replica deployment, Jenkins retained as emergency fallback. Tradeoff: per-service canary Prometheus analysis templates require upfront SLO definition work for each service but prevent false rollback aborts caused by applying uniform thresholds across services with different baseline error rates. Tradeoff: Sealed Secrets key rotation requires re-encrypting all repository secrets — planned as quarterly maintenance window with runbook.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose ArgoCD ApplicationSets with Git generator over Flux CD for richer multi-environment promotion UI and native Argo Rollouts integration. Confirmed CloudSQL over self-managed PostgreSQL for ArgoCD state to get managed HA without operator overhead. Selected Sealed Secrets over HashiCorp Vault to avoid additional infrastructure dependency; Vault revisited in Year 2 roadmap. Canary Prometheus thresholds defined per service in rollout_strategies to accommodate heterogeneous game service SLOs.

metadata.notes: GitOps migration for live-service game studio with SOC 2 audit trail and Argo canary rollouts.

project_goal.value: Scalable game tournament platform for indie and amateur esports organizers supporting bracket management, participant registration, match reporting, prize pool tracking, and live bracket embeds for streaming overlays.
project_goal.rationale: Core product purpose defined in discovery workshops with esports organizers

target_users.value: Tournament organizers (primary), registered participants, spectators, and streaming overlay consumers
target_users.rationale: Four distinct user roles with different access patterns identified

project_class.rationale: Requires server-side bracket logic, real-time updates, and a React frontend
