Orchestration Python script provisions an EC2 spot fleet (one c5.2xlarge server instance, one c5.2xlarge load generator) via boto3 using a pre-baked AMI with profiling agents installed. Server binary fetched from S3 and deployed to server instance. Load scenario script runs for configured duration. Profiling samples streamed to InfluxDB Cloud at 1-second resolution. Post-run, orchestration fetches run statistics, runs scipy Mann-Whitney U test against stored PostgreSQL baseline, and posts a GitHub Check Run with pass/fail and p99 regression delta. Lambda function subscribed to EC2 spot interruption EventBridge events handles cleanup and marks run as interrupted for retry.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Python 3.12, boto3, influxdb-client, scipy, PyGithub. Infrastructure: EC2 c5.2xlarge spot (server + load generator), pre-baked AMI with game profiling agent, Lambda for teardown. CI: GitHub Actions reusable workflow with OIDC federation to AWS IAM. Data: InfluxDB Cloud (profiling timeseries), PostgreSQL 16 RDS (baselines), S3 (binary artifacts and raw archives). Monitoring: CloudWatch for fleet events, Grafana over InfluxDB for trend dashboards.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Fleet Provisioner: boto3 RequestSpotFleet call with c5.2xlarge launch template, IMDSv2 enforced, IAM instance profile scoped to S3 read/write. Binary Deployer: fetches server binary from s3://game-server-binaries/{build_id}/ and verifies SHA256 checksum before deployment to server instance. Scenario Runner: SSH-driven script on load generator launches 200 concurrent bot clients against server instance for 15 minutes. Profiling Collector: agent on server instance writes frame_time_ms and memory_resident_mb to InfluxDB at 1-second granularity. Regression Comparator: scipy Mann-Whitney U test on p50 and p99 frame time distributions against baseline; memory high-watermark compared with configurable threshold. Status Reporter: PyGithub creates or updates GitHub Check Run on PR with pass/fail, regression delta percentage, and Grafana deep link.
target_output.system_components: GitHub Actions reusable workflow (orchestration entry point). EC2 spot fleet: 1 server instance + 1 load generator with pre-baked profiling AMI. Lambda teardown function (SAM-deployed, EventBridge trigger). S3 buckets: game-server-binaries (write-protected, CI-only), profiling-archives (run outputs). PostgreSQL 16 RDS (baselines, run metadata). InfluxDB Cloud (profiling timeseries). Grafana (trend dashboards over InfluxDB). CloudWatch (fleet health and spot interruption metrics).
target_output.workflows: Regression Run: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on successful server build; OIDC federation grants fleet provisioner IAM role; boto3 requests spot fleet; orchestration polls until both instances are running; deploys binary with checksum verification; executes 15-minute load scenario via SSH; collects profiling output; writes timeseries to InfluxDB; uploads raw CSV to S3; runs Mann-Whitney U comparison against PostgreSQL baseline; posts GitHub Check Run result with p99 regression delta. Spot Interruption Recovery: EC2 spot interruption notice event fires EventBridge rule; Lambda invokes ec2:TerminateInstances on fleet by tag; Lambda updates run_metadata status to interrupted; GitHub Actions workflow detects interrupted status via run_id polling and retries up to 2 times with fresh fleet.
target_output.data_model: build_artifacts (build_id VARCHAR PK, repo VARCHAR, branch VARCHAR, commit_sha CHAR(40), s3_binary_key VARCHAR, sha256_checksum CHAR(64), uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). benchmark_scenarios (scenario_id VARCHAR PK, name VARCHAR, bot_count INT, duration_seconds INT, script_s3_key VARCHAR). performance_baselines (baseline_id UUID PK, scenario_id FK, branch VARCHAR, commit_sha CHAR(40), p50_frame_ms FLOAT, p99_frame_ms FLOAT, memory_hwm_mb INT, sample_count INT, captured_at TIMESTAMPTZ). test_run_results (run_id UUID PK, build_id FK, scenario_id FK, status ENUM running/completed/interrupted/inconclusive, p50_frame_ms FLOAT, p99_frame_ms FLOAT, memory_hwm_mb INT, regression_detected BOOL, mannwhitney_p_value FLOAT, regression_delta_pct FLOAT, started_at, completed_at). InfluxDB measurement server_perf: tags build_id, scenario_id, instance_id; fields frame_time_ms, memory_resident_mb, cpu_percent at 1-second resolution.
target_output.api_design: GitHub Checks API (outbound calls from orchestration): POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/check-runs — create check run with status in_progress on fleet provision. PATCH /repos/{owner}/{repo}/check-runs/{id} — update to completed with conclusion pass/failure and output summary containing p99 regression delta and Grafana URL. InfluxDB write API: POST /api/v2/write — line protocol ingestion of server_perf measurements. InfluxDB query API: POST /api/v2/query — Flux query to compute p50/p99 from run measurement. PostgreSQL queries: SELECT baseline for scenario_id + branch; INSERT test_run_results on run completion. Lambda invoked by EventBridge rule (no HTTP API surface); input event contains instance_id and fleet_id from spot interruption notice.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IAM: GitHub Actions OIDC role permits ec2:RequestSpotFleet, ec2:DescribeInstances, ec2:TerminateInstances scoped to fleet tag; s3:GetObject on binaries bucket; s3:PutObject on profiling-archives bucket. EC2 instance roles: s3:GetObject on binaries bucket and s3:PutObject on profiling-archives only; no describe or IAM permissions. IMDSv2 enforced on all EC2 instances via launch template setting. Binary integrity: SHA256 checksum of server binary verified before deployment; mismatched checksum aborts run and fails PR check. Network: EC2 instances in private VPC subnet; security groups allow only SSH from GitHub Actions runner IP range and internal load test traffic between server and load generator instances.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: No persistent application deployment — all compute is ephemeral. PostgreSQL RDS db.t3.medium single-AZ (non-critical; baseline data recreatable from git history) in private subnet. Lambda teardown function deployed via SAM template from tooling repo on each tooling repo push. Reusable GitHub Actions workflow pinned to tag in tooling repo; game server repos reference specific version tag. Pre-baked AMI rebuilt monthly with latest profiling agents via Packer pipeline in tooling repo. Spot fleet launch template specifies multiple availability zones for best spot capacity. Run budget enforced by orchestration script timeout at 28 minutes with fleet termination if budget exceeded.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch metric filters on EC2 spot interruption notice events — alerts if interruption rate exceeds 2 per day (suggesting persistent capacity issues). GitHub Actions step logs capture each orchestration phase with timestamps for fleet provision time, deploy time, scenario duration, and comparison time. Grafana dashboard over InfluxDB shows frame time p50 and p99 trend per commit on main branch, memory high-watermark trend, and regression event markers. Run success rate and p99 regression detection rate tracked in PostgreSQL and queryable from Grafana via PostgreSQL data source.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Per run cost: 2x c5.2xlarge spot at $0.12/hr for 30 minutes = ~$0.12 plus InfluxDB write cost ~$0.01. Total per-run cost well under $5 constraint. At 50 builds per day: ~$6/day or ~$180/month. PostgreSQL RDS db.t3.medium: ~$30/month. InfluxDB Cloud usage tier: ~$50/month for retention volume. Total tooling cost ~$260/month. Multi-scenario scaling in Phase 2 multiplies per-run cost linearly by scenario count.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — MVP (8 weeks): Build Python orchestration script with single load scenario (200 bots, 15 minutes). Provision EC2 spot fleet logic with IMDSv2 and IAM instance roles. Deploy Lambda teardown function with EventBridge spot interruption trigger. Set up InfluxDB Cloud and PostgreSQL RDS. Implement Mann-Whitney U comparison against static baseline. Integrate GitHub Check Run API. Run on 3 game server repos in dry-run mode (non-blocking checks). Phase 2 — Multi-Scenario and Baseline Branching (4 weeks): Add parallel multi-scenario execution on separate spot fleets. Implement per-branch baseline versioning in PostgreSQL. Add Grafana dashboard with regression trend markers. Enable blocking PR checks. Phase 3 — Subsystem Profiling (ongoing): Add per-subsystem profiling (networking tick, physics step, AI evaluation) from server profiling agent instrumentation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Orchestration scripts live in tooling repo under /regression-detector, versioned with semver tags. Python scripts use type annotations and are tested with pytest; fleet provisioning logic mocked via moto in unit tests. Reusable GitHub Actions workflow must be pinned to tag by consumers, not floating branch reference. Baseline update script requires manual invocation with explicit commit_sha argument to prevent accidental baseline overwrite. All boto3 calls wrapped with retry logic using botocore retry config.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: spot interruption mid-profiling produces incomplete timeseries causing false regression detection. Mitigation: minimum sample_count threshold (900 samples for 15-minute run at 1s resolution); runs below threshold marked inconclusive rather than failed. Risk: pre-baked AMI becoming stale causes profiling agent version mismatch with analysis script. Mitigation: monthly AMI rebuild via Packer with automated compatibility test. Tradeoff: InfluxDB Cloud over self-hosted InfluxDB trades operational simplicity for vendor dependency and egress cost; acceptable given internal tooling budget. Tradeoff: SSH-based scenario execution over a more orchestrated approach is simpler to implement but limits parallelism within a single run — acceptable for MVP single-scenario scope.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Mann-Whitney U over t-test for frame time distribution comparison because frame times are non-normal and the U test handles outliers from GC pauses and OS scheduling more reliably. Confirmed InfluxDB Cloud over PostgreSQL timescaledb for profiling data because InfluxDB write throughput at 1-second resolution is better suited to the volume. Baseline branching deferred to Phase 2; MVP tracks baselines only on main branch to avoid branch proliferation complexity.

metadata.notes: Ephemeral EC2 spot fleet regression detector for game server CI with Mann-Whitney statistical comparison.

profile.domain: Live-Service Action RPG Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment System

project_goal.value: AI-driven dynamic difficulty adjustment system replacing hand-authored difficulty scripts in a live-service action RPG, using player behavioral telemetry and an RL-tuned model to continuously modulate enemy stats, spawn rates, and loot drop probabilities per player session
project_goal.rationale: Hand-authored scripts cannot adapt to player skill variance across millions of concurrent sessions; RL model enables per-player engagement optimization

target_users.value: Game servers consuming difficulty parameter overrides per player session; game analytics team consuming model performance dashboards
target_users.rationale: Internal service with no direct player-facing interface; consumed by game server tick loop

project_class.rationale: Core product is an RL inference and retraining pipeline with real-time adjustment API

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, analytics, realtime, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Needs realtime inference API, telemetry ingestion, RL model retraining batch jobs, analytics dashboards, and internal auth

complexity_level.rationale: Millions of concurrent player sessions each requiring per-session difficulty parameter inference within game server tick budget

risk_level.rationale: Bad model deployment could degrade player experience at scale; internal service so no external attack surface beyond game server auth

data_sensitivity.rationale: Telemetry is gameplay behavioral data with no PII; player_id references are internal identifiers not linked to personal data in this service

external_exposure.rationale: Service is consumed only by game servers on the same internal GCP network; no public endpoints

access_model.value: GCP Workload Identity service accounts; game servers authenticate via signed GCP identity tokens; no human user auth
access_model.rationale: Internal service-to-service auth using GCP-native identity

feature_scope.value: Real-time difficulty parameter inference per player session, telemetry event ingestion pipeline, RL model retraining weekly batch job, shadow deployment with A/B comparison for model promotion, difficulty profile CRUD for game designers, engagement KPI dashboards
feature_scope.rationale: Full system replacing hand-authored difficulty scripts end-to-end

mvp_scope.value: Single game zone real-time inference API, ClickHouse telemetry ingestion, basic engagement KPI computation, manual model deployment workflow
mvp_scope.rationale: Validate RL model effectiveness in one zone before rolling out to all game zones

security_baseline.value: GCP Workload Identity for service auth, VPC-native networking with no public endpoints, model artifact signing for provenance verification, ClickHouse access restricted to DDA service accounts
security_baseline.rationale: Internal service; primary security controls are network isolation and service identity

frontend_stack.value: None - internal AI service with no browser-facing UI; analytics dashboards via Grafana
frontend_stack.rationale: ai_system with internal_only exposure; no frontend specialist required

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for inference API and retraining orchestration
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem dominates RL and ML tooling; FastAPI for performant async inference endpoints

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for high-volume gameplay telemetry events; PostgreSQL 16 for model configs, difficulty_profiles, and adjustment_decisions audit trail
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse handles billions of telemetry rows with columnar compression; PostgreSQL for structured config and audit

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in us-central1; ClickHouse on dedicated node pool; weekly batch jobs as GKE CronJobs
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot eliminates node management overhead; dedicated ClickHouse nodes for storage I/O isolation

privacy_retention_policy.value: Telemetry events retained 6 months in ClickHouse hot storage, then dropped; model artifact versions retained indefinitely in GCS; adjustment_decisions audit retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No PII in telemetry; retention driven by model retraining window and audit requirements

future_scope.value: Multi-zone RL model with cross-zone difficulty coherence, player churn prediction integration, real-time A/B testing framework for difficulty parameters
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap after single-zone validation

constraints.value: Inference API must respond within one game server tick (33ms P99); weekly retraining job must complete within 4 hours; infrastructure budget under $8000/month
constraints.rationale: 33ms tick budget is non-negotiable for game loop integration; 4-hour retraining window fits weekly maintenance window

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for infrastructure metrics; custom dashboards for engagement KPIs (session completion rate, death rate per zone, loot satisfaction score); model drift alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Engineering standard stack; domain KPIs essential for validating model effectiveness

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with ML experiment tracking in MLflow; model promotions require game team sign-off
execution_preference.rationale: ML iteration cadence aligned with weekly retraining; game team approval gate prevents rogue model promotions

llm_integration.value: RL policy model (PPO-based) trained on player behavioral telemetry; inference via ONNX Runtime for low-latency serving; weekly retraining on GKE batch nodes
llm_integration.rationale: ONNX Runtime provides cross-framework inference with sub-5ms CPU latency for policy evaluation

project.goal: Replace hand-authored difficulty scripts with RL-based per-session dynamic difficulty adjustment
project.target_users: Game servers and analytics team
project.timeline: Single-zone MVP in 8 weeks, full rollout in Q3

backend.model_serving: ONNX Runtime for PPO policy inference

security.auth: GCP Workload Identity service accounts

data.configs: PostgreSQL 16 for model configs and audit

reasoner_reviews.product: The RL-based DDA system correctly targets per-session engagement optimization rather than global difficulty curves. The shadow deployment with A/B comparison is essential for validating model quality before affecting live players. KPI definitions (death rate, session completion, loot satisfaction) must be precisely specified to avoid proxy metric gaming.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ONNX Runtime for PPO policy inference is the right choice for the 33ms tick budget. The FastAPI async inference handler must pre-load the ONNX model into memory at startup to avoid cold-start latency. Redis session cache for difficulty_profiles must be populated at session start, not lazily on first inference request.
reasoner_reviews.security: GCP Workload Identity eliminates static service account key management risk. ClickHouse access should be scoped to SELECT for inference service and INSERT for telemetry ingestor; admin operations restricted to pipeline service account. Model artifact signing in GCS prevents unauthorized model substitution in the serving pipeline.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 33ms inference SLO requires a pre-loaded ONNX model with warm Redis session cache. The 4-hour weekly retraining window is tight for large telemetry datasets; ClickHouse columnar scans should be partitioned by session_date to limit retraining data scan to last 90 days. $8000/month budget is achievable with GKE Autopilot cost-based autoprovisioning.

backend.service_design: Two FastAPI services: DDAInferenceService (synchronous inference endpoint consuming session telemetry context and returning difficulty parameter overrides) and TelemetryIngestorService (async batch writer consuming game server event streams to ClickHouse). Shared DifficultyProfileRegistry loads active profiles from PostgreSQL into memory on startup.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/sessions/{session_id}/difficulty-params accepts {zone_id, player_telemetry_window, current_difficulty_profile} returns {enemy_stat_multipliers, spawn_rate_modifier, loot_drop_weights}; internal only via VPC service mesh
backend.background_jobs: Weekly GKE CronJob: PPO retraining job pulls 90-day telemetry from ClickHouse, runs training on GPU node pool, evaluates against held-out session cohort, exports ONNX artifact to GCS with version tag and SHA256 signature
backend.scaling_notes: DDAInferenceService HPA on CPU 50% target; pre-loaded ONNX model in Python process memory; Redis cache for per-session difficulty_profiles reduces PostgreSQL reads to once per session start
backend.failure_handling: Inference timeout fallback: if ONNX Runtime inference exceeds 25ms, return cached last difficulty_profile for session; circuit breaker on ClickHouse writes prevents telemetry backpressure from blocking inference path

security.auth_design: GCP Workload Identity Federation; each GKE pod annotated with service account; game server requests carry GCP identity token validated by FastAPI GCP auth middleware
security.authorization_model: Inference service account: read difficulty_profiles from PostgreSQL, read/write Redis session cache. Telemetry service account: INSERT to ClickHouse telemetry table only. Pipeline service account: full GCS access for model artifact read/write
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured logs on every inference call include session_id, zone_id, model_version, inference_latency_ms, difficulty_params returned; stored in Cloud Logging with 1-year retention for game team audits

data.entities: player_sessions (id, player_id, zone_id, started_at, ended_at, engagement_score), telemetry_events (session_id, event_type ENUM kill/death/loot_pickup/zone_exit/ability_use, timestamp, payload_json) in ClickHouse, difficulty_profiles (id, name, zone_id, base_params_json, active), model_versions (id, version_tag, gcs_artifact_path, sha256, promoted_at, status ENUM candidate/shadow/production/retired), adjustment_decisions (session_id, model_version_id, input_context_json, output_params_json, inference_latency_ms, decided_at) in PostgreSQL
data.storage_design: ClickHouse telemetry_events table partitioned by toYYYYMMDD(timestamp) with TTL 180 days; PostgreSQL for configs and audit trail; GCS bucket gcs://dda-models/{env}/{version_tag}/policy.onnx with object versioning
data.analytics_events: Session completion rate per zone per model_version, mean death count per session per difficulty tier, loot drop satisfaction proxy (loot_pickup events per session), inference latency P50/P99 per model version, retraining job duration and dataset row count

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot us-central1; DDAInferenceService Deployment with HPA; TelemetryIngestorService Deployment; weekly RetrainJob CronJob on GPU node pool; ClickHouse StatefulSet on dedicated highmem node pool; Redis via GCP Memorystore
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: pytest with coverage, mypy type check, Docker build, push to GCR, GitOps deploy via ArgoCD; model promotion pipeline separate from service deploy: requires game team approval in GitLab before production slot update
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping FastAPI /metrics endpoint; Grafana dashboards for inference latency P99, telemetry ingest lag, engagement KPI trends per zone; PagerDuty alert on inference P99 >33ms sustained 2 minutes
devops.rollback_strategy: ArgoCD automatic rollback on failed Kubernetes readiness probe; model rollback: update model_versions active slot to previous production version in PostgreSQL; Redis session caches invalidated on model slot change

ISS-001.title: ONNX model cold-start latency violates 33ms tick budget on pod restart
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify model pre-loading at pod startup. On Kubernetes pod restart, ONNX Runtime loads the model from GCS on first inference request, causing 2-8 second cold-start latency that violates the 33ms game server tick budget for initial session requests after restarts.

history.note: Identified during security and constraints review

ISS-002.title: Telemetry ingest path shares FastAPI process with inference, causing backpressure risk
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan co-located telemetry ingest and inference in the same FastAPI service. Under high telemetry write load, async ClickHouse batch writes compete with ONNX inference CPU cycles, risking inference latency breaches during peak player sessions.

focus_issues.title: ONNX model cold-start latency violates 33ms tick budget on pod restart
focus_issues.detail: Model loaded lazily from GCS on first inference request; pod restarts cause 2-8s cold-start violating game server tick SLO

focus_issues.title: Telemetry ingest path shares process with inference, causing backpressure risk
focus_issues.detail: Co-located telemetry writes and ONNX inference compete for CPU under peak load, risking latency breaches

latest_recommendations: Implement ONNX model pre-loading in FastAPI lifespan startup hook; add Kubernetes readiness probe gated on model load completion to prevent traffic before model is warm
Separate telemetry ingestor into its own Kubernetes Deployment so ClickHouse batch write load cannot compete with inference CPU cycles

previous_plan.title: Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment AI System - Initial Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: RL-based DDA system on GKE Autopilot using FastAPI for inference and telemetry ingest, ClickHouse for gameplay events, PostgreSQL for model configs, and ONNX Runtime for PPO policy serving.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Single FastAPI service handling both inference and telemetry ingestion. ONNX model loaded from GCS on first request. ClickHouse for telemetry, PostgreSQL for configs.
previous_plan.data_model: player_sessions, telemetry_events in ClickHouse, difficulty_profiles, model_versions, adjustment_decisions in PostgreSQL.
previous_plan.workflows: Inference: game server calls POST /v1/sessions/{id}/difficulty-params; ONNX evaluates policy; returns parameter overrides. Telemetry: game server posts events; FastAPI batches to ClickHouse.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revision round 2 for the RL-based DDA system. Two issues must be resolved: ISS-001 (ONNX model cold-start violating 33ms tick budget) and ISS-002 (telemetry ingest competing with inference CPU). Fix for ISS-001: pre-load ONNX model in FastAPI lifespan startup hook with Kubernetes readiness probe gated on model load completion. Fix for ISS-002: split telemetry ingestor into a separate Kubernetes Deployment with its own HPA and ClickHouse batch write budget, eliminating CPU contention on the inference service.

fix_report.action_taken: Added ONNX model pre-loading in FastAPI lifespan async context manager: on startup, downloads current production model artifact from GCS to local ephemeral storage, initializes onnxruntime.InferenceSession, stores in app state; Kubernetes readiness probe gates on /health/ready endpoint returning 200 only after model load completes; rolling deploy ensures at least 1 warm pod serves traffic during restarts
fix_report.expected_outcome: Pod restarts no longer serve inference requests until ONNX model is fully loaded; 33ms P99 SLO maintained across restarts; cold-start latency contained to pod initialization phase

fix_report.action_taken: Separated TelemetryIngestorService into its own Kubernetes Deployment with independent HPA scaling on telemetry queue depth; inference service no longer handles ClickHouse writes; game servers route telemetry events to /ingest endpoint on ingestor service; circuit breaker on ingestor prevents backpressure from ClickHouse slow writes affecting ingestor availability
fix_report.expected_outcome: Inference service CPU is fully dedicated to ONNX evaluation; peak telemetry spikes during large player events do not impact inference latency; each service scales independently based on its own load profile

target_output.title: Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment AI System - Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: An RL-powered dynamic difficulty adjustment system for a live-service action RPG, running on GKE Autopilot with two separated services: DDAInferenceService serving per-session enemy stat, spawn rate, and loot weight overrides via ONNX Runtime PPO policy inference, and TelemetryIngestorService batching gameplay events to ClickHouse. Revision 2 resolves ONNX cold-start latency via startup pre-loading and eliminates telemetry-inference CPU contention via service separation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-service GKE deployment: DDAInferenceService (Python 3.12 FastAPI) pre-loads the production ONNX PPO policy artifact from GCS at pod startup before accepting traffic, gated by a Kubernetes readiness probe on /health/ready. Inference requests from game servers arrive via VPC-internal service mesh, validated by GCP Workload Identity token middleware, evaluated against the pre-loaded model, and returned within 33ms P99. TelemetryIngestorService (separate Deployment) receives event streams from game servers and batches INSERT operations to ClickHouse without touching the inference code path. Weekly GKE CronJob retrains the PPO model on 90-day ClickHouse telemetry, exports a new ONNX artifact to GCS, deploys as shadow model for A/B comparison, and promotes after game team approval.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, onnxruntime-cpu for inference, httpx for GCS artifact download at startup. Data: ClickHouse partitioned by session_date for telemetry (TTL 180 days), PostgreSQL 16 for difficulty_profiles and model_versions, GCS for ONNX artifact storage, GCP Memorystore Redis for per-session difficulty_profile cache. ML: PPO training via stable-baselines3 on GPU CronJob nodes, ONNX export via torch.onnx.export. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, ArgoCD for GitOps, Prometheus + Grafana. CI: GitLab CI with pytest, mypy, and model promotion approval gate.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Real-Time Inference: game servers POST session context (zone_id, 60-event telemetry window, current difficulty tier) to DDAInferenceService; pre-loaded ONNX policy evaluates engagement features and returns enemy_stat_multipliers, spawn_rate_modifier, loot_drop_weights within tick budget. Telemetry Ingestion: TelemetryIngestorService receives kill/death/loot_pickup/zone_exit/ability_use events, batches 500 events or 1-second intervals, bulk-inserts to ClickHouse telemetry_events. Weekly Retraining: CronJob assembles 90-day session cohort from ClickHouse, trains PPO model on engagement reward signal, evaluates on held-out cohort, exports ONNX artifact with SHA256 signature. Shadow A/B: new model candidate served to 5% of sessions alongside production model; engagement KPI comparison over 72 hours before promotion decision.
target_output.system_components: DDAInferenceService Kubernetes Deployment (HPA CPU 50%, min 3 pods for warmth, readiness probe on model load), pre-loaded onnxruntime.InferenceSession in pod app state, GCP Memorystore Redis for difficulty_profile session cache, TelemetryIngestorService Kubernetes Deployment (HPA on pending event queue depth, independent from inference), ClickHouse StatefulSet on dedicated highmem node pool (telemetry_events partitioned by date), PostgreSQL 16 via Cloud SQL (difficulty_profiles, model_versions, adjustment_decisions audit), GCS bucket dda-models for ONNX artifact versions, PPO RetrainJob GKE CronJob on GPU node pool, ArgoCD for service and model slot GitOps, Prometheus + Grafana for inference latency and engagement KPI dashboards.
target_output.workflows: Session Inference: game server sends POST /v1/sessions/{session_id}/difficulty-params with GCP identity token; FastAPI middleware validates Workload Identity; handler fetches difficulty_profile from Redis cache (populated at session start); ONNX InferenceSession.run() evaluates PPO policy with 60-event window features; writes adjustment_decision to PostgreSQL async; returns parameter overrides within 33ms P99. Telemetry Ingestion: game server sends POST /v1/events batch to TelemetryIngestorService; service accumulates 500-event or 1-second batches; bulk INSERT to ClickHouse telemetry_events; if ClickHouse write latency >500ms, circuit breaker opens and events are dropped with counter increment (acceptable loss for telemetry). Weekly Model Retraining: CronJob at Sunday 02:00 UTC; queries ClickHouse for sessions from last 90 days with engagement scores; trains PPO model on GKE GPU node; exports policy.onnx to gcs://dda-models/candidate/{timestamp}/; writes model_versions row with status candidate; shadow deployment routes 5% of inference traffic to candidate model via weighted Kubernetes service; after 72h A/B comparison, game team approves promotion which updates model_versions production slot and triggers ArgoCD rollout refreshing ONNX model in inference pods.
target_output.data_model: player_sessions (id UUID, player_id VARCHAR, zone_id VARCHAR, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ, engagement_score NUMERIC) in PostgreSQL. telemetry_events (session_id UUID, event_type ENUM kill/death/loot_pickup/zone_exit/ability_use, timestamp DateTime, payload_json String) in ClickHouse partitioned by toYYYYMMDD(timestamp) TTL 180 days. difficulty_profiles (id UUID, name VARCHAR, zone_id VARCHAR, base_params_json JSONB, active BOOL) in PostgreSQL. model_versions (id UUID, version_tag VARCHAR, gcs_artifact_path VARCHAR, sha256 VARCHAR, promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM candidate/shadow/production/retired) in PostgreSQL. adjustment_decisions (session_id UUID, model_version_id UUID, input_context_json JSONB, output_params_json JSONB, inference_latency_ms INT, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ) in PostgreSQL.
target_output.api_design: Internal VPC-only endpoints. POST /v1/sessions/{session_id}/difficulty-params body {zone_id, player_telemetry_window: [{event_type, timestamp, payload}], current_difficulty_profile_id} returns {enemy_stat_multipliers: {hp: 1.2, damage: 0.9}, spawn_rate_modifier: 0.8, loot_drop_weights: {common: 0.5, rare: 0.4, epic: 0.1}}. GET /v1/sessions/{session_id}/difficulty-history returns last 10 adjustment_decisions for session. POST /v1/events body [{session_id, event_type, timestamp, payload_json}] on TelemetryIngestorService. GET /health/ready on DDAInferenceService returns 200 only after ONNX model load complete.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GCP Workload Identity Federation: each GKE pod uses annotated service account; game server requests carry GCP identity tokens validated by FastAPI middleware before handler invocation. ClickHouse access: inference service account SELECT only; ingestor service account INSERT only on telemetry_events table; pipeline service account full GCS access for model artifact read/write. ONNX artifact integrity: SHA256 stored in model_versions table; DDAInferenceService verifies downloaded artifact hash before loading into InferenceSession; mismatched hash aborts pod startup. VPC-native networking: all services on private GKE cluster with no external load balancer; game servers access via VPC peering.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: pytest with 85% coverage gate, mypy --strict, Docker build, push to GCR, ArgoCD GitOps deploy to staging then production. DDAInferenceService Deployment: min 3 replicas (ensures at least 1 warm pod during rolling restart), HPA CPU 50% target max 20 replicas, readiness probe /health/ready with initial delay 30s to allow model download from GCS. TelemetryIngestorService Deployment: HPA on pending_events_queue_depth custom metric, min 2 replicas. PPO RetrainJob: GKE CronJob schedule '0 2 * * 0' (Sunday 02:00 UTC), GPU node pool selector, 4-hour activeDeadlineSeconds, job metrics pushed to Prometheus. Model promotion: GitLab MR approval from game team updates model_versions production slot in PostgreSQL via promotion script; ArgoCD detects PostgreSQL state change and triggers inference pod rolling restart to load new ONNX artifact.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics from FastAPI /metrics: inference_latency_seconds histogram, inference_requests_total counter, model_version label on all metrics, onnx_session_ready gauge (0 until model loaded). Grafana dashboards: Inference Latency P99 per model_version, Telemetry Ingest Lag (ClickHouse write delay), Engagement KPIs per zone (session completion rate, mean death count, loot pickup rate) compared between model versions. PagerDuty alerts: inference P99 >33ms sustained 2 minutes, retraining CronJob failure, ONNX model hash verification failure. ClickHouse system.query_log monitored for slow telemetry scans during retraining.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $7200/month: GKE Autopilot inference pods 3-20 replicas ($1800/month baseline), TelemetryIngestorService 2-10 replicas ($600/month), ClickHouse dedicated highmem nodes ($2400/month), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL ($400/month), GCP Memorystore Redis ($300/month), GPU node pool for weekly retraining ($400/month amortized), GCS storage and egress ($300/month). Scaling: ONNX inference is stateless CPU-bound; HPA scales to 20 pods within 2 minutes for player population spikes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Single game zone inference API with ONNX PPO model pre-loading fix, TelemetryIngestorService split, ClickHouse telemetry pipeline, PostgreSQL configs and audit, basic engagement KPI Grafana dashboard, GitLab CI/CD, GCP Workload Identity auth. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Weekly retraining CronJob on GPU node pool, shadow A/B deployment with 5% traffic split, game team model promotion approval workflow in GitLab, multi-zone difficulty_profiles. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Full zone rollout replacing all hand-authored scripts, loot satisfaction KPI refinement, model drift Prometheus alerts, ClickHouse retention TTL automation.
target_output.development_guidelines: FastAPI lifespan context manager must complete ONNX model download and InferenceSession init before readiness probe returns 200. All inference handlers async; ONNX evaluation runs in thread pool executor to avoid blocking asyncio event loop. ClickHouse batch inserts use max_insert_block_size 500 rows or 1s flush interval. Model artifact version pinned in PostgreSQL; never load from GCS path without verifying SHA256. Retraining job output must export ONNX with opset 17 minimum for onnxruntime compatibility. mypy --strict enforced in CI; no Any type annotations in inference handlers.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: RL model overfits to majority player behavior and reduces difficulty for expert players. Mitigation: stratified training cohort sampling by engagement tier; monitor expert player session completion rate separately. Risk: ClickHouse slow query during retraining window causes 4-hour deadline miss. Mitigation: partition pruning on session_date limits scan; add query timeout and alerting on retraining job lag. Tradeoff: 5% shadow traffic for A/B means 5% of players may receive suboptimal difficulty during candidate evaluation; acceptable given 72-hour window and small sample size. Risk: ONNX model version mismatch if production slot updated while pods are mid-inference. Mitigation: model version pinned per inference pod until pod restart; no hot model swap.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed ONNX Runtime over TorchServe for inference given lower memory footprint and no Java runtime dependency. Selected ClickHouse over BigQuery for telemetry because GKE-local cluster avoids BigQuery query costs on frequent retraining scans. GPU node pool for weekly retraining chosen over Vertex AI Training to avoid egress costs on 90-day telemetry export. Redis session cache TTL set to match average session length (45 minutes) to avoid stale difficulty_profile cache hits.

metadata.notes: RL-based DDA system revision resolving ONNX cold-start SLO violation and telemetry-inference CPU contention via service separation.

project_goal.value: Research prototype for studying age verification and parental consent flows in online gaming platforms under COPPA and UK Age Appropriate Design Code requirements, testing friction-aware verification UX patterns against a simulated player onboarding pipeline.
project_goal.rationale: Academic and compliance research objective to evaluate verification UX friction tradeoffs

target_users.value: Compliance researchers and UX study participants running simulated player onboarding scenarios
target_users.rationale: Internal research team and study participants, not production end-users

project_class.rationale: Exploratory research system, not a production deployment; rapid iteration over compliance UX variants

capabilities.rationale: Requires a full onboarding flow UI, simulated auth, data capture, and research analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Multiple UX variants and compliance flow simulation, but no production-scale or external integrations

risk_level.rationale: Prototype processes simulated personal data with COPPA and AADC compliance requirements even in research context

data_sensitivity.rationale: Simulated player accounts may be operated by real research participants whose interaction data constitutes personal data

external_exposure.rationale: Research environment accessible only to authenticated study participants and researchers

access_model.value: Custom JWT for simulated player accounts; separate researcher admin JWT for scenario configuration and results access
access_model.rationale: Distinct access tiers for study participants versus researchers controlling the experiment

feature_scope.value: Configurable age verification UX flow variants, simulated parental consent email dispatch, synthetic player profile generation, friction event logging, consent audit trail, compliance scenario test runner, researcher analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for running comparative compliance UX studies

mvp_scope.value: Three age verification UX variants, parental consent simulation flow, friction event logging, researcher results dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable prototype for first research session

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, JWT auth, no real minors' data collected, all synthetic profiles must be clearly labeled, researcher access logs retained, consent audit trail append-only
security_baseline.rationale: Ethics board requires strict synthetic data boundary and audit trail

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript (Vite)
frontend_stack.rationale: Rapid prototype iteration with React; Vite for fast dev cycle

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Fastify
backend_stack.rationale: Simple REST API with good TypeScript support for prototype development pace

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for audit logs and compliance scenario records

hosting_target.value: DigitalOcean App Platform
hosting_target.rationale: Managed hosting for research prototype with minimal ops overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: All research session data deleted 90 days after study conclusion; no real minors' data collected; synthetic participant profiles purged on study close
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Ethics board mandated data minimization and retention limits for research data

future_scope.value: Additional verification method variants (government ID simulation, live face check simulation), cross-jurisdiction scenario comparison, publication-ready findings export
future_scope.rationale: Planned follow-on research phases

constraints.value: No real minors' data under any circumstances; all synthetic profiles explicitly tagged; study must run on < $300/month; consent audit log must be immutable
constraints.rationale: Ethics and budget constraints for research prototype

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for system health; all friction events and consent steps emitted as structured log events for research analysis
observability_baseline.rationale: Research findings depend on high-fidelity event capture -- observability doubles as the research data collection layer

execution_preference.value: Iterative weekly sprints; research team reviews prototype each week and adjusts variant configurations
execution_preference.rationale: Tight research feedback loop requires weekly iteration

compliance_context.value: COPPA (primary research focus for US child data protections); UK Age Appropriate Design Code (AADC) Article 25 GDPR-derived obligations; GDPR Article 8 (children's consent requirements); all test data must be fully synthetic with no real minors involved
compliance_context.rationale: Research specifically studies compliance flows; the prototype itself must comply with the regulations it studies

project.goal: Research prototype simulating COPPA and AADC age verification UX variants to measure friction and compliance coverage
project.target_users: Compliance researchers and adult study participants acting as simulated players
project.timeline: Initial research session in 6 weeks

frontend.state: useState and React Query for scenario state

security.auth: Custom JWT (no external provider for prototype simplicity)
security.transport: TLS 1.3 via DigitalOcean App Platform managed cert

constraints.synthetic_data: No real minors; all profiles tagged SYNTHETIC

reasoner_reviews.product: The prototype scope is correctly focused on the research question: does higher verification friction reduce completion rates, and how does this vary across COPPA versus AADC requirements? The three UX variant framework is well-designed for A/B comparison. The researcher dashboard must present per-variant completion rates and friction event drop-off analysis as first-class outputs.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The consent audit log immutability requirement is non-negotiable for an ethics board. A PostgreSQL rule preventing DELETE on consent_audit_logs is the right approach -- same pattern as the append-only audit log in production compliance systems. Round 1 plan left the parental consent simulation mechanism underspecified: the prototype needs a configurable mock email dispatch path (actual email or simulated in-app notification) to test different consent UX paths.
reasoner_reviews.security: Custom JWT is acceptable for a research prototype but the researcher role must be strictly separated from participant access at the API route level. The synthetic data label (is_synthetic=true) must be enforced at INSERT time via a database constraint or trigger, not application-layer trust. Researcher access to raw participant friction event data should require re-authentication to prevent passive data exposure.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $300/month is achievable on DigitalOcean App Platform: basic Fastify service ~$12/month, managed PostgreSQL smallest tier ~$15/month, React static site hosting free, total well within budget including storage.

backend.service_design: Fastify API with route groups: /scenarios (researcher: CRUD flow variant configs), /sessions (participant: create, get current), /onboarding (participant: submit verification steps), /consent (participant: consent flow, parental notification simulation), /events (internal: friction event logging), /analytics (researcher: variant comparison, completion funnels)
backend.api_patterns: REST at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0; JWT auth via Authorization: Bearer; role claim in JWT payload distinguishes participant from researcher; all research analytics endpoints require researcher role
backend.business_modules: Scenario engine: each flow_variant defines an ordered array of verification steps (dob_entry, id_prompt, parental_email, in_app_consent, none); participant session instantiates the active variant and tracks step completion. Friction logger: every step shown, step abandoned, back navigation, and error message displayed are logged as friction_events with timestamp delta. Consent simulation: when minor detected, mock parental consent can be delivered as in-app notification (instant simulation), simulated email (configurable delay 0-60 seconds), or held (test timeout path).
backend.failure_handling: Step navigation errors logged as friction_events with error_type; partial session completion retained for research analysis; no payment or external service failures possible in research context

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with Vite: participant onboarding flow views and researcher dashboard tab
frontend.pages_and_flows: Participant: scenario entry (assign random variant), DOB entry screen, ID verification prompt (simulated), parental consent flow (variant-dependent), onboarding complete screen. Researcher: variant configuration editor, live session monitor, completion funnel charts, friction heatmap per step, consent audit log viewer.
frontend.state_management: React Query for scenario config and session data; local useState for multi-step onboarding form state with step navigation history for back-button friction logging

security.auth_design: Custom JWT: participant tokens issued on session_create (no login required for study participants); researcher tokens issued via email+password login with bcrypt verification; token claims include role (participant|researcher) and study_id
security.authorization_model: Participant: POST /onboarding/steps, POST /consent/*, GET /sessions/:id (own only); Researcher: GET /analytics/*, GET /scenarios, POST /scenarios, GET /consent/audit-log (re-auth required); no cross-study data access
security.privacy_controls: All participant friction events tagged with session_id (no PII linkage unless researcher explicitly enables pseudonymous tracking in scenario config); synthetic flag enforced at DB level; consent audit log readable only by researcher role with re-authentication

data.entities: flow_variants (id UUID PK, study_id FK, name TEXT, steps_json JSONB, is_active BOOL, created_by FK, created_at). test_player_accounts (id UUID PK, session_id FK, assigned_variant_id FK, synthetic_age_bracket ENUM under13/13to17/18plus, is_synthetic BOOL NOT NULL DEFAULT true, created_at). age_verification_attempts (id UUID PK, session_id FK, step_name TEXT, outcome ENUM passed/failed/abandoned, duration_ms INT, error_displayed TEXT, attempt_number SMALLINT, created_at). parental_consent_records (id UUID PK, session_id FK, consent_method ENUM in_app/simulated_email/held, consent_status ENUM pending/confirmed/declined/timed_out, notification_sent_at, responded_at, simulation_delay_ms INT). friction_events (id UUID PK, session_id FK, variant_id FK, step_name TEXT, event_type ENUM step_shown/step_abandoned/back_navigated/error_shown/completed, timestamp_offset_ms BIGINT, created_at). consent_audit_logs (id BIGSERIAL PK, session_id FK, action TEXT NOT NULL, actor_role TEXT, details_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()) -- append-only via PostgreSQL rule. compliance_test_scenarios (id UUID PK, study_id FK, jurisdiction ENUM coppa/aadc/gdpr_art8, description TEXT, created_by FK).
data.storage_design: DigitalOcean managed PostgreSQL 16 (basic-1 tier, 1 GB RAM, 10 GB storage); no Redis required at research prototype scale
data.retention_and_deletion: All session data, friction events, and consent records purged 90 days after study_end_date via scheduled Fastify cron job; consent_audit_logs retained for ethics board review period (1 year); synthetic player accounts deleted on study close

ISS-001.title: Parental consent simulation mechanism underspecified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan stated parental consent would be simulated but did not define the three simulation paths (in-app instant, simulated email with delay, held/timeout) or how researchers configure which path a variant uses.

ISS-002.title: Consent audit log immutability not enforced at database level
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned append-only audit log but implemented it as application-layer convention only. Ethics board requires database-enforced immutability.

focus_issues.title: Parental consent simulation mechanism underspecified
focus_issues.detail: Three simulation paths need explicit definition and variant-level configurability in flow_variants schema.

focus_issues.title: Consent audit log immutability not enforced at database level
focus_issues.detail: Application-layer append-only convention is insufficient for ethics board audit trail requirement.

latest_recommendations: Define three distinct parental consent simulation modes in flow_variants steps_json schema
Add PostgreSQL rule or trigger preventing DELETE on consent_audit_logs table
Specify researcher re-authentication requirement for audit log access

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must close the two high-severity issues: define the three parental consent simulation paths with researcher-configurable variant parameters, and enforce consent audit log immutability via a PostgreSQL rule rather than application-layer convention. The architecture is otherwise correct for a research prototype on DigitalOcean. I will add consent_method as an explicit field in the flow variant config schema and specify the PostgreSQL rule in the data model and deployment sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined three consent simulation paths in flow_variants steps_json: in_app (instant in-app notification, no email), simulated_email (configurable delay 0-60 seconds before consent link activates), and held (no consent dispatched, tests timeout/block path). Parental_consent_records table updated with consent_method ENUM and simulation_delay_ms. Consent simulation workflow described explicitly in workflows section.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified PostgreSQL rule CREATE RULE no_delete_consent_audit AS ON DELETE TO consent_audit_logs DO INSTEAD NOTHING in the data model and deployment sections. Rule verified in integration tests by asserting that DELETE on consent_audit_logs has no effect and row count is unchanged. Researcher re-authentication step added to GET /consent/audit-log route description.

target_output.title: COPPA Age Verification Research Prototype Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype for comparative UX studies of COPPA, UK AADC, and GDPR Article 8 age verification and parental consent flows in online gaming onboarding. Built with React 18 Vite SPA and Node.js Fastify on DigitalOcean App Platform with managed PostgreSQL 16. Round 2 resolves the two high-severity issues from round 1: parental consent simulation now supports three configurable paths (in_app instant, simulated_email with delay, held/timeout), and the consent audit log is enforced as immutable at the database level via a PostgreSQL rule for ethics board compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 Vite SPA delivers the participant onboarding flow variants and the researcher analytics dashboard as a single application with role-gated navigation. Fastify REST API on DigitalOcean App Platform manages scenario configuration, session lifecycle, friction event logging, and consent simulation dispatch. PostgreSQL 16 (DigitalOcean managed) stores all research data including flow variants, participant sessions, age verification attempts, parental consent records, and an append-only consent audit log. No Redis is required at prototype scale. DigitalOcean App Platform provides managed TLS certificates and environment variable injection. GitHub Actions handles CI with lint, type-check, and test gates before deployment.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Recharts (funnel charts). Backend: Node.js 22, Fastify, TypeBox, bcryptjs (researcher password hashing), jsonwebtoken. Database: PostgreSQL 16 managed on DigitalOcean (basic-1, 1 GB RAM). Auth: custom HS256 JWT with participant and researcher roles. CI: GitHub Actions. Hosting: DigitalOcean App Platform. Observability: Prometheus via fastify-metrics, Grafana Cloud free tier, structured JSON logs for friction event research data.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Flow Variant Management: researchers define age verification step sequences (dob_entry, id_prompt, parental_email, in_app_consent, none) and consent simulation method per variant. Participant Session Simulation: adult study participants enter as simulated players with assigned age bracket (under13/13to17/18plus) and assigned variant; full onboarding UI presented. Age Verification Simulation: DOB entry with under-13 detection, optional simulated government ID prompt (image upload stub, not processed), onboarding gate logic per variant. Parental Consent Simulation: three modes -- in_app (immediate consent prompt shown to participant), simulated_email (countdown timer mimics email delay, then consent link activates), held (session blocked, tests timeout/abandonment). Friction Event Logging: every step shown, abandoned, or errored logged with millisecond timestamp offset for drop-off analysis. Researcher Analytics Dashboard: per-variant completion funnel, median time-on-step, consent confirmation rate by method, friction event heatmap. Consent Audit Log: immutable record of every consent action for ethics board review.
target_output.system_components: React SPA (DigitalOcean static site): participant onboarding flow and researcher dashboard. Fastify API (DigitalOcean App service, 1 instance): REST API for all session, scenario, friction, and consent logic. PostgreSQL 16 (DigitalOcean managed, basic-1): flow_variants, test_player_accounts, age_verification_attempts, parental_consent_records, friction_events, consent_audit_logs, compliance_test_scenarios tables. PostgreSQL rule on consent_audit_logs preventing DELETE. GitHub Actions: CI pipeline (lint, tsc, Vitest, DigitalOcean deploy). Grafana Cloud free tier: dashboards for system metrics and research event rates.
target_output.workflows: Participant Onboarding Simulation: researcher creates a study session link for a participant with a specified age_bracket (under13/13to17/18plus); participant clicks link and receives a JWT scoped to that session; the assigned flow_variant steps_json array is fetched; participant navigates each verification step in order; every step_shown and any step_abandoned or error_shown events are logged as friction_events with timestamp_offset_ms from session start; if synthetic age < 13 and variant requires parental consent, the consent simulation workflow is triggered. Parental Consent Simulation: three paths based on variant consent_method config. Path A (in_app): a consent confirmation dialog is immediately shown to the participant within the onboarding flow, simulating a parent reviewing on the same device -- participant confirms or declines; outcome logged to parental_consent_records with consent_method=in_app and consent_status=confirmed/declined; consent_audit_logs row inserted. Path B (simulated_email): onboarding flow shows 'consent email sent' screen with countdown timer equal to simulation_delay_ms; when timer expires, a 'parent confirmed' event fires automatically or participant can manually click 'parent declined' to test rejection; outcome logged with consent_method=simulated_email; consent_audit_logs row inserted. Path C (held): onboarding blocks at consent gate with 'awaiting parental approval' message; session times out after configurable hold_timeout_seconds with no automatic resolution -- tests abandonment and re-attempt UX; consent_audit_logs row inserted with consent_status=timed_out. Researcher Audit Log Review: researcher navigates to /analytics/consent-audit; Fastify issues a re-authentication challenge (current password prompt); on success, GET /consent/audit-log returns all consent_audit_logs rows for the study ordered by created_at; no DELETE or PATCH endpoint exists for this resource.
target_output.data_model: flow_variants (id UUID PK, study_id FK, name TEXT, steps_json JSONB NOT NULL, consent_method ENUM in_app/simulated_email/held DEFAULT in_app, simulation_delay_ms INT DEFAULT 0, hold_timeout_seconds INT DEFAULT 300, is_active BOOL, created_by FK, created_at). test_player_accounts (id UUID PK, session_id FK, assigned_variant_id FK, synthetic_age_bracket ENUM under13/13to17/18plus NOT NULL, is_synthetic BOOL NOT NULL DEFAULT true, CHECK (is_synthetic = true), created_at). age_verification_attempts (id UUID PK, session_id FK, step_name TEXT NOT NULL, outcome ENUM passed/failed/abandoned NOT NULL, duration_ms INT, error_displayed TEXT, attempt_number SMALLINT DEFAULT 1, created_at). parental_consent_records (id UUID PK, session_id FK, consent_method ENUM in_app/simulated_email/held NOT NULL, consent_status ENUM pending/confirmed/declined/timed_out NOT NULL, notification_sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, responded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, simulation_delay_ms INT). friction_events (id UUID PK, session_id FK, variant_id FK, step_name TEXT NOT NULL, event_type ENUM step_shown/step_abandoned/back_navigated/error_shown/completed NOT NULL, timestamp_offset_ms BIGINT NOT NULL, created_at). consent_audit_logs (id BIGSERIAL PK, session_id FK, action TEXT NOT NULL, actor_role TEXT NOT NULL, details_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()) -- immutable: CREATE RULE no_delete_consent_audit AS ON DELETE TO consent_audit_logs DO INSTEAD NOTHING; CREATE RULE no_update_consent_audit AS ON UPDATE TO consent_audit_logs DO INSTEAD NOTHING. compliance_test_scenarios (id UUID PK, study_id FK, jurisdiction ENUM coppa/aadc/gdpr_art8, description TEXT, created_by FK, created_at).
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1. Participant endpoints (participant JWT): POST /sessions (create session, returns assigned variant and JWT), GET /sessions/:id/variant (get step sequence), POST /sessions/:id/steps/:step_name/complete (mark step, log friction event), POST /sessions/:id/consent/action {action: confirm|decline|timeout} (log consent decision). Researcher endpoints (researcher JWT): POST /auth/login, GET /scenarios, POST /scenarios, POST /scenarios/:id/variants, PUT /scenarios/:id/variants/:variant_id, GET /scenarios/:id/analytics/funnel (per-variant completion rates), GET /scenarios/:id/analytics/friction (step-level drop-off), GET /consent/audit-log (requires re-auth challenge via POST /auth/reauth). No DELETE endpoints for sessions or consent records. Rate limiting: 50 req/min per session token on participant endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT: participant tokens issued on POST /sessions with role=participant and session_id claim (no PII, no name, no email); researcher tokens issued via email+bcrypt password login with role=researcher and study_id claims. Route-level role guards in Fastify via preHandler hooks. Researcher re-authentication: POST /auth/reauth accepts current researcher password and issues a short-lived (5-minute) elevated token required for consent audit log access. Synthetic data enforcement: test_player_accounts table has CHECK constraint (is_synthetic = true) and application always sets is_synthetic=true; a separate DB-level trigger logs any INSERT with is_synthetic=false to an anomaly_log and rejects the insert. GDPR/COPPA research compliance: no real children's data; ethics board approved synthetic profiles only; all session data purged 90 days after study_end_date via Fastify cron job (node-cron); consent_audit_logs retained 1 year for ethics board review. Audit log immutability: PostgreSQL rules block both DELETE and UPDATE on consent_audit_logs; verified in integration test suite.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: DigitalOcean App Platform: Fastify API deployed as a basic-xxs service ($12/month, 512MB RAM, 1 vCPU); React SPA deployed as static site (free); managed PostgreSQL basic-1 ($15/month). Total $27/month well within $300 budget. GitHub Actions CI: ESLint + tsc --noEmit, Vitest unit tests including consent audit log immutability test (INSERT row, attempt DELETE, assert row count unchanged), DigitalOcean App Platform deploy via doctl CLI. Migrations via node-postgres migration scripts run as DigitalOcean Job component before app deploy. PostgreSQL rules applied in an initial migration. Environment variables: JWT_SECRET, DB_URL, NODE_ENV injected via App Platform encrypted env vars. No container registry required -- App Platform builds from source.
target_output.observability: fastify-metrics exposes /metrics for Prometheus scraping by Grafana Cloud (free tier). Custom metrics: participant_sessions_created_total (counter), friction_events_total (counter, labels: step_name, event_type), consent_confirmations_total (counter, labels: consent_method), consent_timeouts_total (counter), variant_completions_total (counter, labels: variant_id). Structured JSON logs (pino) for all API requests with session_id and variant_id context fields -- logs exported to Grafana Cloud Loki free tier for research event query. Grafana dashboards: per-variant completion funnel, consent confirmation rate by method, friction event drop-off heatmap by step.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: $27/month for research prototype: Fastify App service $12, managed PostgreSQL basic-1 $15. Static SPA hosting free. Grafana Cloud free tier sufficient for 10 Prometheus metrics and 50GB Loki logs. Expected research data volume: < 5GB across all studies. No scaling required -- single Fastify instance handles research session load. If prototype evolves toward a demonstration platform, upgrade to PostgreSQL general-purpose tier and add App Platform auto-scaling.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Initial Prototype (6 weeks): three age verification UX variants (low friction, medium friction, high friction), in_app and simulated_email consent paths, friction event logging, researcher dashboard with completion funnel and consent rate charts, consent audit log with PostgreSQL immutability rules, ethics board data controls (synthetic constraint, 90-day purge cron). Phase 2 Extended Study (4 weeks): held/timeout consent path, compliance_test_scenarios tagging by jurisdiction (COPPA, AADC, GDPR Article 8), cross-variant statistical comparison export (CSV for research publication), additional ID verification stub variant (simulated document upload step). Phase 3 Publication Ready (3 weeks): findings export with anonymized aggregate results, variant configuration sharing for reproducibility, documentation of prototype architecture for methodology section.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeBox schemas for all Fastify route input and output -- no untyped req.body. Vitest integration tests must cover: (1) consent audit log INSERT + attempted DELETE asserts no rows deleted, (2) test_player_accounts INSERT with is_synthetic=false rejected by constraint, (3) participant JWT cannot access researcher analytics routes. Friction events must be logged within the Fastify route handler before response is sent -- not fire-and-forget. Ethics board review required before any change to consent_audit_logs schema or purge schedule. All study data changes documented in a lab notebook (markdown file in repo).
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: DigitalOcean App Platform Fastify instance cold-start latency during inactive periods could introduce artificial friction event timing artifacts. Mitigation: configure keep-alive pings or upgrade to always-on service tier for active research sessions. Risk: consent_audit_logs PostgreSQL rules could be dropped accidentally during a migration refactor. Mitigation: migration script CI check reads pg_rules and fails build if no_delete_consent_audit rule is absent. Tradeoff: custom JWT without an external identity provider reduces integration complexity appropriate for a prototype but means researcher account management is manual -- acceptable for a small research team.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Parental consent simulation: three explicit paths (in_app, simulated_email with configurable delay, held/timeout) defined in flow_variants schema and workflow -- resolved in round 2. Audit log immutability: PostgreSQL rules blocking DELETE and UPDATE on consent_audit_logs, verified in integration tests -- resolved in round 2. Ethics board synthetic data boundary: CHECK constraint on test_player_accounts (is_synthetic=true) plus trigger logging any violation attempt -- resolved. Study data purge: node-cron job in Fastify purges sessions 90 days after study_end_date with retention exception for consent_audit_logs (1 year) -- resolved.

metadata.notes: Round 2 COPPA research prototype revision resolving consent simulation path definition and database-enforced audit log immutability for ethics board compliance.

project.goal: LLM-powered AI game master with persistent world state and content safety for text adventure multiplayer
project.target_users: Authenticated text adventure players
project.timeline: MVP single-player 14 weeks; shared-world multiplayer 24 weeks

frontend.state: Svelte stores for narrative feed, WebSocket client for real-time command/response
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with dark terminal aesthetic

backend.framework: FastAPI async with WebSocket endpoint for session communication
backend.llm: OpenAI GPT-4o via openai Python SDK; Pinecone client for vector retrieval

security.auth: Firebase Auth JWT validated on WebSocket handshake
security.prompt_safety: Input sanitization stripping injection patterns before LLM context assembly
security.content_safety: Classifier on all LLM outputs before delivery to client

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 (Cloud SQL) for world state and player data
data.vector: Pinecone index for narrative memory retrieval

devops.hosting: Cloud Run for narrative API; GKE Autopilot for WebSocket servers

constraints.content_safety: Zero unmoderated AI content in shared streams

confirmed_decisions.world_state_conflict: optimistic locking via PostgreSQL row-level version counter

reasoner_reviews.product: The core product loop -- player types command, AI generates narrative response that advances the shared world -- is well-defined. Content safety is the existential risk; a single policy-violating AI output in a shared chat stream would be a platform liability event. The Pinecone vector memory is the key differentiator for narrative coherence across long sessions.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: By round 4 the architecture is well-converged. The remaining risk is world state concurrency: when multiple players act simultaneously in the same world region, the LLM context assembly must reflect the latest committed world state or two players will receive contradictory narrative responses. The optimistic locking strategy on world_state_snapshots needs explicit conflict resolution semantics -- retry or queue-serialize per region.
reasoner_reviews.security: Prompt injection defenses are the primary security concern for an AI game master accepting free-text player commands. Input sanitization must strip known injection patterns before context assembly. The content safety classifier should run as a synchronous gate before any LLM output is streamed to the client; never stream first and moderate after in a shared channel.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 3-second P99 latency target for GPT-4o narrative responses is achievable for most prompt sizes but will be challenged by long context windows (deep session histories). Context window management -- truncating older narrative_memories and relying on Pinecone semantic retrieval instead -- is the key latency control.
reasoner_reviews.critic: After four rounds the architecture is substantially solid. The only remaining weak point is the lack of explicit world region partitioning for concurrent multi-player scalability. The plan should define how world_regions map to isolated world state partitions that can be locked independently.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with two deployment targets: Cloud Run for the narrative_api (LLM inference, vector retrieval, content safety -- stateless, scales 0-50 instances); GKE Autopilot for session_server (WebSocket connections, world state dispatch -- stateful per region partition). World state conflict resolution: optimistic locking with version counter on world_state_snapshots; concurrent conflicting writes trigger re-query and LLM re-generation for the slower player.
backend.llm_integration_design: Context assembly pipeline: 1) retrieve top-5 narrative_memories from Pinecone by cosine similarity to player command embedding; 2) fetch current world_region state snapshot from PostgreSQL; 3) assemble prompt from system_prompt_template + world_context + retrieved_memories + recent_command_history (last 6 turns) + player_command; 4) stream GPT-4o completion. Context window budget: 8,000 tokens max with oldest memories dropped first if exceeded.
backend.failure_handling: OpenAI API timeout (>5s): return pre-authored fallback narrative for the player's current world region; do not commit world state change. Content safety block: return sanitized refusal response, log to content_moderation_logs, increment player_safety_strike counter.

frontend.app_structure: SvelteKit 2 app with SSR for initial session load. Client-side WebSocket connection to session_server for command submission and narrative streaming. Narrative feed rendered as scrollable terminal-style log.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (Firebase Auth), World Select (available world regions), Game Session (command input + streaming narrative feed + player character status panel), Moderator Review (content_moderation_logs with decision form)
frontend.state_management: Svelte writable store: narrativeFeed (array of narrative_entries), sessionState (connected/disconnected/reconnecting), characterStatus (HP, location, inventory summary)

security.auth_design: Firebase idToken validated on WebSocket upgrade handshake via firebase-admin verifyIdToken; decoded uid attached to session. Command input sanitized by stripping XML tags, system-role injection attempts, and known prompt delimiters before context assembly.
security.privacy_controls: Narrative session logs purged after 90 days via Cloud Scheduler job. GDPR erasure: DELETE endpoint cascades player_characters, active_sessions, narrative_memories (Pinecone namespace delete by player_id).
security.incident_response_notes: Content safety block events logged to content_moderation_logs; automated alert if block_rate exceeds 5% of sessions in any 1-hour window, indicating possible adversarial prompt injection campaign.

data.entities: player_characters (id UUID PK, player_id, character_name, current_region_id FK, hp, inventory_json, level, created_at), world_regions (id UUID PK, world_id FK, region_name, description_seed TEXT, state_json JSONB, state_version INTEGER, last_modified_at TIMESTAMPTZ), active_sessions (id UUID PK, player_character_id FK, session_server_id, started_at, last_command_at), narrative_memories (id UUID PK, session_id FK, player_character_id FK, content TEXT, embedding_id TEXT, created_at), rule_adjudications (id UUID PK, session_id FK, command_text, outcome_type, world_state_diff_json, llm_tokens_used INTEGER, latency_ms INTEGER, created_at), content_moderation_logs (id UUID PK, session_id FK, player_id, raw_llm_output TEXT, classifier_score REAL, action TEXT CHECK IN (allowed, blocked, flagged), reviewed_by UUID, created_at)
data.consistency_notes: world_regions.state_version incremented on every committed world state change; LLM context assembly reads state at version N; write uses UPDATE WHERE state_version = N (optimistic lock); on conflict (version mismatch), re-fetch and retry LLM generation once before queuing behind the winning write.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service: narrative_api (stateless, auto-scales 0-50 instances on request count). GKE Autopilot: session_server Deployment (2-8 pods, HPA on WebSocket connection count). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA. Pinecone Serverless index. Cloud Scheduler for 90-day session log purge and snapshot archival.
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run and GKE metrics. Langfuse self-hosted on GKE for LLM trace capture (prompt, response, token count, latency). Custom metrics: narrative_generation_latency_p99 (alert > 3s), content_safety_block_rate (alert > 5%), world_state_conflict_rate (alert > 10%).
devops.rollback_strategy: Cloud Run traffic splitting allows instant rollback to prior revision. GKE rolling update with readiness probe on /health; failed pods blocked from receiving traffic. Prompt template rollback via git revert and re-deploy.

ISS-001.title: World state conflict resolution undefined for concurrent multi-player region actions
ISS-001.detail: When two players in the same world region submit commands simultaneously, both LLM context assemblies may read the same state_version and both attempt to commit conflicting world state diffs. The optimistic locking approach was mentioned in round 3 but the retry and fallback behavior was not defined.

history.note: Optimistic locking added but retry semantics missing

ISS-002.title: Context window overflow not handled -- long sessions may exceed GPT-4o 128k token limit
ISS-002.detail: Sessions with hundreds of command/response turns accumulate large narrative_memories. The round 3 plan set a soft 8,000 token budget but did not define the eviction strategy for when the full assembled context exceeds this budget.

focus_issues.title: World state conflict resolution undefined for concurrent multi-player region actions
focus_issues.detail: Retry and fallback behavior after optimistic lock conflict must be defined explicitly to prevent inconsistent narrative delivery.

focus_issues.title: Context window overflow not handled for long sessions
focus_issues.detail: Context eviction strategy missing; without it long-session players will hit token budget failures.

latest_recommendations: Define explicit optimistic lock retry: on state_version conflict, re-fetch world region state and retry LLM generation once; if second conflict occurs, queue the player command behind the winning write with user notification

Implement context window budget enforcement: count tokens in assembled context before LLM call; if over 7,500 tokens, drop oldest narrative_memories until under budget, always preserving the 3 most recent Pinecone retrievals and the last 4 command turns
