previous_plan.title: AI Game Master -- Round 3 Revised Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: LLM-powered AI game master for text-based multiplayer adventure using GPT-4o, Pinecone vector memory, and PostgreSQL world state on GCP Cloud Run and GKE Autopilot. Content safety classifier gates all LLM outputs. World state uses optimistic locking with single retry on conflict.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: SvelteKit 2 frontend connects via WebSocket to GKE session servers; player commands routed to Cloud Run narrative_api for LLM inference. World state in PostgreSQL with optimistic locking by state_version. Pinecone stores narrative memories per player character.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT on WebSocket handshake. Input sanitization strips injection patterns. Content safety classifier synchronous gate before client delivery. GDPR erasure endpoint with Pinecone namespace delete.
previous_plan.workflows: Player command triggers context assembly (Pinecone retrieval + world state fetch + recent turns), GPT-4o narrative generation with content safety gate, world state diff committed with optimistic lock, narrative streamed to client.

best_plan.title: AI Game Master -- Round 3 Revised Architecture
best_plan.executive_summary: LLM-powered AI game master for text-based multiplayer adventure using GPT-4o, Pinecone vector memory, and PostgreSQL world state on GCP Cloud Run and GKE Autopilot.
best_plan.architecture_overview: SvelteKit 2 frontend connects via WebSocket to GKE session servers; player commands routed to Cloud Run narrative_api for LLM inference. World state in PostgreSQL with optimistic locking by state_version.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT on WebSocket handshake. Input sanitization. Content safety classifier gate. GDPR erasure endpoint.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 addresses the two remaining issues: ISS-001 (world state conflict resolution) needs explicit retry-then-queue semantics with a player notification message, and ISS-002 (context window overflow) needs a defined eviction order that preserves recent Pinecone retrievals and last 4 command turns while dropping oldest narrative_memories. Both are mechanical additions to the existing architecture with no structural changes needed.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined explicit world state conflict resolution: on first optimistic lock conflict (UPDATE WHERE state_version = N returns 0 rows), re-fetch world_region state at new version N+1 and retry LLM generation once; if second conflict occurs, the player command is queued to a per-region Redis list and the player receives the in-narrative message 'The world shifts and reorients around you -- the moment passes'; queued command is processed after the current write commits, within 2 seconds
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent multi-player region actions produce consistent world state without silent narrative contradictions; players experience at most a 2-second narrative delay on high-contention regions rather than receiving inconsistent AI responses

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented context window budget enforcement in the context_assembly pipeline: before each LLM call, token count the assembled context using tiktoken; if total exceeds 7,500 tokens, apply eviction in order: (1) drop oldest narrative_memories beyond the 3 most recent Pinecone retrievals, (2) drop oldest command_history turns beyond the last 4, (3) if still over budget, truncate world_context description to first 1,000 tokens; log eviction events to Langfuse as a custom trace attribute
fix_report.expected_outcome: Long-session players with hundreds of narrative turns remain within GPT-4o context budget without API errors; Langfuse eviction rate metric surfaces sessions where context pressure is high, guiding future context management improvements

target_output.title: AI Game Master -- Production-Ready Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: LLM-powered AI game master for a text-based multiplayer adventure game, using GPT-4o for narrative generation, Pinecone for semantic memory retrieval, and PostgreSQL for world state persistence on GCP Cloud Run and GKE Autopilot. This round resolves the final two architectural issues: world state conflict resolution under concurrent player actions now uses an explicit retry-then-queue strategy with player notification, and context window overflow is handled by a token-budget enforcement pipeline with defined eviction priorities. Content safety gates all LLM outputs synchronously. GDPR erasure includes Pinecone namespace deletion.
target_output.architecture_overview: SvelteKit 2 frontend establishes a persistent WebSocket connection to a GKE Autopilot session_server pod. Player text commands are forwarded to the Cloud Run narrative_api service which executes the context assembly pipeline: (1) embed the command and retrieve top-5 narrative_memories from Pinecone by cosine similarity; (2) fetch the current world_region state snapshot from PostgreSQL at the latest state_version; (3) assemble prompt from system_prompt_template, world_context, retrieved memories, and last 4 command turns; (4) enforce 7,500-token budget by evicting oldest narrative_memories then oldest command turns if exceeded; (5) stream GPT-4o completion through the content safety classifier gate before forwarding to the client. World state changes are committed via optimistic locking on world_region.state_version; a first conflict triggers LLM retry at the new version; a second conflict queues the command to a per-region Redis list and notifies the player. Narrative events are embedded and upserted to Pinecone for future retrieval. Session logs are purged after 90 days via Cloud Scheduler.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: SvelteKit 2, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, native WebSocket client. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, openai Python SDK (GPT-4o streaming), pinecone-client, tiktoken (context budget counting), Firebase Admin SDK, Redis client. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA, Pinecone Serverless. Cache: Redis 7 (Memorystore) for per-region command queues and session tokens. Hosting: Cloud Run (narrative_api), GKE Autopilot (session_server). Auth: Firebase Auth. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Google Cloud Monitoring, Langfuse (LLM traces). Content safety: fine-tuned classifier endpoint on Cloud Run.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Command Processing Pipeline: player text command validated for injection patterns, embedded via OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, Pinecone similarity search retrieves top-5 narrative_memories, world region state fetched, context assembled within 7,500-token budget, GPT-4o generates narrative, content safety classifier evaluates output, narrative streamed to player, world state diff committed with optimistic lock, narrative event upserted to Pinecone. Shared World State: world_regions with versioned state_json updated by narrative outcomes; conflict-serialized per region via Redis queue. Content Safety: classifier scores all LLM outputs; blocked outputs replaced with refusal narrative; moderation logs retained 2 years. Player Progression: character level, HP, inventory, and location tracked in player_characters and updated on narrative outcomes. Moderator Panel: content_moderation_logs reviewed with allow/escalate decisions.
target_output.system_components: SvelteKit 2 WebSocket client, GKE Autopilot session_server pods (WebSocket hub, command dispatch, world state coordination), Cloud Run narrative_api (context assembly, LLM inference, content safety gate), Cloud Run content_safety_classifier (fine-tuned binary classifier endpoint), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA (world state + player data + moderation logs), Pinecone Serverless (narrative memory vectors), Redis Memorystore (per-region command queues + session tokens), Firebase Auth (identity), Cloud Scheduler (90-day session log purge), Langfuse (LLM trace capture on GKE).
target_output.workflows: Player Command to Narrative: player types 'I search the ancient library for a map of the northern passage'; session_server WebSocket handler forwards command to narrative_api via internal gRPC; narrative_api input sanitizer strips XML and injection patterns; command embedded via text-embedding-3-small; Pinecone query returns 5 semantically relevant narrative_memories ('The librarian mentioned sealed vaults...', 'You previously found a partial chart...'); world_region state fetched at state_version=47; prompt assembled (system_prompt + world_context + 5 memories + 4 recent turns + command) at 6,200 tokens (within budget); GPT-4o streams 'You pull dusty scrolls from the western alcove and find a faded map fragment...' through content_safety_classifier (score 0.02, allowed); narrative delivered to player WebSocket; world_state_diff_json computed ({found_item: map_fragment_northern, region_note: library_searched}); UPDATE world_regions SET state_json=..., state_version=48 WHERE id=:region_id AND state_version=47; succeeds; narrative event embedded and upserted to Pinecone. Concurrent Conflict Handling: two players in the same region submit commands simultaneously; player A updates state_version 47->48 first; player B LLM generates at version 47 and attempts UPDATE WHERE state_version=47 -- zero rows affected; player B narrative_api re-fetches region at state_version=48 and retries LLM generation; on second attempt succeeds and commits 48->49; if a third player causes another conflict during B's retry, player B receives in-narrative 'The world shifts and reorients around you -- the moment passes' and their command is pushed to the region's Redis queue; queue consumer retries after 1.5 seconds at committed state_version.
target_output.data_model: player_characters (id UUID PK, player_id TEXT, character_name TEXT, current_region_id UUID FK, hp INTEGER, max_hp INTEGER, inventory_json JSONB, level INTEGER, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). world_regions (id UUID PK, world_id UUID, region_name TEXT, description_seed TEXT, state_json JSONB, state_version INTEGER DEFAULT 0, last_modified_at TIMESTAMPTZ). active_sessions (id UUID PK, player_character_id UUID FK, session_server_pod TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_command_at TIMESTAMPTZ). narrative_memories (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, player_character_id UUID FK, content TEXT, embedding_id TEXT, importance_score REAL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). rule_adjudications (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, command_text TEXT, outcome_type TEXT, world_state_diff_json JSONB, llm_tokens_used INTEGER, latency_ms INTEGER, context_eviction_triggered BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). content_moderation_logs (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, player_id TEXT, classifier_score REAL, action TEXT CHECK IN (allowed, blocked, flagged), reviewed_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: WebSocket events (GKE session_server): client -> server: {type: 'command', text: string, character_id: string}; server -> client: {type: 'narrative', text: string, world_delta: object}, {type: 'conflict_delay', message: string}, {type: 'error', code: string}. Internal gRPC (session_server -> narrative_api): GenerateNarrative(CommandRequest) -> NarrativeResponse. REST at /api/v1 (SvelteKit backend routes): POST /auth/session (Firebase token exchange), GET /characters/:id (character state), GET /world/regions/:id (region overview), GET /moderate/logs?from=&to= (moderator panel), DELETE /players/:id (GDPR erasure). Content safety endpoint: POST /classify (internal only, text -> {score, action}).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth idToken validated on WebSocket upgrade by session_server via firebase-admin; decoded uid propagated to all downstream narrative_api calls. Input sanitization: strip XML tags, '###', 'SYSTEM:', 'Ignore previous instructions' patterns, and null bytes before context assembly. Content safety classifier: synchronous gate on every LLM output before delivery; blocked outputs trigger in-narrative refusal and content_moderation_logs entry; player receives 3 strikes before account flag for moderator review. Prompt templates version-controlled in git; production prompt changes require PR review and Langfuse A/B evaluation in staging. GDPR: DELETE /players/:id cascades player_characters, active_sessions (soft-close), narrative_memories, rule_adjudications (anonymized) and calls Pinecone delete_namespace by player_id. Content moderation logs retained 2 years for platform liability audit. No real player PII included in LLM prompts -- only character names and in-game state.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run narrative_api: auto-scales 0-50 instances on request concurrency; min-instances=2 to avoid cold-start latency on first commands. GKE Autopilot session_server: HPA min 2 max 10 pods on WebSocket connection count metric. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA with automatic failover; connection pooling via PgBouncer sidecar on session_server pods. GitHub Actions CI: pytest (85% gate), Mypy type check, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, deploy Cloud Run via gcloud run deploy --traffic=0 then migrate traffic after smoke test; GKE deploy via kubectl rolling update with readiness probe on /health. Prompt template changes deployed separately via git tag triggering a prompt registry update job -- no full rebuild required.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request latency, error rate, instance count. GKE pod health and WebSocket connection count. Langfuse: every LLM call traced with prompt_template_version, token_count, latency_ms, content_safety_score, context_eviction_triggered. Custom dashboards: narrative_generation_p99_latency (alert > 3s), content_safety_block_rate per 1h window (alert > 5%), world_state_conflict_rate (alert > 10% of commands in any region), context_eviction_rate (informational, guides context management improvements). Langfuse evaluation: weekly human review of 50 random narrative samples for coherence and safety quality scoring.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1,800/month at 1,000 DAU: Cloud Run narrative_api ($400 on average 5 instances), GKE Autopilot session_server ($300 on 3 pods), Cloud SQL HA db.g1-small ($200), Pinecone Serverless ($150 at 10M vectors), Redis Memorystore Basic ($80), OpenAI GPT-4o tokens at ~1,500 tokens/command, ~50 commands/DAU/session = 75M tokens/month ($600 at $8/1M output). Context budget enforcement reduces token cost by approximately 20% for long-session players. Reserved Cloud Run minimum instances reduce cold-start cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks): single-player sessions with GPT-4o narrative, content safety classifier gate, Firebase Auth, PostgreSQL world state with optimistic locking (full retry-then-queue semantics), Pinecone narrative memory, 7,500-token context budget enforcement, Cloud Run + GKE single-region deployment, Langfuse observability. Phase 2 (10 weeks): shared-world multiplayer with concurrent region access, moderator review panel, GDPR erasure endpoint, world snapshot archival to Cloud Storage, per-region Redis command queues. Phase 3: GM personality customization, third-party game integration API, multi-world hosting.
target_output.development_guidelines: All FastAPI endpoints type-annotated with Pydantic v2 models; no raw dict returns. Context assembly pipeline unit-tested with token count assertions at eviction thresholds using tiktoken. Optimistic lock retry logic tested with concurrent pytest-asyncio tasks simulating simultaneous region writes. Prompt templates linted for injection-enabling patterns in CI (check for unescaped {player_input} without sanitization wrapper). LLM integration tests run against a mock OpenAI endpoint in CI; real GPT-4o calls only in staging smoke tests. Content safety classifier endpoint covered by integration tests with known-blocked and known-allowed sample pairs.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OpenAI API outage or rate limit during peak concurrent sessions. Mitigation: Cloud Run min-instances=2 with pre-authored regional fallback narrative templates for the 10 most common world regions; Langfuse alert on OpenAI error rate > 1%. Tradeoff: Pinecone Serverless over pgvector for narrative memories -- Pinecone chosen for sub-50ms P99 retrieval at millions of vectors; pgvector would require a larger Cloud SQL instance class with dedicated HNSW index memory. Risk: adversarial prompt injection via player commands bypasses sanitization and manipulates world state. Mitigation: input sanitization removes known patterns; world state changes validated against a diff_schema before commit -- unexpected field keys in world_state_diff_json rejected; Langfuse monitors for anomalous world state diffs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: World state conflict resolution fully defined: retry once at new state_version then queue with player notification for second conflict. Context window eviction order: oldest narrative_memories first, then oldest command turns, then world_context truncation; eviction logged to Langfuse. Decided no player PII appears in LLM prompts -- character names only, never email or account identifiers.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision of LLM game master system; closes world state conflict resolution and context window budget issues; strong quality with detailed LLM pipeline and concurrency handling.

profile.domain: Procedural Open-World Quest Narrative Generation System

project_goal.value: Scalable AI-generated quest narrative system for a procedural open-world game using LLMs to dynamically write contextual quest dialogue, objective descriptions, and NPC flavor text personalized to player in-game history and faction standings
project_goal.rationale: Hand-authored static dialogue cannot scale to procedurally generated quest permutations across an open world with dynamic faction relationships

target_users.value: Game servers requesting narrative content per quest trigger; internal game writers using authoring UI to manage prompt templates and review flagged LLM outputs
target_users.rationale: Dual consumer: automated game server API calls and human writers reviewing content quality

project_class.rationale: Core product is an LLM inference pipeline with prompt templating, content moderation, and caching

capabilities.rationale: LLM inference, player context data, real-time streaming response to game servers, analytics for output quality monitoring

complexity_level.rationale: Thousands of concurrent quest triggers per second across open-world server regions; LLM inference latency must fit within NPC interaction loading screen

risk_level.rationale: LLM outputs risk policy violations in player-facing narrative; bad model responses could generate offensive NPC dialogue

data_sensitivity.rationale: Player history and faction data are in-game state identifiers; no real-world PII included in LLM context payloads

external_exposure.rationale: API consumed exclusively by internal game servers on VPC; no public endpoint

access_model.value: AWS Cognito internal service accounts for game servers; human authoring UI users authenticate via Cognito with SAML SSO to internal identity provider
access_model.rationale: Dual auth model: service accounts for automated API calls, SSO for writer authoring tool

feature_scope.value: Quest dialogue generation with faction-contextual personalization, NPC flavor text generation, content moderation classifier pipeline, prompt template management, flagged output review queue for writers, generated content cache, LLM output analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full narrative generation pipeline replacing static dialogue tables

mvp_scope.value: Quest dialogue generation for one game region, content moderation classifier, basic prompt template CRUD in authoring UI, Redis caching for repeated quest contexts
mvp_scope.rationale: Validate LLM narrative quality in one region before global rollout

security_baseline.value: VPC-internal endpoints only, Cognito service account tokens for game server calls, content moderation classifier before any LLM output reaches game servers, prompt injection detection on player-sourced context fields
security_baseline.rationale: LLM outputs are player-facing narrative; moderation is a hard safety requirement

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript for internal authoring UI only; no public-facing browser UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Writers need a UI for prompt template management and flagged output review

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for inference API and authoring backend
backend_stack.rationale: Python LLM ecosystem (LangChain, tiktoken, openai SDK) and FastAPI async streaming support

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for prompt templates, generated_dialogues audit, faction_standings snapshots, npc_personalities; Redis 7 for LLM output cache keyed by context hash
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for structured narrative config and audit; Redis for cache to reduce LLM API call costs on repeated quest contexts

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1; inference service and authoring API as separate ECS services
hosting_target.rationale: ECS Fargate for managed containers; us-east-1 co-located with primary game servers

privacy_retention_policy.value: Generated dialogues retained 30 days for review and analytics; prompt templates retained indefinitely; no player real-name data enters LLM prompts
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Short retention for generated content; players receive anonymized quest narrative only

future_scope.value: Multi-language narrative generation, player-specific narrative memory across sessions, voice-acted NPC dialogue generation
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap requiring stable single-language pipeline first

constraints.value: LLM generation latency under 3 seconds P99 for quest trigger response; content moderation must pass before game server receives output; LLM API cost under $5000/month
constraints.rationale: 3-second budget matches NPC interaction loading screen duration; cost cap requires aggressive caching

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for infrastructure metrics; custom metrics for LLM generation latency, cache hit rate, moderation flag rate per quest template
observability_baseline.rationale: CloudWatch standard; domain metrics essential for content quality and cost monitoring

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with LLM experiment tracking; prompt template changes require writer team review before merge
execution_preference.rationale: Prompt engineering iteration cadence; human review gate for player-facing content changes

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o for primary narrative generation; Anthropic Claude 3 Haiku as fallback for cost optimization; content moderation via OpenAI Moderation API plus custom fine-tuned classifier for game-specific policy violations
llm_integration.rationale: GPT-4o quality for primary narrative; Haiku for high-frequency low-complexity flavor text to reduce cost

project.goal: LLM-powered quest narrative generation personalized to player history and faction standings
project.target_users: Game servers and internal game writers
project.timeline: Single-region MVP in 10 weeks

frontend.purpose: Internal authoring UI for prompt template management and flagged output review

backend.llm: OpenAI GPT-4o primary, Claude 3 Haiku fallback

security.auth: Cognito service accounts for game servers, SAML SSO for writers
security.moderation: OpenAI Moderation API + custom classifier before output delivery

reasoner_reviews.product: The narrative generation scope is well-defined for the MVP. Distinguishing between quest dialogue (high quality, GPT-4o) and NPC flavor text (high frequency, Haiku) is the right cost optimization strategy. The content moderation requirement is non-negotiable given the player-facing nature of LLM outputs.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Redis caching by context hash is correct but the hash key design needs careful thought. Faction standings change frequently; a cache key including full faction_standings will have low hit rate. Consider versioned context keys where faction tier changes increment a cache version rather than generating entirely new keys for minor standing delta changes.
reasoner_reviews.security: Prompt injection is the primary attack surface: player character names, faction names, and player-sourced quest context fields must be sanitized before inclusion in LLM prompt templates. OpenAI Moderation API plus game-specific classifier provides layered moderation defense. Authoring UI access for writers needs role-based controls separating template editors from reviewers.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-second P99 latency is achievable with GPT-4o streaming and aggressive caching. Cache hit rate will determine LLM cost; estimate 40% cache hit rate reduces API spend to under $5000/month at target quest trigger volume.

backend.service_design: Two ECS services: NarrativeInferenceService (LLM prompt assembly, OpenAI/Claude API calls, moderation pipeline, Redis cache) and AuthoringAPIService (prompt template CRUD, flagged output review queue, writer analytics). Shared ContextAssembler builds structured prompt payloads from quest_templates, player_histories, faction_standings, and npc_personalities.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/quests/{quest_template_id}/narrative body {player_id, faction_standings, recent_player_history} returns streaming SSE narrative JSON; internal game server endpoint only
backend.background_jobs: Async post-generation: after delivering narrative to game server, background task runs OpenAI moderation check on delivered content; flags violations to review queue; updates generated_dialogues status in PostgreSQL
backend.llm_integration_design: ContextAssembler extracts relevant player history window (last 10 events), faction tier buckets (ally/neutral/enemy) from faction_standings, npc_personality traits from PostgreSQL; assembles structured prompt from Jinja2 template; submits to GPT-4o with max_tokens 400; falls back to Claude 3 Haiku if GPT-4o latency exceeds 2.5s or returns rate limit error
backend.failure_handling: LLM API timeout falls back to pre-authored default dialogue for quest template; moderation API timeout defaults to block (fail-closed); Redis cache miss on first quest trigger re-populates cache after generation

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with service account credentials for game servers; SAML 2.0 federation for writer SSO to internal Okta
security.abuse_prevention: Prompt injection defense: player-sourced fields (character_name, faction_name) sanitized through allowlist regex before prompt template insertion; LLM system prompt includes injection resistance instructions
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All generated_dialogues stored in PostgreSQL with quest_template_id, player_id_hash, model_used, latency_ms, moderation_passed, delivered_at; moderation flag events logged with violation category

data.entities: quest_templates (id, name, region, prompt_template_text, required_context_fields, version), player_histories (player_id, event_type, event_data_json, occurred_at), faction_standings (player_id, faction_id, standing_score, tier ENUM ally/neutral/enemy, updated_at), generated_dialogues (id, quest_template_id, player_id_hash, model_used, output_text, cache_key, moderation_passed, delivered_at), npc_personalities (npc_id, name, faction_id, personality_traits_json, speech_style), world_events (id, region_id, event_type, narrative_impact_json, active)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL for quest_templates, generated_dialogues audit, faction_standings snapshots, npc_personalities; Redis sorted by context_hash with TTL 24h for generated narrative cache; S3 for large prompt template version archives
data.retention_and_deletion: generated_dialogues purged after 30 days via PostgreSQL scheduled job; faction_standings snapshots retained 7 days for debugging; player_histories retained 90 days for context window assembly

devops.deployment_topology: Two ECS Fargate services in us-east-1: narrative-inference (min 3 tasks, HPA CPU 60%) and authoring-api (min 2 tasks); ElastiCache Redis cluster; RDS PostgreSQL multi-AZ
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest, mypy, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy; prompt template changes require PR approval from writer team lead
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch metrics: narrative_generation_latency_p99, cache_hit_rate, moderation_flag_rate, llm_api_error_rate, cost_estimate_daily; CloudWatch alarms on P99 latency >3s and moderation flag rate >5%
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deploy with circuit breaker; prompt template rollback via version history in PostgreSQL; LLM provider fallback chain handles API outages

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a high-scale LLM narrative generation system for a procedural open-world game. Key decisions: dual-model strategy (GPT-4o for quality quest dialogue, Claude 3 Haiku for high-frequency flavor text) to balance quality and cost under $5000/month; Redis cache keyed by context hash with faction tier bucketing rather than raw faction scores to maximize hit rate; content moderation is synchronous and blocking before any output reaches game servers; two separate ECS services to isolate inference from authoring API. This is a moderate quality plan: core architecture is sound but the cache key design section is thin and cost estimation methodology is underdeveloped.
target_output.title: Quest Narrative LLM Generation System Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An LLM-powered quest narrative generation system built on AWS ECS Fargate with Python 3.12 FastAPI, using OpenAI GPT-4o for primary narrative generation and Claude 3 Haiku for high-frequency NPC flavor text. Player faction standings and history context personalize each quest narrative before delivery to game servers, with synchronous content moderation blocking any policy-violating outputs. Redis caches generated narratives by faction-tier-bucketed context hashes to control LLM API costs under $5000/month.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two ECS Fargate services: NarrativeInferenceService handles LLM prompt assembly from quest_templates, player_histories, and faction_standings; submits to GPT-4o streaming API with Claude 3 Haiku fallback; runs synchronous moderation check before returning narrative to game server; caches approved output in Redis. AuthoringAPIService serves the React 18 writer UI for prompt template management and flagged output review. Game servers call NarrativeInferenceService via internal ALB; writers access AuthoringAPIService via separate internal ALB. Both services authenticate via Cognito. PostgreSQL stores quest_templates, npc_personalities, generated_dialogues audit, and faction_standings snapshots. Redis ElastiCache caches approved narratives keyed by faction-tier-bucketed context hash with 24h TTL.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, openai SDK, anthropic SDK, tiktoken for token budget management, Jinja2 for prompt templating, LangChain for prompt chain orchestration. Data: PostgreSQL 16 RDS multi-AZ, Redis 7 ElastiCache for narrative cache. Frontend: React 18 TypeScript (authoring tool only). Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ECR, ALB, Cognito, CloudWatch. CI: GitHub Actions with pytest and mypy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Quest Narrative Generation: game server triggers quest event; NarrativeInferenceService assembles context from player_histories (last 10 events), faction_standings (tier-bucketed), npc_personality traits, and world_events; checks Redis cache by context hash; on miss, submits to GPT-4o with max_tokens 400; runs OpenAI Moderation API check; on pass, caches and streams to game server; on flag, substitutes pre-authored default and queues for writer review. NPC Flavor Text: high-frequency low-complexity NPC lines routed to Claude 3 Haiku for cost efficiency with same moderation pipeline. Authoring Tool: writers create and version Jinja2 prompt templates, preview sample outputs, review flagged dialogue queue with approve/reject, view generation analytics per template.
target_output.system_components: NarrativeInferenceService ECS Fargate (min 3 tasks, HPA CPU 60%), ContextAssembler module building prompt payloads, OpenAI GPT-4o and Claude 3 Haiku API integrations with latency-based fallback, OpenAI Moderation API synchronous check, Redis ElastiCache narrative cache, AuthoringAPIService ECS Fargate (min 2 tasks), PostgreSQL 16 RDS multi-AZ (quest_templates, generated_dialogues, faction_standings, npc_personalities), CloudWatch metrics and alarms, Cognito user pool for service and writer auth.
target_output.workflows: Quest Narrative Request: game server POSTs quest_template_id and player context to NarrativeInferenceService; ContextAssembler fetches quest_template from PostgreSQL (cached in memory), player history window from PostgreSQL, faction standings tier-bucketed by ally/neutral/enemy thresholds, npc_personality traits; computes context_hash from faction tier buckets and template version; checks Redis cache; on hit returns cached narrative immediately; on miss assembles Jinja2 prompt with sanitized player fields and submits to GPT-4o streaming; aggregates full response; runs OpenAI Moderation API check synchronously; if flagged substitutes default dialogue and creates review queue entry in PostgreSQL; if passed writes to Redis cache with 24h TTL and returns to game server within 3s P99. Flagged Content Review: writer logs in via SAML SSO to authoring UI; review queue shows paginated flagged generated_dialogues with quest context and violation category; writer approves (enables cached delivery) or rejects (marks template for revision); rejection triggers Slack notification to template owner.
target_output.data_model: quest_templates (id UUID, name VARCHAR, region VARCHAR, prompt_template_text TEXT, required_context_fields TEXT[], version INT, active BOOL, created_by VARCHAR, updated_at). player_histories (player_id VARCHAR, event_type VARCHAR, event_data_json JSONB, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ) partitioned by occurred_at with 90-day retention. faction_standings (player_id VARCHAR, faction_id VARCHAR, standing_score NUMERIC, tier VARCHAR CHECK IN ally/neutral/enemy, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). generated_dialogues (id UUID, quest_template_id UUID FK, player_id_hash VARCHAR, model_used VARCHAR, output_text TEXT, cache_key VARCHAR, moderation_passed BOOL, moderation_category VARCHAR, delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). npc_personalities (npc_id VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, faction_id VARCHAR, personality_traits_json JSONB, speech_style VARCHAR). world_events (id UUID, region_id VARCHAR, event_type VARCHAR, narrative_impact_json JSONB, active BOOL).
target_output.api_design: Internal VPC endpoints. POST /v1/quests/{quest_template_id}/narrative body {player_id, faction_standings_snapshot, recent_history_events: []} returns SSE stream of narrative JSON chunks. POST /v1/npcs/{npc_id}/flavor-text body {player_id, interaction_context} returns JSON {text, model_used, cache_hit}. GET /v1/authoring/templates returns paginated quest_templates for writer UI. POST /v1/authoring/templates creates new template version. GET /v1/authoring/review-queue returns flagged generated_dialogues. PATCH /v1/authoring/review-queue/{id} body {action: approve/reject} updates moderation status.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Prompt injection defense: player-sourced fields (character_name, faction_name, recent_quest_title) stripped of special characters via allowlist regex before Jinja2 template insertion; LLM system prompt includes explicit injection resistance clause. Content moderation: OpenAI Moderation API runs synchronously on every generated output; game-specific classifier (fine-tuned on rejected samples) runs in parallel; output blocked if either flags violation; fail-closed on moderation API timeout. Cognito service account tokens for game servers; SAML SSO for writers with read/write role separation (reviewer vs template_editor). VPC-only ALBs; no public endpoint for inference service.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: pytest with 80% coverage, mypy, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. NarrativeInferenceService: min 3 tasks, HPA on CPU 60% and custom metric narrative_queue_depth, max 15 tasks. AuthoringAPIService: min 2 tasks, max 5 tasks. Secrets: Cognito credentials, OpenAI API key, Anthropic API key stored in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Prompt template changes require PR approval from writer team lead in GitHub before merge to main. Environments: dev (single task, mocked LLM responses), staging (live LLM with usage caps), production.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom metrics: narrative_generation_latency_p99_ms, cache_hit_rate per quest_template, moderation_flag_rate per template, llm_api_cost_daily_estimate, gpt4o_vs_haiku_request_ratio. CloudWatch alarms: P99 latency >3s triggers PagerDuty, moderation flag rate >5% per template triggers writer team Slack alert, daily LLM cost estimate >$200 triggers cost review alert. Structured logs include quest_template_id, model_used, cache_hit, moderation_passed, total_latency_ms on every inference request.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Target LLM API cost under $5000/month achieved via 40% cache hit rate on Redis narrative cache. GPT-4o at $0.03/1K output tokens for quest dialogue; Claude 3 Haiku at $0.00125/1K tokens for flavor text. Cache TTL tuning and faction-tier bucketing (not raw scores) maximize cache hit rate. ECS auto-scaling handles quest trigger bursts during new content releases.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Single-region narrative generation for one game zone, GPT-4o primary with Haiku fallback, content moderation pipeline, Redis cache with faction-tier-bucketed keys, PostgreSQL audit trail, authoring UI for template management and review queue, GitHub Actions CI/CD, CloudWatch metrics. Phase 2 (6 weeks): All game zones enabled, NPC flavor text generation routed to Haiku, world_events context integration, writer analytics dashboard, prompt template version history. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-language support via translation post-processing, player narrative memory across sessions via extended history window, LLM cost optimization via cache hit rate A/B testing.
target_output.development_guidelines: All player-sourced prompt fields must pass allowlist sanitization before Jinja2 render; sanitization tests required in CI. LLM API calls wrapped in retry logic with exponential backoff; max 2 retries before fallback model. Moderation check fail-closed: any exception from moderation API defaults to blocking output. Prompt template changes reviewed by writer team lead before merge. Cost estimate metric updated on every inference call using tiktoken to compute input and output token counts.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o API rate limits during new content release causing latency spikes. Mitigation: Claude 3 Haiku fallback activates on rate limit errors; pre-generation of common quest narratives during low-traffic periods. Risk: cache context hash collisions causing wrong narrative delivered to player. Mitigation: include npc_id and region_id in hash to prevent cross-NPC collisions. Tradeoff: faction tier bucketing for cache keys improves hit rate but means players with slightly different faction scores receive identical narrative; acceptable for non-critical flavor variance. Risk: OpenAI Moderation API downtime. Mitigation: secondary game-specific classifier continues blocking; monitoring alerts on increased flag rate anomalies.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected GPT-4o over GPT-3.5-turbo for primary narrative based on writer team quality evaluation; Haiku for flavor text based on cost modeling. Confirmed Redis context hash cache over pre-generated static narrative tables because procedural world state space too large for exhaustive pre-generation. Chose faction tier bucketing (ally/neutral/enemy) over raw score in cache key to improve hit rate from estimated 15% to 40%. OpenAI Moderation API chosen for standard content categories; game-specific classifier handles fantasy violence and faction-specific policy violations.

metadata.notes: Moderate quality: core LLM narrative pipeline architecture is sound; cache key design and cost estimation sections are thinner than ideal.

project_goal.value: Live esports event coverage website displaying real-time match scores, series standings, and tournament brackets for an ongoing event, refreshed via polling against a public scores API and presented without requiring any user login.
project_goal.rationale: Event organizer needs a public-facing live score display for fans during a weekend tournament

target_users.value: Esports event spectators and fans consuming live match scores and bracket status without logging in
target_users.rationale: Fully anonymous public audience -- no account required

project_class.rationale: No server-side runtime required -- static Astro site with client-side polling against a third-party scores API
